###### Magic Collaboration Studio / Magic Collaboration Studio

Released on: January 19, 2024

2024x Hot Fix 1 is now available for Magic Collaboration Studio / Magic Collaboration Studio with several bug fixes and performance improvements.

1357821467

INLINE

- The issue causing undesired session invalidation has been fixed. This fix eliminates problems like:
  - Losing uncommitted changes when working with Cameo Collaborator documents.
  - Generating extra requests to the server from services like Resources and Teamwork Cloud Admin.
  - Cache invalidations and performance issues.
- The performance concern where the Web Application Platform welcome page takes an extended time to load has been fixed.
- The issue where resources failed to load when changing role scope for large user groups has been eliminated.

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

### Magic Collaboration Studio

Released on: July 5, 2024

Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh1 further improves your user experience with added branch managing capabilities and enhanced branch visualization. The ability to set the DSLS license in Teamwork Cloud Admin, added support for OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect, a new available report type, and other improvements greatly contribute to increased ease of use and functionality.

Version 2024x Refresh1 ofCameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud introduces major enhancements in commenting, PDF export, change tracking, and model hardening. Most notable improvements include the ability to create custom comment templates and a significant increase in PDF generation performance, as well as model hardening capabilities, including opening a document without publishing the project and linking Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements. For added convenience, we also introduced new document editing features, such as persisting commenting/editing mode, the ability to add a video, and a one-click saving option in Rich Text editor. But that‘s not all; we made other huge usability improvements, such as advanced comment customization options, support for diagram and table comparison, and the ability to open historical document versions.

### Magic Collaboration Studio and Teamwork Cloud Admin

#### Enhanced Branch Management

###### Managing Branches in Resources Application

To streamline branch management and offer you greater control and convenience, we introduce the Branches pane in the resource details view. This significant new feature of the Resources application allows you to efficiently [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project branches' space=''], including archiving, renaming, and deleting them, directly from the web user interface.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing resource branches in Resources application.

###### Improved Visual Branch Representation

2024x Refresh1 includes some subtle yet impactful enhancements to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing resource history' space='']. Archived branches are now clearly distinguishable with a greyed-out tag for easier navigation and organization. Additionally, you can now adjust column sizes effortlessly using a convenient column size slider, providing greater flexibility and customization within the interface.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visual branch representation improvements.

#### Setting DSLS License in Teamwork Cloud Admin

We’re happy to announce two exciting updates relating to license management in Teamwork Cloud Admin.

First, you can now utilize DSLS licenses seamlessly within the Settings application, simplifying the process of setting and managing licenses. Additionally, we‘re introducing support for triad licenses, enabling you to configure up to three DSLS server addresses for a robust server failover mechanism.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting DSLS license in Teamwork Cloud Admin.

#### Added Support for OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect

In this release, we expanded our authentication capabilities to include OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect clients alongside OAuth 1.0a, facilitating secure, consent-driven access while safeguarding user credentials. Users can now authorize client apps to access resources without compromising their credentials, ensuring enhanced privacy and security. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing OAuth client keys' space='']>>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting OAuth 2.0 client in Teamwork Cloud Admin.

#### Application Deployment through Kubernetes

We are introducing a new unified deployment strategy for Magic Collaboration Studio, Web Application Platform, and Cassandra through Kubernetes. With Kubernetes deployment, users can seamlessly manage and scale their applications with greater flexibility and consistency.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Other Fixes/Improvements

- Resource Synchronization now supports a secured connection (TLS).
- Task manager is now accessible from any application and you can download Task manager reports.
- A new type of report is available: the Locked resources report, displaying resources that have locked elements.
- Now you only need the Resource Manager role to manage branch level permissions instead of the Manage User Permissions permission, which was included solely in the Security Manager role.
- From now on Cassandra-related properties from Magic Collaboration Studio configuration files apply to Authserver as well, so it is no longer necessary to configure Cassandra-related properties in the authserver.properties file.

### Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

#### Commenting

Cameo Collaborator now lets you customize comments by creating different comment types, such as issue, question, or comment, and adding as many additional input fields as necessary for each type. These extensions are essential to satisfy the specific needs of your project.

###### Ability to Customize Comments

This version includes a new ability to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates' space=''] through a Profile Diagram. Previously limited to modifying only the title, body, and urgency of comments, you now have the option to tailor additional input fields and properties to suit your specific needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing a comment template with a Profile Diagram.

###### Comment Table

We enhanced the comment review process in models by automatically generating a hierarchical comment table under the package that represents the document. This table is created when the very first comment is added and lists all comments, along with their priority and resolution status.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing Cameo Collaborator comments table in the modeling tool.

#### Improved PDF Export

###### Significant Increase in Performance

The performance of the PDF export functionality has greatly improved, resulting in much faster export times, as well as the ability to handle considerably larger documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Password Protection for PDFs

For added security of sensitive project information, you can now protect PDFs with a password when generating a file for export.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Protecting a generated Cameo Collaborator PDF file with a password.

###### Text Search in Diagrams

To help you manage project data more efficiently and locate specific information more quickly, we introduce the ability to search for text within diagrams in exported PDF documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Searching for text within a diagram of an exported Cameo Collaborator document PDF file.

###### Exporting Section Titles as Bookmarks

Cameo Collaborator document section titles are now exported to PDFs as bookmarks, allowing you to navigate through the document with ease.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Section titles displayed as PDF document bookmarks.

#### Change Tracking

In response to customer feedback and critical requirements, we introduce major improvements to change tracking capabilities. You can now easily track changes in documents by opening historical versions and view what edits were made by which user at a specific time. Additionally, you can compare diagrams and tables across different versions,allowing you to understand how the model evolved, who was involved in the process, and how it led to important requirements and architectural decisions.

###### Accessing and Linking Historical Versions of Documents

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening historical version document' space=''] of Cameo Collaborator documents and open them in a read-only mode. This allows you to monitor content changes and make informed decisions, helping to review and improve project workflows.

Additionally, you can share a link to a specific document version. This is useful when a model has changes significant to system engineers, who can share the relevant version with others for review.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing or linking a historical version of a document.

###### Comparing Diagrams and Tables from Different Document Versions

We implemented the ability to easily compare diagrams and tables across different versions of a document. This allows you to effortlessly track the evolution of your content and gain valuable insights into how your elements have evolved over time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Comparing diagrams in different Cameo Collaborator document versions using Fade mode.

#### Model Hardening

In the initial phases of systems engineering, informal ideas often arise, necessitating lightweight capturing methods. Recognizing this need, Cameo Collaborator nowenables non-modelers to easily suggest project changes in a wiki form, which engineers can then use to build models. They can input requirements or other data which modelers can then convert into SysML/UAF models.

###### Opening Cameo Collaborator Document Without Project Publishing

To help non-modelers share ideas more quickly and efficiently, we introduce the ability to open a Cameo Collaborator document without publishing the associated project first.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening a document of an unpublished project with changes reflected in the modeling tool.

###### Linking Cameo Collaborator Paragraphs to Other Model Elements

We now allow linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to model element properties of numerical or textual type. This allows you to view up-to-date information and edit a Cameo Collaborator document without the need to manipulate the actual model in a modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements.

#### User Experience Improvements

###### Persisting Commenting or Editing Mode

When you enter commenting or editing mode, it will no longer close automatically after creating or editing a comment. Instead, the mode will persist until you manually close it or choose to discard/commit your changes to the server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commenting mode in Cameo Collaborator remains active when managing comments.

###### One-Click Saving in Rich Text Editing Form

Now you can save your edits with just one click while editing values in Rich Text Editing forms, eliminating the need to minimize the form. Introducing the Save button in the toolbar simplifies the workflow, enhancing user experience by reducing the steps required to save changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Clicking Save to save the comment and exit Rich Text editing mode.

###### Ability to Add Video to a Cameo Collaborator Document

You can now enrich your Cameo Collaborator documents with dynamic multimedia content by adding a video instead of a still image. This enhances communication and collaboration between teams and creates a more engaging and comprehensive experience.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Video in a Cameo Collaborator document.

###### Legend Support in Non-editable Documents

Addressing customer feedback, we introduce the ability to display table legends in non-editable Cameo Collaborator documents.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Viewing a corresponding table with its legend in the modeling tool and Cameo Collaborator.

#### otherOther Fixes/Improvements

- It is no longer necessary to specify a Cameo Collaborator URL in modeling tool Environment options, as the URL is retrieved automatically.
- Minor usability improvements in the Comments panel in modeling tools.
- You can now set up a redundant three-server configuration for Flexnet License Server.
- We added the option to export tables to CSV and JSON formats in addition to PDF and HTML.
- Users are now informed about a possibly outdated Cameo Collaborator document if the project it was published from has a newer version.
- SLF4J was removed and Log4j is now used for logging .

Section Titles as Bookmarks</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Cameo Collaborator document section titles are now exported to PDFs as bookmarks, allowing you to navigate through the document with ease.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="section_name_bookmark.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Section titles displayed as PDF document bookmarks.</h6><h2>Change Tracking</h2><p>In response to customer feedback and critical requirements, we introduce major improvements to change tracking capabilities. You can now easily track changes in documents by opening historical versions and view what edits were made by which user at a specific time. <span>Additionally, you can compare diagrams and tables <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">across different versions, </span><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">allowing you to understand how the model evolved, who was involved in the process, and how it led to important requirements and architectural decisions.</span></span></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Accessing and Linking Historical Versions of Documents</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening historical version document" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[access historical versions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of Cameo Collaborator documents and open them in a read-only mode. This allows you to monitor content changes and make informed decisions, helping to review and improve project workflows.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Additionally, you can share a link to a specific document version. This is useful when a model has changes significant to system engineers, who can share the relevant version with others for review. </p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewing_historical_document_version.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Viewing or linking a historical version of a document.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Comparing Diagrams and Tables from Different Document Versions</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">We implemented the ability to easily compare diagrams and tables across different versions of a document. This allows you to effortlessly track the evolution of your content and gain valuable insights into how your elements have evolved over time. </span></p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:height="926" ac:width="1080"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparing_versions_cc_fade.gif" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Comparing diagrams in different Cameo Collaborator document versions using Fade mode.</h6><h2>Model Hardening</h2><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">In the initial phases of systems engineering, informal ideas often arise, necessitating lightweight capturing methods. Recognizing this need, Cameo Collaborator now </span><span>enables non-modelers to easily suggest project changes in a wiki form, which engineers can then use to build models. They can input requirements or other data which modelers can then convert into SysML/UAF models.</span></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Opening Cameo Collaborator Document Without Project Publishing</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">To help non-modelers share ideas more quickly and efficiently, we introduce the ability to open a Cameo Collaborator document without publishing the associated project first.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="opening_document_without_publishing.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Opening a document of an unpublished project with changes reflected in the modeling tool.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Linking Cameo Collaborator Paragraphs to Other Model Elements</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">We now allow linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to model element properties of numerical or textual type. This allows you to view up-to-date information and edit a Cameo Collaborator document without the need to manipulate the actual model in a modeling tool.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="linking_elements_to_paragraphs.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 40.0px;">Linking Cameo Collaborator paragraphs to other model elements.</h6><h2>User Experience Improvements</h2><h4>Persisting Commenting or Editing Mode</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);">When you enter commenting or editing mode, it will no longer close automatically after creating or editing a comment. Instead, the mode will persist until you manually close it or choose to discard/commit your changes to the server.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(13,13,13);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="persisting_commenting_mode.gif" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Commenting mode in Cameo Collaborator remains active when managing comments.</h6><h4>One-Click Saving in Rich Text Editing Form</h4><p><span>Now you can save your edits with just one click while editing values in Rich Text Editing forms, eliminating the need to minimize the form. Introducing the Save button in the toolbar simplifies the workflow, enhancing user experience by reducing the steps required to save changes.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_rich_text_editing.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Clicking Save to save the comment and exit Rich Text editing mode.</span></h6><h4>Ability to Add Video to a Cameo Collaborator Document</h4><p><span>You can now enrich your Cameo Collaborator documents with dynamic multimedia content by adding a video instead of a still image. This enhances communication and collaboration between teams and creates a more engaging and comprehensive experience.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="video.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Video in a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h4>Legend Support in Non-editable Documents</h4><p><span>Addressing customer feedback, we introduce the ability to display table legends in non-editable Cameo Collaborator documents.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_with_legend.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Viewing a corresponding table with its legend in the modeling tool and Cameo Collaborator.</h6><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5b14424-bfd8-4e27-b396-618461111e40"><ac:parameter ac:name="">other</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Fixes/Improvements</h2><ul class="alternate" style="text-align: left;"><li>It is no longer necessary to specify a Cameo Collaborator URL in modeling tool Environment options, as the URL is retrieved automatically.</li><li>Minor usability improvements in the Comments panel in modeling tools.</li><li>You can now set up a redundant three-server configuration for Flexnet License Server.</li><li>We added the option to export tables to CSV and JSON formats in addition to PDF and HTML.</li><li>Users are now informed about a possibly outdated Cameo Collaborator document if the project it was published from has a newer version.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">SLF4J was removed and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Log4j is now used for logging</span></span>.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=194643188 space=MCS2024xR2 version=8 -->
## PAGE 00003: 2024x Refresh2 Version News

- page_id: `194643188`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/194643188/2024x+Refresh2+Version+News
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2024-11-08T08:35:46.680+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

top

### Magic Collaboration Studio

Released on: November 8, 2024

The latest Magic Collaboration Studio release introduces branch creation directly from the web UI, simplifying project management without needing a modeling tool. New webhook support for specific project branches adds flexibility in tracking model changes, and users can now generate personal access tokens for secure, password-free CLI authentication. Additionally, conditional user groups offer dynamic access control based on SAML IdP attributes, ensuring user criteria compliance on every login.

Cameo Collaborator is now integrated with the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, enabling you to publish and view Cameo Collaborator documents directly on the platform. You can now include content from one document to another, making it easier to enrich your documents. Additionally, you can now link paragraphs to model element properties directly within Cameo Collaborator.

****

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

****

- 
- 
- 
- 

#### Teamwork Cloud

##### Creating Branches via Web UI

We are excited to announce a powerful new feature: the ability to create project branches directly from the web user interface! This eliminates the need to have access to a modeling tool, making it easier and faster than ever to manage your projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the resource history dialog to create project branches.

##### Webhooks for Project Branches

You can now create webhooks for the project trunk as well as for its specific branches. This enhancement allows more flexibility and control when monitoring model change events.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting a branch for webhook to listen to.

##### Personal Access Token

You can now use the My Account application to generate a personal access token, providing a secure and convenient way to authenticate command-line tools. With this token, you can authenticate without needing to enter your username and password, enhancing both security and ease of use.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating a personal access token in the My Account application.

##### Conditional User Groups

In certain security-related authentication scenarios it is necessary for user access rights to be determined dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account. Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh2 introduces the ability to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values whenever you log in.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of enabling and defining a conditional user group.

##### Other

Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x Refresh2 comes with improved visual branch representation for mobile devices.

#### Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

##### Cameo Collaborator Integration with the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform

We are excited to announce that with the release of version 2024x Refresh2, Cameo Collaborator is integrated with the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform. This enhancement expands Cameo Collaborator capabilities, allowing you to not only work with documents on Teamwork Cloud, but also publish and view them on the**3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

##### Composing Documents from Other Documents' Content

We are excited to announce a powerful feature that provides a new way to augment Cameo Collaborator documents. You can now include content, such as diagrams and tables, from one document into another. This allows you, for example, to compose a document entirely from the content of several different documents. Depending on the selected option, the included content will be updated when the source document changes orwill stay the same as it was at the time of inclusion..

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Inserting content for another document.

##### New Way to Link Paragraphs to Model Elements

In the previous version of Cameo Collaborator, we introduced the ability to link paragraphs to numerical and textual model element properties. This release adds further improvements to this functionality, allowing you to link paragraphs not only in a modeling tool but in Cameo Collaborator as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of linking a paragraph to the requirement text.

##### Other Improvements

Editable Cameo Collaborator documents are often enriched by adding images. Now, when exporting these documents to PDF or HTML formats, all attached images are included in the generated files.

**Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Collaboration Studio' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=194643275 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00004: 2024x Version News

- page_id: `194643275`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/194643275/2024x+Version+News
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-11-08T08:30:08.872+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

### Magic Collaboration Studio

Released on: November 10, 2023

Version 2024x of Magic Collaboration Studio introduces updates that enhance your user experience. You can now conveniently visualize branch activity, use a new role to manage user group memberships, and select the exact branches to listen for when creating webhooks. You can also filter out users with locked elements in the Users application, track data markings' changes in the Audit report, and use DSLS licensing framework in addition to FlexNet.

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2024x brings a range of exciting enhancements as well. This version empowers you to customize PDF/HTML templates, cross-reference tables and figures within documents, use a new Wiki document viewing mode for a smoother navigation and editing experience, share specific diagram versions, export tables to Excel, and offers additional smaller improvements.

#### NOTE: 2024x Hot Fix 2 available

2024x Hot Fix 2 available

2024x Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes, and vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Hot Fix 2 Version News' space='NMDOC']>]]>

****

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

****

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

### Teamwork Cloud and Teamwork Cloud Admin

##### Visual Branch Representation

We implemented a visual representation of branch activity, which enhances your experience by helping you to better understand and track merges, branches, and associated commit information.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visual representation of branch activity.

##### New User Group Membership Manager Role

To streamline user group management, we introduce the new User Group Membership Manager role. This role allows you to efficiently add or remove users within specific user groups, thus, you no longer need your administrator’s assistance for routine user group management tasks.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### User Group Membership Manager role permissions and scope.

##### Selecting Branch for Webhooks

When creating a webhook, you can select the specific branch or branches in which the webhook will listen for events. This enhanced control capability is available for resource-scoped webhooks of the commit or tagged commit event type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Choosing branches in which the webhook will listen for events.

##### Other fixes/improvements

- A new filter is available in the **Users**application, allowing you to view users with locked elements.
- The **Audit**report now additionally tracks and displays changes in data markings.
- In addition to the Flexnet licensing framework, Magic Collaboration Studio now supports the **DSLS licensing framework**.

##### Deployment Changes

- **Web Application Platform:**Apache Tomcat version requirements have changed. Now the minimum supported version is 10.1.
- **Web Application Platform:**the servlet specification has been changed to Jakarta 10.
- Magic Collaboration Studio no longer supports CentOS 7 due to its approaching EOL. Instead, it now supports Oracle Linux 8.

### Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

##### Customizable Templates for Document Export to PDF/HTML

With Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2024x, customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents has never been easier. Now, you can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document. After downloading, you can make any desired changes and then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates. Therefore, this exciting feature empowers you to generate different outputs from the same document, allowing you to satisfy the unique requirements of different recipients. You will be able to alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font, and even add a custom logo to the document.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Uploading a custom PDF/HTML template to a Cameo Collaborator document.

##### Adding Cross References in Cameo Collaborator Documents

One crucial aspect of document creation involves cross-referencing different parts of the document. The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a convenient and easy way to incorporate references to any part of a Cameo Collaborator document, including figures and tables located within specific sections. We are confident that this functionality will assist you in producing documents that are both easy to read and navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding a hyperlink to a presentation element in a document section.

##### A New Document Viewing Mode

This release introduces a new document viewing mode - the Wiki view. Similar to the Document view, it hides the details of the model while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view. The Wiki view will provide you with a smoother content editing experience when the data needs to be managed in a wiki-style environment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching to the Wiki view.

##### Enhanced Diagram Sharing Capabilities

Besides being able to acquire a dynamic link to the latest version of a diagram in a Cameo Collaborator document, you now have the option to share a link to the current version of the diagram. This enables recipients to access a specific diagram version, even if the document is updated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Getting the link to a specific (current) version of a diagram.

##### Exporting Tables to Exce

This release introduces a new functionality, which allows you to export tables within Cameo Collaborator documents to the Excel format.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting a table to the Excel format.

##### Other Improvements

- In addition to FlexNet, Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud now supports the DSLS licensing server as well.
- The email notification capability has been improved and now works with mailing servers using SSL/TLS encryption.
- Now, Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings inside tables, ensuring consistency between documents and related server projects.
- The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin allows you to publish a new type of resource - **3D**EXPERIENCE Systems Traceability resource (TRA) - which can be used in the Systems Traceability app on the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- With the improved Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin, you can now select which document to update in case several documents are published from the same project.

**Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation' space='MCS2024x']

**News of earlier versions**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Refresh2 Version News' space='MCS2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Refresh1 Version News' space='MCS2022xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Version News' space='MCS2022x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1 style="text-align: center;">Magic Collaboration Studio</h1><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);"> Released on: November 10, 2023 </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Version 2024x of Magic Collaboration Studio introduces updates that enhance your user experience. You can now conveniently visualize branch activity, use a new role to manage user group memberships, and select the exact branches to listen for when creating webhooks. You can also filter out users with locked elements in the Users application, track data markings' changes in the Audit report, and use DSLS licensing framework in addition to FlexNet.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(52,53,65);">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2024x brings a range of exciting enhancements as well. This version empowers you to customize PDF/HTML templates, cross-reference tables and figures within documents, use a new Wiki document viewing mode for a smoother navigation and editing experience, share specific diagram versions, export tables to Excel, and offers additional smaller improvements.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ae05274-7eae-4b9d-9eac-29fd70dbe646"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2024x Hot Fix 2 available</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">2024x Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes, and vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products</span> </span> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="2024x Hot Fix 2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Teamwork Cloud and Teamwork Cloud Admin" /> </strong></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Visual Branch Representation" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="New User Group Membership Manager Role" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Selecting Branch for Webhooks" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Other fixes/improvements" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Deployment Changes" /></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud" /> </strong></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Customizable Templates for Document Export to PDF/HTML" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding Cross References in Cameo Collaborator Documents" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="A New Document Viewing Mode" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Enhanced Diagram Sharing Capabilities" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting Tables to Excel" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Other Improvements" /></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Teamwork Cloud and Teamwork Cloud Admin</h1><h3>Visual Branch Representation</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">We implemented a visual representation of branch activity, which enhances your experience by helping you to better understand and track merges, branches, and associated commit information.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="visual_branch_representation.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Visual representation of branch activity.</h6><h3>New User Group Membership Manager Role</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To streamline user group management, we introduce the new User Group Membership Manager role. This role allows you to efficiently add or remove users within specific user groups, thus, you no longer need your administrator’s assistance for routine user group management tasks.</span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:height="106" ac:width="550"><ri:attachment ri:filename="User Group Membership Manager.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">User Group Membership Manager role permissions and scope.</h6><h3>Selecting Branch for Webhooks</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">When creating a webhook, you can select the specific branch or branches in which the webhook will listen for events. This enhanced control capability is available for resource-scoped webhooks of the commit or tagged commit event type.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_branches_to_listen.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Choosing branches in which the webhook will listen for events.</h6><h3>Other fixes/improvements</h3><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A new filter is available in the <strong>Users </strong>application, allowing you to view users with locked elements.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The <strong>Audit </strong>report now additionally tracks and displays changes in data markings.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In addition to the Flexnet licensing framework, Magic Collaboration Studio now supports the <strong>DSLS licensing framework</strong>.</span></li></ul><h3>Deployment Changes</h3><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <strong>Web Application Platform: </strong>Apache Tomcat version requirements have changed. Now the minimum supported version is 10.1. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <strong>Web Application Platform: </strong>the servlet specification has been changed to Jakarta 10. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Magic Collaboration Studio no longer supports CentOS 7 due to its approaching EOL. Instead, it now supports Oracle Linux 8.</span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h1>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud</h1><h3>Customizable Templates for Document Export to PDF/HTML</h3><p>With Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2024x, customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents has never been easier. Now, you can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document. After downloading, you can make any desired changes and then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates. Therefore, this exciting feature empowers you to generate different outputs from the same document, allowing you to satisfy the unique requirements of different recipients. You will be able to alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font, and even add a custom logo to the document.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PDF_HTML_templates.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Uploading a custom PDF/HTML template to a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><h3><br />Adding Cross References in Cameo Collaborator Documents</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">One crucial aspect of document creation involves cross-referencing different parts of the document. The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a convenient and easy way to incorporate references to any part of a Cameo Collaborator document, including figures and tables located within specific sections. We are confident that this functionality will assist you in producing documents that are both easy to read and navigate.</span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_presentation_element.png" /></ac:image> </span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adding a hyperlink to a presentation element in a document section.</h6><h3><br />A New Document Viewing Mode</h3><p>This release introduces a new document viewing mode - the Wiki view. <span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">Similar to the Document view, it</span> hides the details of the model while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view. The Wiki view will provide you with a smoother content editing experience when the <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">data needs to be managed in a wiki-style environment.</span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="wiki_view.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Switching to the Wiki view.</h6><h3>Enhanced Diagram Sharing Capabilities</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">Besides being able to acquire a dynamic link to the latest version of a diagram in a Cameo Collaborator document, you now have the option to share a link to the current version of the diagram. This enables recipients to access a specific diagram version, even if the document is updated.</span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="getting_diagram_link.png" /></ac:image> </span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Getting the link to a specific (current) version of a diagram.</h6><h3>Exporting Tables to Exce</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">This release introduces a new functionality, which allows you to export tables within Cameo Collaborator documents to the Excel format.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_tables.png" /></ac:image> </span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Exporting a table to the Excel format.</h6><h3>Other Improvements</h3><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">In addition to FlexNet, Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud now supports the DSLS licensing server as well.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">The email notification capability has been improved and now works with mailing servers using SSL/TLS encryption.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">Now, Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings inside tables, ensuring consistency between documents and related server projects.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin allows you to publish a new type of resource - <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE Systems Traceability resource (TRA) - which can be used in the Systems Traceability app on the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tra_resource.png" /></ac:image> <br /><br /></span></li><li>With the improved Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin, you can now select which document to update in case several documents are published from the same project.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Documentation</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Collaboration Studio 2024x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>News of earlier versions</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2022xR2" ri:content-title="2022x Refresh2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2022xR1" ri:content-title="2022x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2022x" ri:content-title="2022x Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924478 space=MCS2024xR2 version=6 -->
## PAGE 00005: Accessing Magic Collaboration Studio web applications

- page_id: `191924478`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924478/Accessing+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+web+applications
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-04-03T16:56:39.619+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Magic Collaboration Studio license' space=''] and starting Magic Collaboration Studio, you can log in to Magic Collaboration Studio web applications.

To sign in to Magic Collaboration Studio web applications

1. In an internet browser, go to*http(s)://<server_address>:<port>/webapp*. For example, if the machine IP is **10.1.1.123** and the default port is used, the URL will be *[https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp](https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp)*. The default port is **8****443**.
2. When the authentication page opens, enter your username and password to sign in. The default credentials are **Administrator/****Administrator**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The main Web Application Platform page(*[https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp](https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp))*displayed after signing in.

Once you are logged in, you will be redirected to the landing page depending on your role/ permissions. To navigate to a specific web application, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using the app bar' space='']. Depending on their roles and permissions, some users may not be able to access some specific content in the Magic Collaboration Studio system or view its details.

The first page that opens after successful login varies according to the user role or their latest activity in the Teamwork Cloud Admin.

##### Signing out of Magic Collaboration Studio web applications

To sign out of Magic Collaboration Studio web applications

- In the top right corner click [ATTACHMENT filename='sign_out.png'] button and select Sign out .

Depending on their roles and permissions, some users may not be able to access some specific content in the Magic Collaboration Studio system or view its details.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1799632d-6f6a-4317-ab77-d1f47a6c8738"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The first page that opens after successful login varies according to the user role or their latest activity in the Teamwork Cloud Admin.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Signing out of Magic Collaboration Studio web applications</h3><p><br /></p><p>To sign out of Magic Collaboration Studio web applications</p><hr /><ul><li>In the top right corner click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sign_out.png" /></ac:image> button and select <strong>Sign out</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925608 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00007: Adding a logo to the cover page

- page_id: `191925608`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925608/Adding+a+logo+to+the+cover+page
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-11-03T16:34:20.930+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates > Adding logos
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1825042050

1825042072

1825042048

**On this page:**

4

1825042062

There are two ways to add your logo to the cover page:

- Configuring the logo image in the Cameo Collaborator template properties and using this template when publishing a document. The logo will appear in the Cameo Collaborator document and on the cover page of the exported document. The cover page template has a placeholder at the top of the page where the logo is placed.
- Removing the current placeholder for the logo and adding another logo image in the desired place of the cover page the template. In this case you will need to modify the <template folder>/common/coverPage.html file. To add the logo image to the cover page template, do one of the following:
  - Encode the image as the Base64 encoded string and use it as the value of the *img src* attribute.This is the preferred way to indicate a source of an image in templates. The URL must be absolute, relative URLs are not going to work.The URL must be accessible from the server where the web application platform is deployed if users are exporting a document to the PDF format.The URL must be accessible from the user computers if users are exporting a document to the HTML format. The URL will appear in the output HTML file as is.Use a URL to the remote image as the value of the *img src*attribute:

##### Example of adding a logo to the cover page by modifying an HTML template

Let’s assume that you want to make your logo appear on the cover page without configuring the logo for the project. Or you just want to use another logo image for the cover page than the one configured in the template used for publishing. In addition, you want your new logo to appear at the top of the cover page, the same place where the placeholder for the default logo is configured.

To add a logo to the cover page by modifying an HTML template

1. Encode the image as a Base64 string. You can use an online converter or your own code or tools to make the conversion.
2. Open the <template folder>/common/cover-page.html file.
3. Find the div tag with the company-logo-container.
4. Locate a single img tag within the div .
5. Replace the src of the img tag with <img src="{data:image/<image type>;base64,<image as a Base64 encoded string> }" alt="<alt text>">.
6. If necessary, use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the logo image the way you want. **Code example when using the PNG image type**<div id="company-logo-container"> 
 <img src="data:image/png;base64," width="200" height="200" alt="Logo"/></div>

If the document is exported using the modified template, the cover page with a custom logo may look like the one below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the cover page with a custom logo.

##### Example of configuring a logo in a Cameo Collaborator template

Let’s assume that you want to configure a logo image in the Cameo Collaborator template you use for publishing a document. In this case, you can either modify the default template or create your own custom template. For more information on hoe to create custom document templates, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a custom template' space='MCS2024xR2'].

This example demonstrates how to modify the *SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator*.

To configure a logo in a Cameo Collaborator template based on the *SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator*

1. Open the SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator Cameo template in a modeling tool.
2. Clear the Standard/System Profile checkbox in the Project Properties dialog so the template is not considered a system file. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a custom template' space='MCS2024xR2'].
3. Open the Specification window of the Document element as shown below. [ATTACHMENT filename='specification_window_of_document_element.png']
4. In the Specification window, select your logo image as the value of the Image property. [ATTACHMENT filename='image_property.png']
5. Save the template file.
6. Open the project from which you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document.
7. In the main menu, select Tools > Cameo Collaborator > Publish .
8. In the Cameo Collaborator Publisher dialog, select to use your custom template. Select Use Local Template or Use Server Template depending on where the template is stored. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_template.png']
9. After the template is used select it in the Template drop-down box.
10. Define other publishing properties and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space='MCS2024xR2'] .

If the logo image was successfully added, it will be displayed in the App bar of the Cameo Collaborator document as shown below. Furthermore, the logo image will be displayedon the cover page of the exported document as well.If you want the logo to be displayed in a non-default location or make other changes, you canadjust the styles of the cover page in the*common/cover-page-styles.html*file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Cameo Collaborator document with a custom logo.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ea0a546-ac6e-4817-966c-54267ccbda23"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1825042050</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="447db5be-72e1-423b-b5fa-567e34453de8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1825042072</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6ecb787-d23f-4cd1-b1b5-fdae91cb43e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1825042048</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5cadc2e-9b1b-4398-9ef6-d48e84ec9dab"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02164683-2634-4053-861f-d42e4b372fdd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1825042062</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are two ways to add your logo to the cover page:</p><ul><li>Configuring the logo image in the Cameo Collaborator template properties and using this template when publishing a document. The logo will appear in the Cameo Collaborator document and on the cover page of the exported document. The cover page template has a placeholder at the top of the page where the logo is placed.</li><li>Removing the current placeholder for the logo and adding another logo image in the desired place of the cover page the template. In this case you will need to modify the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/coverPage.html</em> file.<br />To add the logo image to the cover page template, do one of the following:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Encode the image as the Base64 encoded string and use it as the value of the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">img src</em> attribute.<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> This is the preferred way to indicate a source of an image in templates.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="137c6e6d-2256-4997-8cc0-b8f9763c705e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The URL must be absolute, relative URLs are not going to work.</li><li>The URL must be accessible from the server where the web application platform is deployed if users are exporting a document to the PDF format.</li><li>The URL must be accessible from the user computers if users are exporting a document to the HTML format. The URL will appear in the output HTML file as is.<p class="auto-cursor-target">Use a URL to the remote image as the value of the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">img src</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> attribute:<br /></span></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Example of adding a logo to the cover page by modifying an HTML template</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to make your logo appear on the cover page without configuring the logo for the project. Or you just want to use another logo image for the cover page than the one configured in the template used for publishing. In addition, you want your new logo to appear at the top of the cover page, the same place where the placeholder for the default logo is configured.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a logo to the cover page by modifying an HTML template</p><hr /><ol><li>Encode the image as a Base64 string. You can use an online converter or your own code or tools to make the conversion.</li><li>Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/cover-page.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>div</em> tag with the <em>company-logo-container.</em></li><li>Locate a single <em>img</em> tag within the <em>div</em>.</li><li>Replace the <em>src</em> of the <em>img</em> tag with <em>&lt;img src=&quot;{data:image/&lt;image type&gt;;base64,&lt;image as a Base64 encoded string&gt; }&quot; alt=&quot;&lt;alt text&gt;&quot;&gt;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If necessary, use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the logo image the way you want.</p><table class="fixed-table wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 1087.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><strong>Code example when using the PNG image type</strong></th></tr><tr><td><p>&lt;div id=&quot;company-logo-container&quot;&gt;<br />   &lt;img src=&quot;data:image/png;base64,&quot; width=&quot;200&quot; height=&quot;200&quot; alt=&quot;Logo&quot;/&gt;</p><p>&lt;/div&gt;</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></li></ol><p><br />If the document is exported using the modified template, the cover page with a custom logo may look like the one below.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="logo_on_cover_page_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a logo to the cover page" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of the cover page with a custom logo.</h6><p><span><br /></span></p><h3>Example of configuring a logo in a Cameo Collaborator template</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to configure a logo image in the Cameo Collaborator template you use for publishing a document. In this case, you can either modify the default template or create your own custom template. For more information on hoe to create custom document templates, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a custom template" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</p><p>This example demonstrates how to modify the <em>SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator</em>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To configure a logo in a Cameo Collaborator template based on the <em>SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator</em></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator Cameo</em> template in a modeling tool.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Clear the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Standard/System Profile</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> checkbox in the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Project Properties</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span> </span>dialog so the template is not considered a system file. For more information, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a custom template" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li>Open the Specification window of the Document element as shown below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_window_of_document_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a logo to the cover page" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the Specification window, select your logo image as the value of the <strong>Image</strong> property.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a logo to the cover page" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Save the template file.</li><li>Open the project from which you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Cameo Collaborator</strong> &gt; <strong>Publish</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog, select to use your custom template. Select <strong>Use Local Template</strong> or <strong>Use Server Template</strong> depending on where the template is stored.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a logo to the cover page" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>After the template is used select it in the <strong>Template</strong> drop-down box.</li><li>Define other publishing properties and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Publishing documents" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[publish the document]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><p><span> </span></p><p>If the logo image was successfully added, it will be displayed in the App bar of the Cameo Collaborator document as shown below. Furthermore, the logo image will be displayed<span> </span>on the cover page of the exported document as well.<span> If you want the logo to be displayed in a non-default location or make other changes, you can </span>adjust the styles of the cover page in the<span> </span><em>common/cover-page-styles.html</em><span> </span>file.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="document_with_logo.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a logo to the cover page" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Cameo Collaborator document with a custom logo.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925614 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00008: Adding a logo to the header and footer

- page_id: `191925614`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925614/Adding+a+logo+to+the+header+and+footer
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-10T13:34:37.315+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates > Adding logos
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

To make changes to the footer or header of exported documents, you will need to modify the following files:

- For the header - <template folder>/common/header.html
- For the footer - <template folder>/common/footer.html

You can add a logo to the header or footer in one of the following ways:

- Encode the logo image as the Base64 encoded string and use it as the value of the img src attribute.
- Add a URL to the remote image as the value of the *img src* attribute. The URL must be absolute, relative URLs do not work.The URL must be accessible from the server where Web Application Platform is deployed.

##### Example of adding a logo to the header as a Base64 encoded string

Let’s say you want to use a logo in the header of the exported PDF file pages.

To add a logo to the header as a Base64 encoded string

1. Encode the image as a Base64 string. You can use an online converter or use your own code/tools to make the conversion.
2. Open the <template folder>/common/header.html file.
3. Find the closing style tag.
4. Below this tag, create a *div* with an *img* tag, where *<img src="{data:image/<image type>;base64,<Image as a Base64 encoded string> }" alt="<alt text>">.*
5. Add the *style=”position: running(header)”*attribute to the *div.*
6. Find the *@page { @top-left { content: "" }}* within the style definitions and replace the double quotes with *element(header).*
7. Use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the logo image the way you want. Your code may look like the one below if you use a PNG image:

| Code example when using the PNG image type |
| --- |
| <div th:fragment="HeaderFragment" xmlns:th=http://www.thymeleaf.org> <style> @page { @top-left { content: element(header) }} </style> <div style="position: running(header)"> <img src=" data:image/png;base64," width="72" height="22" alt="Logo"/> </div></div> |

The figure below shows an example of the exported document with the logo in the header.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the exported document with the logo in the header.

##### Example of adding a logo to the footer using an absolute URL to the image

Let’s say you want to use a logo in the footer of the exported PDF file pages. In this example, we will use an absolute URL as the image source.

To add a logo to the footer using an absolute URL to the image

1. Open the <template folder>/common/footer.html file.
2. Find the footer tag.
3. Add the img tag with <img src"http(s)://path_to_image" alt="<alt text>" >
4. Use other HTML tags and CSS styling information to display the image the way you want. Your code may look like this (the CSS is inline for simplicity): Code example[IMAGE alt='Logo' src='https://www.3ds.com/assets/3ds-navigation/3DS_corporate-logo_blue.svg'] 
 
]]>

The figure below shows an example of the exported document with the logo in the footer.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the exported document with the logo in the footer.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

