<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925629 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00039: Changing appearance of custom comment fields

- page_id: `191925629`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925629/Changing+appearance+of+custom+comment+fields
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-26T13:54:44.837+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Custom comment fields are rendered in the *comment/comment-fragment.html* file. You can only customize the appearance of comment fields when comments are saved in the project. Comment fields are rendered in the following code block:

```text
]]>
```

##### Hiding custom comment fields

You can hide custom comment fields in the exported document.

To hide custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/comment-fragment.html file.
2. Find the custom comment field code block and remove it from the template.

##### Example of changing the appearance of custom comment fields

Let’s assume that you want to change the current appearance of custom comment fields in the exported document from chips to a simple HTML list.

To change the appearance of custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/comment-fragment.html file.
2. Find the custom comment field code block and change it as displayed below: ****
 ****
]]>

##### Changing the color of the custom comment field chip

You can change the default color of custom comment fields

To change the color of custom comment fields

1. Open the <template_folder>/comment/styles.html file.
2. Find the *style* tag with *th:fragment="CommentStyles"*, locate the *main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip*tag within, and change the property *background-color: #336699;* to any other desired color code: table.main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip {
 background-color: #336699;
 …
 }
…
]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Custom comment fields are rendered in the <em>comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file. You can only customize the appearance of comment fields when comments are saved in the project. Comment fields are rendered in the following code block:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d35c102-e4b7-4bf3-8dd8-348ccdb1ad03"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<div th:if="${comment.customization}" class="comment-customization-fields">
    <div th:each="field : ${comment.customization.fields}"
        class="comment-field-chip">
       <b th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.name, '
', '<br>')}"></b>
       <b th:if="${field.showValue}" th:text="':'"></b>
       <span th:if="${field.showValue}" th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.value, '
', '<br>')}"></span>
    </div>
</div>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Hiding custom comment fields</h3><p>You can hide custom comment fields in the exported document.</p><p><br /></p><p>To hide custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the custom comment field code block and remove it from the template.</li></ol><h3><br />Example of changing the appearance of custom comment fields</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to change the current appearance of custom comment fields in the exported document from chips to a simple HTML list.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the appearance of custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/comment-fragment.html</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the custom comment field code block and change it as displayed below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="50d1faf7-eac6-42da-b85e-54d5c5b04554"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<ul th:if="${comment.customization}" class="comment-customization-fields">
    <li th:each="field : ${comment.customization.fields}">
       <b th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.name, '
', '<br>')}"></b>
       <b th:if="${field.showValue}" th:text="':'"></b>
       <span th:if="${field.showValue}" th:utext="${commentsDisplayUtils.escapeXml(field.value, '
', '<br>')}"></span>
    </li>
</ul>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the color of the custom comment field chip</h3><p>You can change the default color of custom comment fields</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the color of custom comment fields</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/comment/styles.html</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommentStyles&quot;</em>, locate the <em>main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip </em>tag within, and change the property <em>background-color: #336699;</em> to any other desired color code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8bd950fc-25a4-4805-8549-6a37c6336a61"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommentStyles">
   table.main-thread-comment div.comment-field-chip {
            background-color: #336699;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925620 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00040: Changing font color

- page_id: `191925620`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925620/Changing+font+color
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-11-13T10:47:36.196+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates > Changing font properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1918676542

1918676563

1918676540

**On this page:**

4

1918676553

The color for the whole document is not set in templates. It is rgb(0, 0, 0) by default. If you want to set a non-default color, you have to add color information to the HTML template as a CSS *color* property.

However, some elements of exported documents use non-default colors. If you want to change the font color of such an element, you need to locate the element in the templates and change its font color value.

##### Example of changing the font color for the whole document

Let’s assume you want to change the font color for the whole document to rgb(90, 90, 90) (dark grey).

To change the font color for the whole document

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles".
3. Locate the *body* tag within and add the *color: rgb(90, 90, 90);* property to the list of other body style attributes. body {
 color: rgb(90, 90, 90);
 …
 }
…
]]>

If you export a document with the added color property, you will see that the font color of the document text is dark grey, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample document with the font color changed to dark grey.

##### Example of changing the font color of page numbers in the footer

Let’s assume that you want to change the font color of the pagination information at the bottom of the page to blue.

To change the font color of page numbers in the footer to blue

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/footer.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles".
3. Locate the *.footer* class definition and change the color property value to *blue*. .footer {
 color: blue;
 …
 }
…
]]>

Now, the page numbers in the footer of the exported document will be in blue font.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample document with the pagination font color changed to blue.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c24ab7e-cfa2-448c-888d-e2c24c98aff7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1918676542</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84c88650-f29a-4d71-a893-27b98664d5d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1918676563</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a766c29-1e93-473a-a67f-d0bbaaeadae6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1918676540</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a9672bb-8004-4b07-86ab-aca2a3edbb82"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cb93a8d-fe01-4a82-8b36-744b8d24a675"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1918676553</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The color for the whole document is not set in templates. It is rgb(0, 0, 0) by default. If you want to set a non-default color, you have to add color information to the HTML template as a CSS <em>color</em> property.</p><p>However, some elements of exported documents use non-default colors. If you want to change the font color of such an element, you need to locate the element in the templates and change its font color value.</p><h3><br />Example of changing the font color for the whole document</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to change the font color for the whole document to rgb(90, 90, 90) (dark grey).</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font color for the whole document</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>body</em> tag within and add the <em>color: rgb(90, 90, 90);</em> property to the list of other body style attributes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81175f14-144a-4477-83e0-a76157a56f7e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   body {
            color: rgb(90, 90, 90);
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>If you export a document with the added color property, you will see that the font color of the document text is dark grey, as shown below.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_font_color.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing font color" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A sample document with the font color changed to dark grey.</h6><h3><br />Example of changing the font color of page numbers in the footer</h3><p>Let’s assume that you want to change the font color of the pagination information at the bottom of the page to blue.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font color of page numbers in the footer to blue</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/footer.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the style tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>.footer</em> class definition and change the color property value to <em>blue</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d0c6117-27fb-43b9-8a82-cf5ed71f297c"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   .footer {
            color: blue;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Now, the page numbers in the footer of the exported document will be in blue font.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_pagination_font_color.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing font color" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A sample document with the pagination font color changed to blue.</h6><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00042: Changing font size

- page_id: `191925619`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925619/Changing+font+size
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-11-10T15:30:25.069+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates > Changing font properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Font sizes are changed by setting or changing the standard *font-size* CSS property. The base size for the whole document is not set by default, so you will need to add this property to the *<template_folder>/common/styles.html* template.

Some document elements, like headers and cover page headings, have their own font sizes. To change the size of such a font, you need to find out which file defines the property and change its value.

##### Example of changing the font size for the whole document

Let’s assume you want to change the font size for the whole document to 15px.

To change the font size for the whole document to 15px

1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html file.
2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles" .
3. Locate the *body* tag within and add the *font-size: 15px;* property to the list of other body style attributes. body {
 font-size: 15px;
 …
 }
…
]]>

Now, the main font size of the exported document will be 15px.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Font sizes are changed by setting or changing the standard <em>font-size</em> CSS property. The base size for the whole document is not set by default, so you will need to add this property to the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> template.</p><p>Some document elements, like headers and cover page headings, have their own font sizes. To change the size of such a font, you need to find out which file defines the property and change its value.</p><h3><br />Example of changing the font size for the whole document</h3><p>Let’s assume you want to change the font size for the whole document to 15px.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the font size for the whole document to 15px</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;template_folder&gt;/common/styles.html</em> file.</li><li>Find the <em>style</em> tag with <em>th:fragment=&quot;CommonStyles&quot;</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate the <em>body</em> tag within and add the <em>font-size: 15px;</em> property to the list of other body style attributes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97bb7ad3-f7c4-477d-89bc-1aa3755e7bf5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<style th:fragment="CommonStyles">
   body {
            font-size: 15px;
            …
   }
…
</style>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>Now, the main font size of the exported document will be 15px.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924343 space=MCS2024xR2 version=5 -->
## PAGE 00043: Changing server or service address

- page_id: `191924343`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924343/Changing+server+or+service+address
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-05-22T12:17:11.416+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Deployment Guide > Configuration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

If you configure a signed SSL certificate, you may need to switch the server address from an IP address to a Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN). In such cases, you only need to update the public-facing address for Web Application Platform. All other service contact points on the backend remain as internal addresses.

###### Switching from IP address to FQDN for an all-in-one deployment

Edit the fields listed in the table below and replace the IP address with the FQDN.

| Configuration file | Parameter |
| --- | --- |
| authserver.properties | authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist |
| webappplatform.properties | authentication.server.ip |
| service.url |  |

###### Updating server address for all deployments

If you need to update a server address due to deployment or networking changes, edit the following configuration files. Replace all instances of the old address with the new address.

| Configuration file to edit | Default path |
| --- | --- |
| cassandra.yaml | /etc/cassandra/conf |
| application.conf | <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration |
| authserver.properties | <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf |
| webappplatform.properties | <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf |

###### Additional configuration for multi-server deployment

You may need additional configuration changes if services are deployed on different servers.

| Configuration file | Remote service | Parameter to check |
| --- | --- | --- |
| application.conf | cassandra | contact-points in esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver |
| webapp (authentication) | url in esi.auth |  |
| webappplatform.properties | twcloud | twc.ip |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924469 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00044: Changing SSL certificates

- page_id: `191924469`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924469/Changing+SSL+certificates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-22T08:11:26.420+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Important

168993686

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Important

168993688

168993685

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Important

168993687

By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.

##### Changing the SSL certificate file

You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.

To change the SSL certificate file

1. Go to the <web_app_platform_installation_directory>\conf directory and open to edit the server.xml file.
2. In the server.xml file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password: ImportantIf you havecreated the SSL certificatefile as described in the above section, the **certificateKeystorePassword** property value must be the same as the **-storepass** and **-keypass** property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.
  - certificateKeystoreFile="<path_to_certificate_file>"
  - certificateKeystorePassword="<password>"
3. Save and close the server.xml file.
4. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.</p><h3><br />Changing the SSL certificate file</h3><p>You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the SSL certificate file</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;web_app_platform_installation_directory&gt;\conf</em> directory and open to edit the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>In the <em>server.xml</em> file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password:<br /><ul><li><pre>certificateKeystoreFile=&quot;&lt;path_to_certificate_file&gt;&quot;</pre></li><li><pre>certificateKeystorePassword=&quot;&lt;password&gt;&quot;<br /><br /></pre></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="027c18b8-3066-4e11-9adb-e8dab5278eae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you have </span>created the SSL certificate<span> file as described in the above section, t</span>he <strong>certificateKeystorePassword</strong> property value must be the same as the <strong>-storepass</strong> and <strong>-keypass</strong> property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00045: Changing the font for the whole document

- page_id: `191925618`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925618/Changing+the+font+for+the+whole+document
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-11-09T10:30:34.997+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates > Changing font properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1968159610

1968159631

1968174488

**On this page:**

4

1968159620

To change the font for the whole document, you need to do the following:

1. Specify the font in the HTML templates.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the font for the whole document' space='MCS2024xR2'] .
3. If you want the font of the table of contents to be other than the default Arial font, specify it in the properties file.

##### Example of changing the document font to Noto Sans

Let’s assume you want to use the Google *Noto Sans* font as the main font of the document.

To change the default font to Noto Sans

1. Change the font-family in the templates of the document-exporter:
  1. Open the <template_folder>/common/styles.html .
  2. Find the style tag with th:fragment="CommonStyles" .
  3. Locate the body tag within and change the font-family value to "Noto Sans" .
  4. Add a generic font family, e.g., sans-serif, to the end of the list so that an alternative font is selected if the main font is unavailable. body {
 font-family: "Noto Sans", sans-serif;
 …
 }
…
]]>
2. Install the font on the operating system where the document-exporter web application is deployed.
3. Open the *<web_app_platform_install_root>/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties*file and set*Noto Sans*as the value of the*fonts.toc.default*property.

##### Installing fonts

Some publicly available fonts have their own installation scripts and instructions. If this is the case, follow the official installation instructions.

Fonts must be embeddable. You can configure the export to avoid embedding for most of the fonts, but it is not recommended because all users who open the exported document need to have these fonts installed on their computers.

###### Example of installing the Noto Sans font

Let's say you need to install the Noto Sans font.

To install the Noto Sans font on Linux

1. Download the Noto Sans font from the official Google Noto Fonts website.
2. Extract the archive to a directory of your choice.
3. Copy the extracted font files to the /usr/share/fonts/noto directory.
4. Execute the following command to clean the font cache:
5. Execute the following command to check if the new Noto sans font is installed:

To install the Noto Sans font on Windows

1. Download the Noto Sans font from the official Google Noto Fonts website.
2. Extract the archive to a directory of your choice.
3. Select and right-click the extracted font files, then select Install .
4. Open the Windows fonts list to see if the Noto Sans font is installed.

###### Example of installing a custom/proprietary font

Let's assume you need to install a custom font, e.g., *MyCompanyFont*, available as a .tiff file, e.g., *MyCompanyFont.tiff.*

**

To install a custom font on Linux

1. Copy the font file to the /usr/share/fonts/mycompany directory.
2. Execute the following command to clean the font cache:
3. Execute the following command to check if the *MyCompanyFont* font is installed:

To install a custom font on Windows

1. Right-click the MyCompanyFont.tiff file and select Install .
2. Open the Windows fonts list to see if the MyCompanyFont font is installed.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261622960 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00046: Changing the title of the table of contents

- page_id: `261622960`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/261622960/Changing+the+title+of+the+table+of+contents
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-10T09:45:50.560+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: ['mcs', 'twc']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can define the table of contents in the c*ommon/table-of-contents.html* template file and customize the appearance of the table of contents, including:

- The title
- The indentation of items
- The color

##### Example of changing the title of the table of contents for a document

Let’s assume you want to change the default title *Table of Contents* for a document to *Index.*

To change the default title*Table of Contents*

1. Open the *<template folder>/common/table-of-contents.html* file.
2. Find the *h1* element with a value of *Table of Contents*within the html *body*element, and change its value to *Index*: textIndex]]>

If a document is exported using the updated template, the new table of contents title *Index* would appear as the title of the table of contents:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a modified table of contents title.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>You can define the table of contents in the c<em>ommon/table-of-contents.html</em> template file and customize the appearance of the table of contents, including:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="849acf33-e70b-47d9-970a-8a0212b0ddda"><span>The title</span></li><li data-uuid="378925af-387a-4367-b1a0-8eddbf7c7492"><span>The indentation of items</span></li><li data-uuid="1cd0283b-2fba-4acb-af23-ce5d1d18203a"><span>The color</span></li></ul><h3><span>Example of changing the title of the table of contents for a document</span></h3><p><span>Let’s assume you want to change the default title <em>Table of Contents</em> for a document to <em>Index.</em></span></p><p><span>To change the default title<em> Table of Contents</em></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3acd9f8a-c9d9-4f45-9165-07853fe84e58"><span>Open the <em>&lt;template folder&gt;/common/table-of-contents.html</em> file.</span></li><li data-uuid="36272b8c-0f75-46f1-abdb-db2af36aed43"><span>Find the <em>h1</em> element with a value of <em>Table of Contents </em>within the html <em>body </em>element, and change its value to <em>Index</em>:<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4fbc681-0c3f-400d-83c0-a7ee6fe6b6e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[…
<body>
   <h1>Index</h1>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li></ol><p><span>If a document is exported using the updated template, the new table of contents title <em>Index</em> would appear as the title of the table of contents:</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_exported_table_of_contents.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of a modified table of contents title.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924468 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00047: Changing Web Application Platform property values

- page_id: `191924468`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924468/Changing+Web+Application+Platform+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-04T11:26:17.218+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the *webappplatform.properties* file. Click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using no-installer' space='MCS2024xR2'], to learn more about each property.

To change Web Application Platform property values

1. Go to the *<* install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open for editing the webappplatform.properties file.
2. Change the desired property values.
3. Restart the Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file. Click <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using no-installer" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, to learn more about each property.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change Web Application Platform property values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><em>&lt;</em></span><em style="text-align: left;">install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory and open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li>Change the desired property values.</li><li>Restart the Web Application Platform.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924475 space=MCS2024xR2 version=4 -->
## PAGE 00048: Checking current version and user count

- page_id: `191924475`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924475/Checking+current+version+and+user+count
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-02-03T10:13:45.566+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.

To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information

1. Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.
2. Go to http(s)://<cc_for_twc_host>:<cc_for_twc_port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info .

The website displays all licence information, such as licence type, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information</p><hr /><ol><li>Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.</li><li>Go to <em>http(s)://&lt;cc_for_twc_host&gt;:&lt;cc_for_twc_port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info</em>.</li></ol><p><br />The website displays all licence information, such as licence <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d4430fa4-f2e4-49a6-a0d3-6698f17baa5b">type</ac:inline-comment-marker>, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925016 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00049: Checking LDAP connection

- page_id: `191925016`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925016/Checking+LDAP+connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-07-07T08:50:55.874+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.

To check the connection to a LDAP server

1. On the **LDAP Management** page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. Select **Check connection**. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messages will appear.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check the connection to a LDAP server</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the <strong>LDAP Management</strong> page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing users" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Check connection</strong>. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messag<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8d9608cd-f9a4-45af-af63-ab4980ff04c2">es will appear.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925284 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00050: Collaborator View Diagram

- page_id: `191925284`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925284/Collaborator+View+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:53:59.516+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates > Creating a custom template
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.

##### The Document element

A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog when [publishing a project](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MCS2024xR2/Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface).

##### The View element

A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling the Single Tree View' space='MCS2024xR2'].

The main properties of a View element are:

- Skip if Empty - if set as true , a published project does not display views without data.
- Document View - if set as true , this particular view of a document is presented as in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling the Document View' space='MCS2024xR2'].
- **Allow To Switch Property View Mode** - if set as *true*, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. To change the mode when browsing a document, open the view, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select **Model View** or **Document View**.

##### The Viewpoint element

A Viewpoint is used to define the rules that dictate how information is collected from the model and displayed on the web. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram' space='MCS2024xR2'].

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="color: rgb(23,43,77);font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;"><strong>On this page</strong></p><p style="color: rgb(23,43,77);font-size: 14.0px;font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: normal;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adbd07e7-0846-427e-bd25-ba64d756983c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling the Single Tree View" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enabling the Single Tree View mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p>The main properties of a View element are:</p><ul><li><strong>Skip if Empty</strong> -<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, a published project does not display views without data.</span></li><li><strong>Document View</strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> - </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, this particular view of a document is presented as in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling the Document View" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document view mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Allow To Switch Property View Mode</strong> - if set as <em>true</em>, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. ````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925278 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00051: Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram

- page_id: `191925278`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925278/Collaborator+Viewpoint+Method+Diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-14T13:35:35.366+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates > Creating a custom template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

The Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram is a specialized activity diagram that defines a Viewpoint. This diagram allows you to obtain model data using several types of operators and present the obtained data as tables, images, paragraphs, lists, and sections in a web portal.

Once a Viewpoint is applied to a View, exposed elements can be operated by three types of operators: Collect, Sort, and Filter. You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.

##### Collect operators

In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.

##### Filter operators

Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the "FilterBy..." operator needs to have the "CollectBy..." operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.

##### Sort operators

Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.

##### Table Structure operators

Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.

##### Template operators

After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed. Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewpoint_method_diagram_pallet.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.</span></h6><h3>Collect operators</h3><p>In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.</p><h3><br />Filter operators</h3><p>Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the &quot;FilterBy...&quot; operator needs to have the &quot;CollectBy...&quot; operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.</p><h3><br />Sort operators</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.</span></p><h3><br />Table Structure operators</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_structure_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.</h6><h3>Template operators</h3><p>After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed.  Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A" style="">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925355 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00052: Comments pane

- page_id: `191925355`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925355/Comments+pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-07-07T08:51:23.335+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['comments-pane', 'filtering-comments', 'sorting-comments', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

839308113

839308115

839308112

**On this page**

4

839308114

The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The comments pane of a published model portal.

The comments pane can be shown or hidden.

To show/hide the comments pane

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show-hide_comments_pane.png'] in the title bar of a published model portal.

##### Navigating between comments and commented items

Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.

##### Searching for comments

The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.

To search for comments

1. Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.
2. Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:
  - Under the comment search box, select the Unresolved check box, to search only for unresolved comments.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **Unread** check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **High priority** check box, to search for comments with high priority.

When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.

839335150

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure of the document portal' space='MCS2024xR2']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='App bar' space='MCS2024xR2']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space='MCS2024xR2']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49894020-2a87-4a73-afa7-2ab36c88c023"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">839308113</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5628efc-00d0-4e7d-a2b9-9af636ba94ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">839308115</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0dd87eb-ae06-4711-8334-6da9b5aab7b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">839308112</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c822421d-7fbb-4b98-9594-2d5882617869"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc18882d-192b-411c-97e1-85126fd9e032"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">839308114</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comments_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The comments pane of a published model portal.</h6><p style="text-align: left;">The comments pane can be shown or hidden.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">To show/hide the comments pane</p><hr /><ul><li style="text-align: left;">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_comments_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the title bar of a published model portal.</li></ul><h3><span><br />Navigating between comments and commented items</span></h3><p><span>Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.</span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commented_items_in_content_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="graphical_comments_in_content_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><h3>Searching for comments</h3><p>The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.</p><p><br /></p><p>To search for comments</p><hr /><ol><li>Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.</li><li>Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:<br /><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Unresolved</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a"> check box, to search only for unresolved comments.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>Unread</strong> check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>High priority</strong> check box, to search for comments with high priority.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li></ol><p><br />When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aee61b70-b6db-43dd-8790-860459ba750b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">839335150</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Related pages</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Structure of the document portal" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link><br /><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="App bar" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Navigation pane" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925516 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00053: Committing changes

- page_id: `191925516`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925516/Committing+changes
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:51.105+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every time you modify a model in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the changes are saved locally, so you have to commit them to Magic Collaboration Studio when you are done editing. If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.

To commit changes to Magic Collaboration Studio

1. In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).
2. In the **Uncommitted changes** dialog, enter the commit message in the **Commit message** field. This step is optional.
3. In the same dialog, Click the **Commit** button to commit model changes to Magic Collaboration Studio. Discarding model changesIf you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the **Discard** button in the **Uncommitted changes** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Committing model changes to Magic Collaboration Studio.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To commit changes to Magic Collaboration Studio</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog, enter the commit message in the <strong>Commit message</strong> field. This step is optional.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the same dialog, Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button to commit model changes to Magic Collaboration Studio.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d191a22c-cd4f-426f-ba5e-fded8b0c3c7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Discarding model changes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the <strong>Discard</strong> button in the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="committing_changes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Committing changes" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Committing model changes to Magic Collaboration Studio.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924372 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00054: Common Access Card

- page_id: `191924372`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924372/Common+Access+Card
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-05-20T13:46:13.176+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Basic configuration

In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in Web Application Platform.

Next, you need to enable certificate authentication:

```text

```

The next step is to configure which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (**authentication.certificate.username.template**), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (**authentication.certificate.displayname.template**).

Both of these values default to using (CN)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Configuring username

```text

```

The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name).

For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:

Template: *(CN)*, username: *JohnDoe*

Template: *(O)-(CN)*, username: *MyCompany*-*JohnDoe*

Template: *CERT_(CN)*, username: *CERT_JohnDoe*

##### Configuring the value displayed in the login button

To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the **authentication.certificate.displayname.template** property:

```text

```

For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is *CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB*, and the display template is *(CN) CERTIFICATE*, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.

For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication by certificate' space=''].

##### Configuring truststore

CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.

Certificate verification should be enabled in the *WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml* file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on [this page](https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig). E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:

- certificateVerification with the value optional or required (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed).
- truststoreFile - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.
- truststorePassword - truststore password.
- truststoreType - e.g. "JKS" or "PKCS12".

##### Certificate revocation list

The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url** or **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file**, and point it to the location of the revocation list.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

ac:macro-id="c9c5c09e-ae22-4145-8f7f-7646e7e8c473" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Basic configuration</h3><p>In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Web Application Platform.</span></p><p>Next, you need to enable certificate which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (<strong>authentication.certificate.username.template</strong>), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (<strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong>).</p><p>Both of these values default to using (CN)</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="login.png" ac:macro-id="e20ae9f6-f5bc-4ee9-b38d-e2f895e5e10d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.username.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name) </span>. </p><p>For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>(CN)</em>, username: <em>JohnDoe</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>(O)-(CN)</em>, username: <em>MyCompany</em>-<em>JohnDoe</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Template: <em>CERT_(CN)</em>, username: <em>CERT_JohnDoe</em></p><h3><span>Configuring the value displayed in the login button</span></h3><p>To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the <strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong> property:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a94afc71-b451-4bb8-bec3-e194fdeb8601"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is <em>CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB</em>, and the display template is <em>(CN) CERTIFICATE</em>, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.</p><p>For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Authentication by certificate" /></ac:link>.  </p><h3>Configuring truststore</h3><p>CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.</p><p>Certificate verification should be enabled in the <em>WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml</em> file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on <a href="https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig">this page</a>. E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:</p><ul><li><strong>certificateVerification</strong> with the value <em>optional</em> or <em> required </em>  (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed).</li><li><strong>truststoreFile</strong> - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.</li><li><strong>truststorePassword</strong> - truststore password.</li><li><strong>truststoreType</strong> - e.g. &quot;JKS&quot; or &quot;PKCS12&quot;.</li></ul><h3>Certificate revocation list</h3><p>The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url</strong> or <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file</strong>, and point it to the location of the revocation list.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51f3c881-22bb-4a81-85d3-737159d3974d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url=http://someserver.somedomain.com/revocation.lst
authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file=/opt/local/revovcation.lst]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925640 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: Comparing elements in different versions

- page_id: `191925640`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925640/Comparing+elements+in+different+versions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-05-30T13:27:22.579+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Other features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

You can track the evolution of your project and see how your elements have transformed over time by comparing diagrams and tables across different versions of a document.

##### Opening comparison screen

To compare elements in different document versions

1. Navigate to the document section you wish to compare in other versions.
2. At the top right, select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > **Compare versions.**The version comparison screen opens.

##### Structure of comparison screen

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

- Version drop-down. In the app bar at the top of the screen, you can select the versions you wish to compare by choosing them in the Version drop-down list.
- Comparison mode selection. In the centre of the app bar, you can select the mode for table or diagram comparison.
  - Fade: this mode allows you to transition between two overlapping diagrams. Use the sliding toolbar to adjust opacity and discern subtle differences. Fade mode is only available for diagrams. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Vertical: select this mode to view diagrams or tables side by side. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Horizontal: this mode shows diagrams or tables one below the other. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Filtering: when comparing tables, you can click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the table name and enter text to filter out table elements. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Use the sliding toolbar to adjust opacity and discern subtle differences.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf66a76-5e62-4833-a39b-320a874ddc81"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Fade mode is only available for diagrams.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br /><br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="926" ac:width="1080"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comparing_versions_cc_fade.gif"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comparing elements in different versions" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Vertical: </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">select this mode to view diagrams or tables side by side.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_comparison.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comparing elements in different versions" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Horizontal:</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> this mode shows diagrams or tables one below the other.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_comparison_horizontal.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comparing out table elements.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering__compared_tables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comparing elements in different versions" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=198377624 space=MCS2024xR2 version=6 -->
## PAGE 00056: Conditional user groups

- page_id: `198377624`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/198377624/Conditional+user+groups
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-08-06T10:58:54.238+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Creating users and user groups
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

In certain security-related authentication scenarios, user access rights must be determined dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account. Teamwork Cloud allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values by using the conditional user groups feature.

When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To allow users to create conditional user groups, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling conditional user groups' space='TWCloudTWRT']**Conditional user groups** option must be enabled in the Settings application.
- To define the conditions for conditional user groups, SAML attributes must already be configured on the SAML page of the Settings application.

To create a conditional user group

1. Go to the Users application
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating users and user groups' space='TWCloudTWRT'] or select to edit an existing user group.
3. On the group details pane, turn on the **Conditional user group** switch.
4. In the **Condition** box, enter the condition users must meet to be granted the group permissions. . When defining the condition for a conditional user group, make sure to use SAML attributes in the same way they are configured on the **SAML** page of the Settings application.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the user group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a conditional user group.

##### Condition syntax

###### Configuring SAML attributes

Conditions for conditional user groups accept only the SAML attributes configured in the*authserver.properties*file used for SAML integration.

To configure SAML attributes for conditional user groups

1. Open the <install_root> /AuthServer/config/authserver.properties file .
2. Uncomment the**authentication.saml.attributes**property and provide acomma-separated list of the SAML attributes you want to use for conditional groups as its value.
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

###### Condition operators

You can define the conditions for conditional user groups using the configured SAML attributes and the following operators:

- Boolean operators: AND; OR
- Equality operators: ==; !=
- Grouping of statements: ()

#### TIP: Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.

Example. When defining conditions, use SAML attributes the same way the are configured in your SAML integration.

(loginLocation == "Dallas" AND clearance == "Secret") OR department == "ITservices"

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924473 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00058: Configuring data logging

- page_id: `191924473`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924473/Configuring+data+logging
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:58:16.331+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the default data logging settings, including the logging level, for Web Application Platform and Magic Collaboration Studio as described below.

Web Application Platform uses the Apache Log4j logging framework. To learn more, see [https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/](https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/).

To configure the data logging

1. Go to the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the log4j2.properties file.
2. Depending on your needs do one or both of the following:
  - To configure the logging of the Web Application Platform-related information, define the following properties:
    - logger.webappplatform.name
    - logger.webappplatform.level
    - logger.webappplatform.additivity
    - logger.webappplatform.appenderRef.file.ref
  - To change the number and size of logs to be stored, and the log storage time, define the following properties:
    - appender.file.type
    - appender.file.name
    - appender.file.fileName
    - appender.file.filePattern
    - appender.file.layout.type
    - appender.file.layout.pattern
    - appender.file.policies.type
    - appender.file.policies.time.type
    - appender.file.policies.size.type
    - appender.file.policies.size.size
    - appender.file.strategy.type
    - appender.file.strategy.max
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Standard logging levels

Standard logging levels

The Log4J framework has the following standard logging levels: OFF, FATAL, ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE, ALL.

##### How to turn off logging

You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.

To turn off logging

1. Locate the logger you want to turn off.
2. Change the logging level of that logger to "off".
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Turning logging back on

Turning logging back on

To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from OFF to the logging level you want to use.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924467 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00059: Configuring email notifications for services

- page_id: `191924467`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924467/Configuring+email+notifications+for+services
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-15T07:31:59.246+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform services can generate email notifications and send them to a remote email system. To enable email notifications, configure Web Application Platform services, as described below.

To enable email notifications

1. Go to the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory.
2. Open for editing the *webappplatform.properties* file. In the *webappplatform.properties* file:The values of some properties are placeholders, e.g., **mail.from.default=info@company.com**. Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.
3. Specify the following properties under the *Platform mailing engine properties used to send notifications via e-mail* property group:
  - mail.host
  - mail.port
  - mail.username
  - mail.password
  - mail.protocol
  - mail.encoding
  - mail.from.default
4. If the email server uses SSL or TLS, specify the following properties under the Mailing engine TLS secure properties property group (otherwise, go to the next step):
  - mail.smtp.auth
  - mail.smtp.ssl.trust
  - mail.smtp.ssl.enable
  - mail.smtp.starttls.enable
5. Restart Web Application Platform.

##### Examples

Here are several examples of configuring Web Application Platform services to send email notifications:

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1a2e445c-946c-4d9e-a039-9ae91fa1be05">Specify the following properties</ac:inline-comment-marker> under the <em>Platform mailing engine properties used to send notifications via e-mail</em> property group:</p><ul><li><strong>mail.host</strong></li><li><strong>mail.port</strong></li><li><strong>mail.username</strong></li><li><strong>mail.password</strong></li><li><strong>mail.protocol</strong></li><li><strong>mail.encoding</strong></li><li><strong>mail.from.default</strong></li></ul></li><li>If the email server uses SSL or TLS, specify the following properties under the <em>Mailing engine TLS secure properties</em> property group (otherwise, go to the next step):<br /><ul><li><strong>mail.smtp.auth</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.ssl.trust</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.ssl.enable</strong></li><li><strong>mail.smtp.starttls.enable</strong></li></ul></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol><h3><br />Examples</h3><p>Here are several examples of configuring Web Application Platform services to send email notifications:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f464f036-bdd7-435e-918f-ae22f87c6b74"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Without SSL/TLS</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mail.protocol=smtp
mail.encoding=UTF-8
mail.host=gate.company.com
mail.port=25
mail.username=anonymous
mail.password=
mail.from.default=info@company.com]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41b4a47d-ca2e-419a-95f9-a0c6cfb6f9ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">With SSL</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[mail.protocol=smtp
