Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="commenting"><ri:page ri:content-title="Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Commenting is not disabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when publishing a model</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br />To edit a textual comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of a comment and select <strong>Edit</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4e50668-f830-4140-9cf5-e4cc1eceaefc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Displaying comment related actions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not see the icons of comment-related actions, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of a comment.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>When the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane (displayed below) opens on the right side of the document portal, do one or more of the following:<ol><li>Edit the comment title.</li><li>Edit the comment text.</li><li>Click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to change the comment priority.</li></ol><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_coment_pane.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps the comment is updated and displayed on the comments pane.</p><h3>Using custom comment template</h3><p>If the project you are commenting on has a custom comment template, you can use this template when creating a comment. Simply select the template from the Comment customization template drop-down to access custom selection lists, input fields, and other elements.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="using_custom_comment_template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating and editing textual comments" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Selecting a custom comment template.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89e8c7b6-d1f3-4366-aa78-8338894cad35"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information on setting up a custom comment template, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925530 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00073: Creating and managing document sections

- page_id: `191925530`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925530/Creating+and+managing+document+sections
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-08-06T12:53:45.568+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

You can create sections anywhere in a Cameo Collaborator document and reorder them in the scope of the current document view. New sections can later be filled with any type of content, like paragraphs and images.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To create sections in a Cameo Collaborator document:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space='MCS2024xR2'] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

##### Creating document sections

Follow the steps below to create a new section in a Cameo Collaborator document.

To create a document section

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'].
3. Click the Create section icon at the top of a section. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_section.png']
4. In the Section name box of Create section pane, enter the section name.
5. Click the Place section drop-down box and select one of the following options:
  - Below - to create a section below the open section.
  - Above - to create a section above the open section.
  - Inner section - to c reate a subsection within the open section, positioned one level deeper in the navigation tree hierarchy. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
7. Repeat steps 3 to 6 to create more sections or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

The newly created section is empty and only has a name. Now you can fill it with content, like [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating paragraphs' space='MCS2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding media' space='MCS2024xR2'].

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can change a section's name the same way you edit other items in a Cameo Collaborator document. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space='MCS2024xR2'].

##### Reordering document sections

You can reorder the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

Note that the position of a document section can only be changed within the scope of the current document view.

To change the position of a document section

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Manage sections .
2. In the **Manage sections** dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section whose position you what to change. The sections you can reorder are highlighted in the **Manage sections** dialog.
3. Drag the section to a new location, as shown in the figure below. If you want to move a section one level deeper in the document hierarchy, i.e., make it an inner section of another document section, drag and drop the section on top of another section. [ATTACHMENT filename='manage_sections_dialog.png']
4. Once you are happy with the new position of the document section, click the Save button on the bottom right corner of the window.

##### Deleting document sections

You can delete the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

To delete a document section

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Manage sections .
2. In the **Manage sections** dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section you want to delete. The sections you can delete are highlighted in the **Manage sections** dialog.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the section to delete it.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925483 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00074: Creating Cameo Collaborator comment templates

- page_id: `191925483`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925483/Creating+Cameo+Collaborator+comment+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-02-09T14:09:07.153+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the Profile Diagram to customize Cameo Collaborator comment templates according to your needs or specific project requirements. Add additional input fields and properties, such as version selection check boxes or a Jira issue input field, which users can see in the web interface comments panel.

Comment customization is only available for comments stored in the project.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment template

1. Open the project where you want to create a custom Cameo Collaborator comment template.
2. Create a Profile Diagram. To see the Profile Diagram option when creating a new diagram, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the dialog to enable the Expert mode.
3. In the Containment tree, navigate to Project Usages > Model [Document View Collaborator Profile] > Document View Collaborator Profile > Stereotypes .
4. Find the *«* CollaboratorComment [Comment]» stereotype and drag it into the Profile Diagram you created.
5. Create a profile in your Profile Diagram. 
In this part of the process, with the « CollaboratorComment [Comment]» stereotype applied and an empty profile created, your diagram should look something like this: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Create a stereotype element inside the newly created profile element. This is the stereotype that defines your custom comment template.
7. In the name field of the stereotype, enter the name for your custom template . This name will be displayed in the Cameo Collaborator user interface when a user creates a new comment and selects a comment template.
8. Create a generalization link from the new stereotype to the «CollaboratorComment» stereotype. The metaclass of the new stereotype changes to Comment: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click the Create Property button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_property.png'] to add a property . Supported property types: TypeMultiplicityResultString0Not requiredString0..1Not requiredString0..*Not requiredString1RequiredString1..*RequiredStringDefaultRequiredBoolean-ShownEnumeration0Not shownEnumeration0..1List, value is not requiredEnumeration0..*Check box, value is not requiredEnumeration1List, value is requiredEnumeration1..*Not shownEnumerationDefaultList, value is required **Explanation:**
  - If you add a S tring type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as an **input field;**
  - If you add a Boolean type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as a **switch**(with a true/false value);
  - If you add an Enumeration type, it shows in Cameo Collaborator as either a **selection list**or **check box(es)**, depending on the multiplicity.
10. Once you've finished editing the diagram, commit the project to the server. Now, users can use the template you created when adding comments in Cameo Collaborator.

**Example**In our custom template, let‘s display check boxes with the label **Version**and the following values: 
*2024x* 
*2024x Refresh1* 
*2024x Refresh2*

and provide an input field for a Jira issue URL.

To do this, we need to create an Enumeration type element and set the check box values as its properties:[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Then, we add a property, e.g. Version, to the «Project X custom comments»**stereotype, set its type to Versions*,*and its multiplicity to 0..* to display check boxes*. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

To add an input field for a Jira issue URL, we add a Jira Issue URL****property and set its type to String: 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After we commit the project to the server, users can see the****Project X custom comments**template with check boxes and Jira Issue URL input field in the Cameo Collaborator web interface: 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can use the Profile Diagram to customize Cameo Collaborator comment templates according to your needs or specific project requirements. Add additional input fields and properties, such as version selection check boxes or a Jira issue input field, which users can see <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">in the web interface comments panel.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2ced7d9-43a5-4f77-9a12-8b17f330d206"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span data-teams="true">Comment customization is only available for comments stored in the project.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a Cameo Collaborator comment template</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project where you want to create a custom Cameo Collaborator comment template.  </li><li>Create a Profile Diagram.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="404503c8-baa7-40e7-a3b5-a5e5c50cfbf5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To see the Profile Diagram option when creating a new diagram, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expert.png" /></ac:image> at the bottom of the dialog to enable the Expert mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the Containment tree, navigate to <strong>Project Usages &gt; Model [Document View Collaborator Profile] &gt; Document View Collaborator Profile &gt; Stereotypes</strong>.</li><li>Find the<strong><em> «</em></strong>CollaboratorComment [Comment]»<strong> </strong>stereotype and drag it into the Profile Diagram you created.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="08816bee-24f0-405f-959d-7ed33211d874">Create a profile in your Profile Diagram.<br />In this part of the process, with the «</ac:inline-comment-marker>CollaboratorComment [Comment]» <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">stereotype applied and an empty profile created, </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="08816bee-24f0-405f-959d-7ed33211d874">your diagram should look something like this:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="empty_profile_in_cc_comments_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="806b8b58-2812-4f40-baa5-bdca23361d29">Create a stereotype element inside the newly created profile element.</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="af9a9190-d2d4-4902-aa3f-e432b02c8217">This is the stereotype that defines your custom comment template.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>In the name field of the stereotype, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5b3611f5-cb91-4bb9-9790-5c62b9b0e845">enter the name for your custom template</ac:inline-comment-marker>. This name will be displayed in the Cameo Collaborator user interface when a user creates a new comment and selects a comment template.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2c57aa02-d0a2-4934-9ad0-2f19c1f37be6">Create a generalization link from the new stereotype to the «CollaboratorComment» stereotype. The metaclass of the new stereotype changes to Comment:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="stereotype_generalization_link_in_cc_comments_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Click the Create Property button <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="9"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_property.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR1" ri:content-title="Customization Element Properties" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to add <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d95cfa0c-5583-4174-ab9a-3ae54735819d">a property</ac:inline-comment-marker>. Supported property types:<br /><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 31.3248%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 31.7952%;" /><col style="width: 35.0904%;" /><col style="width: 33.1145%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;vertical-align: top;" scope="col">Type</th><th style="text-align: center;" scope="col">Multiplicity</th><th style="text-align: center;" scope="col">Result</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: center;">String</td><td style="text-align: center;">0</td><td style="text-align: center;">Not required</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: multiplicity.</span></li></ul></li><li><p>Once you've finished editing the diagram, commit the project to the server. Now, users can use the template you created when adding comments in Cameo Collaborator.</p></li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><strong>Example<br /></strong>In our custom template, let‘s display <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">check boxes with the label <strong>Version </strong>and the following values:</span><br /><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">2024x </span></em><br /><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">2024x Refresh1</span></em><br /><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">2024x Refresh2</span></em></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and provide an input field for a Jira issue URL.</span></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To do this, w</span></span>e need to create an Enumeration type element and set the check box values as its properties:</span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cc_custom_comments_enumeration_element.png" /></ac:image></span></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Then, we add a property, e.g. Version, to the «Project X custom comments»<em> </em>stereotype, set its type to Versions<em>, </em>and its multiplicity to 0..* to display check boxes<em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="version_property_cc_comments.png" /></ac:image></em></span></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To add an input field for a Jira issue URL, we add a Jira Issue URL<strong> </strong>property and set its type to String:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_comment_diagram_example.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">After we commit the project to the server, users can see the<em> </em><strong>Project X custom comments </strong>template with check boxes and Jira Issue URL input field in the Cameo Collaborator web interface:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="using_custom_comment_template.png" /></ac:image><br /><em> </em></span></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924476 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00075: Creating comment backup

- page_id: `191924476`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924476/Creating+comment+backup
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:48.281+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to create a comment backup for a specific document. You can save the backup as a plain text file for future use, e.g, system recovery.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

In order to create a comment backup and import comments from the backup file, the comments must be stored in a document (not in the project).

To create the comment backup for a Cameo Collaborator document

1. Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP GET request with the following URL and headers:
  - URL: https://<host>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/<uuid>/branch/<uuid>/comments/export. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS2024xR2'] you need to add to the URL. Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (<>) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.
  - Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS2024xR2'] .
2. Save the retrieved data as a plain text file.

The text file with retrieved data is your comment back up. You can use that backup to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document published from the same project.

To import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document from the backup file

1. Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP POST request with the following URL, headers, and body:
  - URL: https://<host>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/<uuid>/branch/<uuid>/comments/import. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS2024xR2'] you need to add to the URL. Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (<>) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.
  - Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS2024xR2'] .
  - Body: the data you retrieved from an HTTP GET request when exporting comments from the document. When you specify the body of the HTTP request, select the raw data type and JSON data format.
2. Reload the document to which you have imported comments.

After completing the above steps and reloading the document, the imported comments should be visible in the comments pane.

##### How to find document and branch IDs

When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to form a URL with document and branch IDs. The following steps explain how to find these IDs.

To find document and branch IDs

1. In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.
2. Select the "comments" HTTP request and locate Request URL in the request headers.
3. Find the target document and target branch IDs in the Request URL.

##### How to find HTTP request headers

When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to know the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent headers. The following steps explain how to find them.

To find Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers

1. In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.
2. Select the "comments" HTTP request.
3. Find the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925478 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: Creating comments and replies

- page_id: `191925478`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925478/Creating+comments+and+replies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-09-27T14:44:45.501+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments > Working with comments as model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When Cameo Collaborator comments are stored as model elements, you can create new textual comments and replies either directly in the diagram where the commented element is displayed or in the Containment tree.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in a diagram

1. Open the diagram where the element that you want to create a comment for is displayed.
2. In the diagram palette, click Collaborator Comment and click anywhere on the diagram pane to create a comment.
3. Enter comment text on the shape of the comment and click anywhere on the diagram pane to save it.
4. Create an Anchor from the comment to the commented element.
5. Find the new comment in the Containment tree of your project and move it to the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype. This Package name is typically the same as the project. Collaborator Comments PackageIf this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. Then place the newly created comment in this Package. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in that Package to be valid and visible in a published document.

The Cameo Collaborator Comment will be visible in a published document once you commit changes to the server and refresh the document. If you want to create comments in the Containment tree of your model, you can do it as described below.

To create a Cameo Collaborator comment in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of your model, right-click the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype and select **Create Element** > **Comment**. Collaborator Comments PackageIf this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. All Cameo Collaborator comments must be stored in such a Package to be valid and visible in a published document.
2. Type the text of the comment and press Enter.
3. Right-click the created comment and select Refactor > Convert To > More Elements > Collaborator Comment .
4. Open the Specification window of the new Cameo Collaborator comment, select the specification cell of the Annotated Element property, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
5. On the left side of the Select Elements dialog, select the element the comment is created for and click [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] to add it to the selected elements area.
6. Click OK and close the Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This figure shows Cameo Collaborator comments in a diagram and the Containment tree.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This Package name is typically the same as the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c7c7892-4b75-4f11-9c34-f8ffa251669d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Collaborator Comments Package</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If this is the first Cameo Collaborator comment created in the project and the Package with the «CollaboratorComments» stereotype does not exist, create a Package dedicated to comments and apply the <span>«CollaboratorComments» stereotype to it. ## PAGE 00077: Creating element values

- page_id: `191925510`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925510/Creating+element+values
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:51.004+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you can create values for stereotype tags and textual properties right in a Cameo Collaborator document. This will simplify your workflow because you will no longer need a modeling tool or any workarounds to add new values to your model.

To create a value for a stereotype tag

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to create a value for. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. On the bottom of the **Edit** pane, click the **Add new value** button.
4. When a new value card is added, enter the desired value.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a value for a stereotype tag in a Cameo Collaborator document.

To create a value for a textual property

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to create a value for. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. When the **Edit** pane opens, enter the desired value in the value card.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a value for a textual property in a Cameo Collaborator document.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925076 space=MCS2024xR2 version=4 -->
## PAGE 00078: Creating indexing configurations

- page_id: `191925076`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925076/Creating+indexing+configurations
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-03-16T12:58:29.113+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To improve the efficiency of resource usage, you can index resources and monitor their element usages within the selected scope of the Magic Collaboration Studio repository. This allows you to see if modifying or deleting certain model elements impacts other models that use these elements.

This chapter explains how to index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of the Magic Collaboration Studio repository so that later you could query these usages through the modeling tool.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To enable this functionality, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] . Elasticsearch allows you to index model element usage data and serve it efficiently when queried from a modeling tool.
- Only users with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User roles and permissions' space=''] can create indexing configurations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating indexing configurations.

To create indexing configurations

1. In the right-side menu of the Settings application, select **Indexing management**.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the screen as shown in the figure above.
3. In the open wizard, select the resource you want to create an indexing configuration for and click **Next**. NoteYou can index just one resource at a time. Note that it is possible to search for element usages of indexed resources only.
4. Select the scope to search for the element usages of the indexed resource, selected in step 3. Click **Next**. Note on ScopeThe scope defines the resources or categories in which the element usages will be searched for. You can select one or several resources or categories as the indexing scope. If you want to search for the indexed resource element usages in the whole repository, select the **Select all** check box at the top of the resource tree.Your chosen scope determines the speed of indexing and the required disk space. See more detailed information in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] section of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] page.
5. In the last step of the wizard, select one of the following indexing modes:
  - Automatic (default) - the resource is automatically indexed every time new project versions are committed in the indexing scope.
  - Manual - the resource is indexed only when indexing is initiated by an authorized user.
  - By specific commit tag - the resource is indexed only when new project versions are committed with a specified commit tag in the indexing scope. If you select this indexing mode, the Commit tag box appears where you need to specify the desired commit tag. [ATTACHMENT filename='by_specific_commit_tag.png']
6. Click the Create button.

The created indexing configuration automatically appears in the Indexing configurations list on the content pane. To refresh the indexing status, reload the web page

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To improve the efficiency of resource usage, you can index resources and monitor their element usages within the selected scope of the Magic Collaboration Studio repository. This allows you to see if modifying or deleting certain model elements impacts other models that use these elements.</span></p><p>This chapter explains how to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of the Magic Collaboration Studio repository so that later you could query these usages through the modeling tool.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="990ba527-bbea-4862-999a-2b1d12717355"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To enable this functionality,</span> you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install Elasticsearch and configure Magic Collaboration Studio]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Elasticsearch allows you to index model element usage data and serve it efficiently when queried from a modeling tool.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Only users with <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="User roles and permissions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the Server Administrator and Index Manager roles]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can create indexing configurations.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_index_configurations.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Creating indexing configurations.</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create indexing configurations</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the right-side menu of the Settings application, select <strong>Indexing management</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the screen as shown in the figure above.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the open wizard, select the resource you want to create an indexing configuration for and click <strong>Next</strong>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfbd7a1e-d206-44ab-a6ec-2d3b816d3ba4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can index just one resource at a time. Note that it is possible to search for element usages of indexed resources only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the scope to search for the element usages of the indexed resource, selected in step 3. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d70c606b-3f79-40cb-825c-4a775dac23ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note on Scope</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The scope defines the resources or categories in which the element usages will be searched for. You can select one or several resources or categories as the indexing scope. If you want to search for the indexed resource element usages in the whole repository, select the <strong>Select all</strong> check box at the top of the resource tree.</li><li>Your chosen scope determines the speed of indexing and the required disk space. See more detailed information in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Systemrequirements"><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[System requirements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the last step of the wizard, select one of the following indexing modes:<ul><li>Automatic (default) - the resource is automatically indexed every time new project versions are committed in the indexing scope.</li><li>Manual - the resource is indexed only when indexing is initiated by an authorized user.</li><li>By specific commit tag - the resource is indexed only when new project versions are committed with a specified commit tag in the indexing scope. If you select this indexing mode, the <strong>Commit tag</strong> box appears where you need to specify the desired commit tag.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="by_specific_commit_tag.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />The created indexing configuration automatically appears in the Indexing configurations list on the content pane. To refresh the indexing status, reload the web page</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925539 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: Creating paragraphs

- page_id: `191925539`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925539/Creating+paragraphs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-08-06T13:18:21.913+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

If a Cameo Collaborator document is published for editing, you can add new paragraphs to it with text, images, and hyperlinks to model elements or other document sections.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To create paragraphs in a Cameo Collaborator document:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space='MCS2024xR2'] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

To create a paragraph

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'].
3. Click the **Create paragraph** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to insert a paragraph. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Create paragraph** screen, enter the paragraph text. Text formattingUse the text formatting toolbar on the top of the **Create paragraph** screen to change the text font, size, color, alignment, etc. You can also use the toolbar to add hyperlinks, tables, and images to the paragraph. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_paragraph_content.png']
5. On the top right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the paragraph.
6. Repeat steps 3 to 5 to create more paragraphs or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] on the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

##### Editing paragraphs

You can edit the content of a paragraph created in a Cameo Collaborator document the same way you edit textual element values. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space='MCS2024xR2'].

##### Changing paragraph location

You can change the location of a paragraph created in Cameo Collaborator by cutting and pasting it in a new location.

To change a paragraph location

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'].
3. Click the Cut icon under the paragraph whose location you want to change. [ATTACHMENT filename='cutting_paragraph.png']
4. Click the **Paste** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to move the paragraph. TipIf you want to move the paragraph from one document section to another, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space='MCS2024xR2'] to open a different section of the same document view and then paste the paragraph. [ATTACHMENT filename='pasting_paragraph.png']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_editing.png'] in the bottom right corner of the content pane to exit the editing mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=194647936 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00080: Creating project branches

- page_id: `194647936`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/194647936/Creating+project+branches
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-11-12T16:33:35.448+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Resources application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create project branches directly in the Resources application without using a modeling tool.

To create a project branch

1. Go to the Resources application.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the right side of the project line and select Resource history .
3. On the line of the project version from which you want to create a branch, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Create branch . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_branch.png']
4. In the Create branch dialog, enter the branch name and comment (optional).
5. Click the Create button.

A new branch is created and displayed on the project history screen. You can also use the Resource app to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project branches' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create project branches directly in the Resources application without using a modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a project branch</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the Resources application.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the project line and select <strong>Resource history</strong>.</li><li>On the line of the project version from which you want to create a branch, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Create branch</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_branch.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Create branch</strong> dialog, enter the branch name and comment (optional).</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />A new branch is created and displayed on the project history screen. You can also use the Resource app to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing project branches" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[manage project branches]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925062 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00081: Creating remote resource configurations

- page_id: `191925062`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925062/Creating+remote+resource+configurations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:58:20.865+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

In Magic Collaboration Studio, you can create remote resource configurations allowing youto synchronize resources from one Magic Collaboration Studio server to another based on the selected synchronization trigger.This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. To learn how you can benefit from this functionality, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space='MCS2024xR2'].

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Be aware that **cross-cluster resource synchronization** is only supported between deployments running the **same major version**. While the **minor version** does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.

- To create remote resource configurations, you must have the Remote Synchronization Manager role. The role scope needs to include the target Magic Collaboration Studio server categories where you want the synchronized resources to be saved.
- Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.
- Remote resource configurations are created and managed on the target Magic Collaboration Studio server.

To create a remote resource configuration

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a remote resource configuration.
3. In the first step of the wizard, log in to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server:
  1. In the **Source Magic Collaboration Studio address** box, enter the address of the source server from which you want to synchronize resources or categories.
  2. In the **Username** and **Password** boxes, enter the credentials of a source server user.
  3. If needed, toggle the **Use Secure Connection (TLS)** button to enable/disable the secure connection. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Required source server user permissionsFor successful synchronization, the user whose credentials are used to log in to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server needs to have the Read Resources permission in the source server.
4. Click **Next**.
5. Select the source server resources and/or categories you want to synchronize with the target Teamwork Could server (the current server) and click **Next**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Select the category in the target Magic Collaboration Studio server where you want the synchronized resources to be stored and click **Next**.
7. Select one of the following synchronization triggers: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Manual** - the resource or category is synchronized only when initiated by a user.
  - **On every new commit** - the resource or category is synchronized whenever a new resource version becomes available in the source server.
  - On specific commit tag - the resource or category is synchronized when a resource is committed to the source server with a specific commit tag.
8. Click **Create**.

A separate remote resource configuration is created for every resource and/or category selected in step 5. All existing remote resource configurations and their details including synchronization status are displayed in the remote repository management screen as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The remote repository management screen where you can create and manage remote resource configurations.

In the following table, you can find the descriptions of synchronizationstatus.

| Status | Description |
| --- | --- |
| In queue | The resource is staged to be synchronized and now is in queue. |
| Not synchronized | The resource is not staged for synchronization and you need to trigger it manually. |
| Synchronized | The resource was synchronized successfully from the source server. |
| Partially synchronized | This is an alert, indicating that there is a more recent version available in the source server. You may want to consider re-synchronizing the resource if your use case requires having the latest version on the target server. Reproducible with manual synchronization configuration. |
| Error | There can be a couple of reasons why there is an error. A reason will be displayed in the tooltip while hovering over the status. |

##### Synchronizing resources manually

After creating a remote resource configuration, the related resource or category is synchronized depending on the selected synchronization mode. However, you can also synchronize resources manually as described below.

Manual

To synchronize resources manually

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. Select the remote resource configurations of the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
3. In the top right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='synchronize.png'] .

##### Deleting remote resource configurations

If remote resource configurations are no longer needed, you can delete them as described below.

To delete a remote resource configuration

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. Select the remote resource configurations you want to delete.
3. In the top right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
4. In the open dialog, choose one of the following:
  - **DELETE CONFIGURATION ONLY**- choose to delete only the configuration, leaving synchronized categories/resources in the target server.
  - **DELETE ALL**- choose to delete the configuration along with the synchronized resources and/or categories.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94d0dcd4-7774-4e7e-9cfb-51040c899fa8"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In Magic Collaboration Studio, you can create remote resource configurations allowing you </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">to synchronize resources from one Magic Collaboration Studio server to another based on the selected synchronization trigger. </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. To learn how you can benefit from this functionality, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Cross-cluster resource synchronization" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross-cluster resource synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cde10e88-3be8-4dba-b27e-f63e0cadfdf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be aware that <strong>cross-cluster resource synchronization</strong> is only supported between deployments running the <strong>same major version</strong>. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.</li><li>Remote resource configurations are created and managed on the target Magic the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the wizard for creating a remote resource configuration.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the first step of the wizard, log in to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server:</span><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Source Magic Collaboration Studio address</strong> box, enter the address of the source server from which you want to synchronize resources or categories.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Username</strong> and <strong>Password</strong> boxes, enter the credentials of a source server user.</span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If needed, toggle the <strong>Use Secure Connection (TLS)</strong> button to enable/disable the secure connection.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tls_slider.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1575fcd-054f-4e67-82f7-ea7d6bf9a1f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Required source server user permissions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For successful synchronization, the user whose credentials are used to log in to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server needs to have the Read Resources permission in the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the source server resources and/or categories you want to synchronize with the target Teamwork Could server (the current server) and click <strong>Next</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_resources.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the category in the target Magic Collaboration Studio server where you want the synchronized resources to be stored and click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select one of the following synchronization triggers:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Manual</strong> - the resource or category is synchronized only when initiated by a user.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>On every new commit</strong> - the resource or category is synchronized whenever a new resource version becomes available in the source server.</span></li><li><strong>On specific commit tag</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> - the resource or category is synchronized when a resource is committed to the source server with a specific commit tag.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ul><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronization_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Create</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br />A separate remote resource configuration is created for every resource and/or category selected in step 5. All existing remote resource configurations and their details including synchronization status are displayed in the remote repository management screen as shown below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remote_repository_management.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The remote repository management screen where you can create and manage remote resource configurations.</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the following table, you can find the descriptions of <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">synchronization </span>status.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Status</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>In queue</strong></td><td>The resource is staged to be synchronized and now is in queue.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Not synchronized</strong></td><td>The resource is not staged for synchronization and you need to trigger it manually.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Synchronized</strong></td><td>The resource was synchronized successfully from the source server.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Partially synchronized</strong></td><td>This is an alert, indicating that there is a more recent version available in the source server. You may want to consider re-synchronizing the resource if your use case requires having the latest version on the target server. Reproducible with manual synchronization configuration.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Error</strong></td><td>There can be a couple of reasons why there is an error. A reason will be displayed in the tooltip while hovering over the status.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3 style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Synchronizing resources manually</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After creating a remote resource configuration, the related resource or category is synchronized depending on the selected synchronization mode. However, you can also synchronize resources manually as described below.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1b8b1e8-a5ac-433a-a480-c50585fe30ca"><ac:rich-text-body>If you select the <strong>Manual</strong> synchronization mode for a remote resource configuration, you need to synchronize resources by yourself as described in this section.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To synchronize resources manually</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Remote repository management</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the remote resource configurations of the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.</span></li><li>In the top right corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronize.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><h3 style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Deleting remote resource configurations</h3><p>If remote resource configurations are no longer needed, you can delete them as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a remote resource configuration</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Remote repository management</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the remote resource configurations you want to delete.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /></span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the open dialog, choose one of the following:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">DELETE CONFIGURATION ONLY</span> </strong>- choose to delete only the configuration, leaving synchronized categories/resources in the target server. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">DELETE ALL</span> </strong>- choose to delete the configuration along with the synchronized resources and/or categories.</span></li></ul></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925201 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00082: Creating resource usage maps

- page_id: `191925201`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925201/Creating+resource+usage+maps
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:58:22.090+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Resource Usage Map
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Usage Map application allows you to create usage maps for one, several or all the projects in the Magic Collaboration Studio server. In addition, you can have have multiple usage maps open simultaneously to compare the usages of different projects as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a resource usage map.

To create a resource usage map

1. In the Resource Usage Map application, click the Select icon. If there are other usage maps open already, the **Select** icon is located at the top left corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map application structure' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. In the open dialog, select the resources whose usages you want to see in the resource usage map by doing one of the following:
  - To select one or several resources, start typing the resource name in the search box and select the desired resources from the search results.
  - To select all resources in one or several categories, select to search for categories as shown in the image below, start typing the category name in the search box, and select the desired categories form the search results.
  - To select all Teamwork Cloud projects, select the **Select all** check-box on the top of the dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the **Select** button.

After completing the above steps, the usage map of the selected resources is displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map application structure' space='MCS2024xR2'] of the web application portal. You can use the filters on the left side of the portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. Tonavigate through all existing usage maps, select a respective tab on the top of the content pane. You can alsoselect a resource in the map to view its details.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00083: Creating roles

- page_id: `191924854`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924854/Creating+roles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-07-07T08:50:47.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Roles application > Managing roles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1062821924

1062821927

1062821926

The **Role**application allows you to create a role and select the permissions you want the role to have. For example, if you have users who will be responsible for viewing resources and making changes, you can assign the Author role to them. This role is a custom role therefore, you can edit or delete it if need be.

To create a user role

1. On the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_role.png'] to open the Create role pane.
2. Type the name of a user role and d esc r i pt i on (opt i o n a l ) .
3. Se l ect at l east o ne pe rmi ssion t o assign t o th e ro l e .
4. Click the Save button.

You can assign the newly created role to one or more users/groups by clicking the **Change** button in the **Role assignments**card. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space='MCS2024xR2']** opens and you will be able to add users/groups.

To set a custom scope to the newly created role, you must add resource(s).

1062821923

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space='MCS2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing custom roles' space='MCS2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=198377607 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00084: Creating users and user groups

- page_id: `198377607`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/198377607/Creating+users+and+user+groups
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-11-18T10:12:12.975+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

##### Creating users

To create a new user account

1. From the left side users/user groups filter select Users.
2. In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button [ATTACHMENT filename='Action_button.png'] and select a Create User button.
3. In the **Create User** pane, fill in the required fields. A text box with a red asterisk (*) means that the information is mandatory. Thus, the **Create** button will be enabled only if you fill in the **Username**, **Password,** and **Confirm password** text boxes.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner to save the user account.

#### TIP: Enable or disable user access

Enable or disable user access

When creating a new user or editing user information, you may select to either enable or disable their access. If you want the user to access the Teamwork Cloud system and carry out the tasks assigned, select **Enable** (selected by default). If you clear the **Enable**, the user's status will be disabled and thus he or she cannot log into Teamwork Cloud Admin and Teamwork Cloud in the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the user account is saved, the **User details**pane will be opened, here you can:

- Edit user details,
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space='MCS2024xR2'] ,
- Assign a user to a group.

##### Creating user groups

To create new user groups

1. From the left users/user groups filter select **User groups**.
2. In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button [ATTACHMENT filename='Action_button.png'] and Select a Create user group button, and in Create user group pane fill in the required fields.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner to save the user group.

After the user group is saved, the **Group details**pane will be opened, here you can:

- Edit group details,
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space='MCS2024xR2'] ,
- Assign users to a group.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Users application structure' space='MCS2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing users and groups' space='MCS2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing users' space='MCS2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Converting users to internal or external' space='MCS2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Copying roles and assignments' space='MCS2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing users' space='MCS2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing user roles and assignments' space='MCS2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Thus, the <strong>Create</strong> button will be enabled only if you fill in the <strong>Username</strong>, <strong>Password,</strong> and <strong>Confirm password</strong> text boxes. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_resource_details.png" /></ac:image></span> on the top right corner to save the user account. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea1e4e0e-f781-4f83-8df0-7db7e11c838d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Enable or disable user access</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When creating a new user or editing user information, you may select to either enable or disable their access. If you clear the <strong>Enable</strong>, the user's status will be disabled and thus he or she cannot log into Teamwork Cloud Admin and Teamwork Cloud in the modeling details </strong>pane will be opened, here you can: </p><ul><li>Edit user details,</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing custom roles" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Assign roles]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</li><li>Assign a user to a group.</li></ul><h3>Creating user groups</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create new user groups</p><hr /><ol><li><p>From the left users/user groups filter select <strong>User groups</strong>.</p></li><li>In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="28"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Action_button.png" /></ac:image> and Select a <strong>Create user group</strong> button, and in <strong>Create user group</strong> pane fill in the required fields.</li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_resource_details.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner to save the user group. </p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>After the user group is saved, the <strong>Group details </strong>pane will be opened, here you can: </p><ul><li>Edit group details,</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing custom roles" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Assign roles]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</li><li>Assign users to a group.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Users application structure" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing users and groups" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing users" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Converting users to internal or external" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Copying roles and assignments" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing users" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Changing passwords]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing user roles and assignments" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924456 space=MCS2024xR2 version=6 -->
## PAGE 00085: Cross-cluster resource synchronization

- page_id: `191924456`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924456/Cross-cluster+resource+synchronization
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-06-30T09:17:09.113+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Be aware that **cross-cluster resource synchronization** is only supported between deployments running the **same major version**. While the **minor version** does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.

**On this page**

4

Magic Collaboration Studio offers a one-way resource synchronization functionality allowing you to move resources from one Magic Collaboration Studio server to another and periodically update them. This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. Therefore you can use resource synchronization as an alternative to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration between disconnected teams' space='MD2024xR2'] and then updating server projects with local changes. In addition, you can perform a one-time synchronization operation to offload resources to another server for archiving.

- Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.
- The synchronization feature checks project uniqueness by Project ID, Resource ID, and Project Name.

##### Using resource synchronization for collaborating

You can periodically synchronize Magic Collaboration Studio resources from the source server to the target server so that other users can use synchronized projects in other projects stored on the target server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting up a periodic synchronization operation to share resources that can be reused on the target server.

To use resource sync for collaboration between disconnected Magic Collaboration Studio sites

1. On the target Magic Collaboration Studio server, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating remote resource configurations' space=''] to synchronize selected resources from the source to the target server:
  1. Connect to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
  2. Select the category on the target Magic Collaboration Studio server where you want the synced resources to be stored.
  3. Select one of the following synchronization triggers: on every commit, on commit with a specific tag, or manual synchronization
2. Use the synchronized resources in your projects stored on the target server.
3. Update synchronized resources when new project versions become available.

- The source server determines all of the resource's metadata. Therefore, if you change the name of a synchronized project on the source server, the name also changes on the target server on subsequent synchronization iteration. If you change the name of a synchronized project on the target server, the name changes back to the one on the source server during synchronization.
- If you move a synchronized project to a different category on the source server, the project continues synchronizing as before. If you do so on the target server, on subsequent synchronization iteration, the synchronized project returns to its original category specified in the synchronization configuration. However, if a whole category is being synchronized and you remove a resource from that category, this resource is not synchronized anymore. Only the remaining resources in the category are synchronized further.

###### Multi-hop synchronization

In the case of external collaboration between separate entities, direct network connectivity is usually not permitted between the two parties due to IT-level security constraints. As a remedy to this issue, the cross-cluster sync feature can be set up to support the so-called multi-hop deployment topology. That is, a separate Magic Collaboration Studio instance can be spun up in a neutral environment outside of the involved organizations' network, so the need to connect the two together is eliminated. Instead, resources can be shared and synced between the source and target in multiple hops through the common/neutral Magic Collaboration Studio repository in the middle.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing resources in multiple hops via a common or neutral environment.

##### Using resource synchronization for resource offloading

If there are dormant recourses in your Magic Collaboration Studio server, you can move them to another server for archiving to free up space on the main server (e.g., production server).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting up a one-time synchronization operation to move resources to another server.

To use resource sync for moving resources to another Magic Collaboration Studio server

1. On the target Magic Collaboration Studio server (e.g., archive server), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating remote resource configurations' space=''] for a single time synchronization operation:
  1. Connect to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server (e.g., production server) and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
  2. Select the category on the target Magic Collaboration Studio server where you want the synced resources to be stored.
  3. Select one the **On every new commit** synchronization trigger.
2. Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Manager' space=''] to remove the synced resources from the source server to free up server space.
3. Remove the remote resource configuration created in step 1.

###### Removing the read-only flag from offloaded resources

If you have used resource synchronization to offload resources to a remote server, these resources are read-only and you can no longer edit them. However, there is a way to remove the read-only flag from synchronized resources and make them active again.

Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.

The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.

To remove the read-only flag from offloaded resources

- Run [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Manager' space=''] and execute the following command: Before running Data Manager, stop Magic Collaboration Studioservices to prevent users from accessing the database. bashDJangotrue,--categoryID=,>]]> Exampledatamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48

##### Configuring TLS encrypted connection for cross-cluster synchronization

###### Certificate Authority-signed certificate

If the source server has TLS set up with a Certificate Authority (CA)-signed certificate, no further actions are necessary. The target server will connect to source automatically using TLS.

###### Internal or self-signed certificate

In order to enable TLS encrypted connection with either a self-signed certificate or certificate signed by your organization internally, update the Java cacerts trustores on the target Magic Collaboration Studio server. Both the Java cacerts used by Magic Collaboration Studio and Web Application Platform on the target server will have to be updated with the source server's public certificate.

Copy the source Magic Collaboration Studio server's public certificate to the target server. Use the **keytool import**command to add the source server certificate to the target server's Java cacerts truststore. This command will need to be executed with the Magic Collaboration Studio Java keytool and Web Application Platform's Java keytool.

```bash
-alias ]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
