<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925600 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00086: Customizing PDF/HTML templates

- page_id: `191925600`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925600/Customizing+PDF+HTML+templates
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:51.994+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.

In a custom document template, you can alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font or add a custom logo to the document.

For more information, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.</p><p>In a custom document template, you can alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font or add a custom logo to the document.</p><p>For more information, refer to the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925292 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00087: Customizing the appearance of a project portal

- page_id: `191925292`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925292/Customizing+the+appearance+of+a+project+portal
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-11-04T08:45:30.724+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

You can customize the appearance of a published project by customizing the template you plan to use for publishing. The best practice is to save an existing predefined template with a different name, and then make changes to the newly created template file. After creating a new template file, you need to use it in the project you intend to publish. There is the option to change the default color of the title bar, document header and text, to add or remove the search button, and other style options. The procedure bellow describes how to modify the *Entire Model* template (stored as *Cameo Collaborator Library.mdzip)*, but you can also modify other predefined templates the same way.

To customize the appearance a project portal

1. Go to the modelLibraries folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and open the Cameo Collaborator template you want to use for publishing as a separate project.
2. Save the project in the same directory using a different name (e.g., Custom template.mdzip ).
3. When you receive the warning not to edit standard/system profiles and libraries, click Continue .
4. Open a View Diagram of the template and double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype to open its properties Specification window.
5. Specify the values of the following properties:
  - 
    - Primary Color Palette - defines the color of the app bar in the project portal.
    - Secondary Color Palette - defines the color of the main portal icons, e.g., icons for creating and saving a comment.
6. Click Close and save the project.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The sample of a project portal showing the colors that can be customized.

##### Setting the default scope for publishing

In the custom template, you can set the default scope for publishing. When the default scope is set, you don't need to specify it while publishing the project. In this case, the default scope is published even if you specify another scope in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog.

To set the default scope for publishing

1. If you haven't done so already, use your custom template in the project you intend to publish.
2. Open the View Diagram of the custom template.
3. In the Containment tree, select the package you want to set as the default scope for publishing (e.g. Categorization ) and drag it onto the diagram pane.
4. Draw the «Expose» dependency from the element with the «Document» stereotype to the package. [ATTACHMENT filename='setting_scope.png'] The figure displays the View Diagram of the Entire Model template with a default scope that is specified through the «Expose» dependency.
5. If you want to add more packages to the default scope, repeat steps 3 and 4.
6. Save the project.
7. Select the custom template in the Cameo Collaborator Publisher dialog when you publish the project to Cameo Collaborator.

##### Setting the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document

The home page of a published project is the diagram that opens by default after the project portal is loaded. It can be set directly in the model that you intend to publish.

To more easily set the home page of your Cameo Collaborator document, see *Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip*file provided with Cameo Collaborator Publisher. The *2. Diagrams in a Package Template*sample of this fileshows you how to create a document displaying all diagrams from a specific package with one diagram set as the home page.

To set the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. If you use a default template to publish Cameo Collaborator documents, proceed to step 2. If you use a custom template do the following:
  1. Open the custom template you intend to use for publishing.
  2. Open the Specification window of the View element which will be used to display the diagram you want to set as the home page.
  3. Click the specification cell of the Active Element property and in the drop-down list select the FindLandingPage value.
  4. Set the **Active Landing Page** property to *true*. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Using multiple ViewsIf your custom template has multiple Views, set the **Active Landing Page** for one View only.
  5. Close the Specification window and save the template.
2. Open the model you intend to publish.
3. Make sure that the Document View Collaborator Profile is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MD2024xR2'] . You can find it in the <intall.root>\profiles directory.
4. In the model browser, right-click the diagram you want to display as the home page of the project portal and select Home Page . [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_home_page.png']

After completing the above steps, the «HomePage» stereotype is applied to the selected diagram. When you publish your model, a diagram with this stereotype is automatically displayed as the home page of a project portal.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong> On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa91ade6-d843-40d6-9c39-55f746636abc"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>You can customize the appearance of a published project by customizing the template you plan to use for publishing. The best practice is to save an existing predefined template with a different name, and then make changes to the newly created template file.</span> After creating a new template file, you need to use it in the project you intend to publish. There is the option to change the default color of the title bar, document header and text, to add or remove the search button, and other style options. The procedure bellow describes how to modify the <em>Entire Model</em> template (stored as <em>Cameo Collaborator Library.mdzip)</em>, but you can also modify other predefined templates the same way.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize the appearance a project portal</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>modelLibraries</em> folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and open the Cameo Collaborator template you want to use for publishing as a separate project.</li><li>Save the project in the same directory using a different name (e.g., <em>Custom template.mdzip</em>).</li><li>When you receive the warning not to edit standard/system profiles and libraries, click <strong>Continue</strong>.</li><li>Open a View Diagram of the template and double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype to open its properties Specification window.</li><li>Specify the values of the following properties:<br /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Primary Color Palette</strong> - defines the color of the app bar in the project portal.</li><li><strong>Secondary Color Palette</strong> - defines the color of the main portal icons, e.g., icons for creating and saving a comment.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> and save the project.</li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="customizing_project_portal.png" /></ac:image> <br /><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">The sample of a project portal showing the colors that can be customized.</span></h6><h3>Setting the default scope for publishing</h3><p>In the custom template, you can set the default scope for publishing. When the default scope is set, you don't need to specify it while publishing the project. In this case, the default scope is published even if you specify another scope in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog. </p><p><br /></p><p>To set the default scope for publishing</p><hr /><ol><li>If you haven't done so already, <a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD185/Using+other+projects+in+a+project">use your custom template in the project</a> you intend to publish.</li><li>Open the View Diagram of the custom template.</li><li>In the Containment tree, select the package you want to set as the default scope for publishing (e.g. <em>Categorization</em>) and drag it onto the diagram pane.</li><li>Draw the «Expose» dependency from the element with the «Document» stereotype to the package.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="setting_scope.png" /></ac:image><br />The figure displays the View Diagram of the <em>Entire Model</em> template with a default scope that is specified through the «Expose» dependency.<br /><br /></li><li>If you want to add more packages to the default scope, repeat steps 3 and 4.</li><li>Save the project.</li><li>Select the custom template in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog when you publish the project to Cameo Collaborator.</li></ol><h3><br />Setting the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document</h3><p>The home page of a published project is the diagram that opens by default after the project portal is loaded. It can be set directly in the model that you intend to publish.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c37cdaf3-a80d-4ce3-bc38-1126c5430c38"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To more easily set the home page of your Cameo Collaborator document, see <em>Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip </em>file provided with Cameo Collaborator Publisher. The <em>2. Diagrams in a Package Template </em>sample of this file </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">shows you how to create a document displaying all diagrams from a specific package with one diagram set as the home page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To set the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document</p><hr /><ol><li>If you use a default template to publish Cameo Collaborator documents, proceed to step 2. If you use a custom template do the following:<ol><li>Open the custom template you intend to use for publishing.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the View element which will be used to display the diagram you want to set as the home page.</li><li>Click the specification cell of the <strong>Active Element</strong> property and in the drop-down list select the <strong>FindLandingPage</strong> value.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set the <strong>Active Landing Page</strong> property to <em>true</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_window_of_a_view.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f92a329-8262-4937-9183-39f87d27ac55"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Using multiple Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your custom template has multiple Views, set the <strong>Active Landing Page</strong> for one View only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Close the Specification window and save the template.</li></ol></li><li>Open the model you intend to publish.</li><li>Make sure that the Document View Collaborator Profile is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[used in your model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can find it in the <em>&lt;intall.root&gt;\profiles</em> directory.</li><li>In the model browser, right-click the diagram you want to display as the home page of the project portal and select <strong>Home Page</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_home_page.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p>After completing the above steps, the «HomePage» stereotype is applied to the selected diagram. When you publish your model, a diagram with this stereotype is automatically displayed as the home page of a project portal.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924454 space=MCS2024xR2 version=6 -->
## PAGE 00088: Data Manager

- page_id: `191924454`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924454/Data+Manager
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-08-18T14:49:25.070+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio administration
- labels: ['reset-admin-password']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

Data Manager is a Magic Collaboration Studio Java utility for interfacing directly with the Cassandra database. The Data Manager package can be found in the *Utilities*directory of your server installation, and as a portable zip package in the 3DS installer distribution.

Data Manager is used to make permanent and irrecoverable modifications to the Cassandra database. A full database backup should always be performed before making any modifications to the database.

Always use the Data Manager package included with your version of Magic Collaboration Studio distribution.

Running Data Manager

To run Data Manager

1. Stop Magic Collaboration Studio services to prevent users from accessing the database.
2. Ensure Cassandra is up and running: use the nodetool status command to verify the database status is Up and Normal .
3. Run a Data Manager command. If Data Manager is executed for the first time on the system, you will be prompted to provide the path to Java. Use the same version of Java as Magic Collaboration Studio. Execute with the *-help*option to ensure Data Manager is working: bashDJangotrue
4. After finishing all Data Manager steps, start Magic Collaboration Studio services to resume user access.

##### Data Manager configuration

Data Manager has its own configuration files, located in the Data Manager installation path, under *configuration*.

###### Database access

For best performance, run Data Manager on the Cassandra server. By default, Data Manager will access the database via Localhost. If you need to run Data Manager remotely from the Cassandra server, modify the**contact-points**field for **datastax-java-driver******in application.conf.

###### Cassandra Authentication

Data Manager will connect to Cassandra on localhost using anonymous authentication. If your Cassandra node is configured to bind to a specific IP address (by default, port 9042 binds to all interfaces), or if you have configured Cassandra to use an authenticator other than *AllowAllAuthenticator*, you will need to modify the**auth-provider******fields in**application.conf accordingly.

###### Logs

Data Manager will append to a detailed log file on each execution. The datamanager.log file is stored in the logs directory of the Data Manager installation path. You can change the location of the log file and the level of logging in logback.xml.

##### Data Manager commands

The following table outlines the commands you can use in Data Manager to manage your data.

| Server tool command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| -help | Displays Help information. |
| -clbl, --clear-liquibase-locks | Clears liquibase locks after an interrupted liquibase migration. |
| -rbs, --recalculate-blob-size | Recalculates blob size of the resources. This should be used when the blob size is too large due to an overly big resource.This process can take a while, depending on the size of the database. |
| -dk, --drop-keyspace <keyspace=value> --silent-drop | Drops persisten keyspace to free up the storage space.Add --silent-drop to run the command silently, i.e., without requesting user permission.You need to clear the removed keyspace snapshots using the nodetool utility command:]]> |
| -dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> | Deletes the given resource(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple resource IDs using space as a separator.If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server. |
| -drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>> | Deletes the given branch(es) and all of its related information. Specify multiple branch ID using a comma as a separator.includeChildBranch has two values: true and false. When the includeChildBranch value is set to false, and the branch has child branches, this branch will not be deleted.When the includeChildBranch value is set to true, the branch including its child branches will be deleted.This command does not work for the branches of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its branches. |
| -drd,--delete-marked-deleted-resources | Deletes all resources that were marked as deleted.If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server. |
| -drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>> | Deletes the given revision(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple revisions using a comma as a separator.includeChildRevision has two values: true and false. When the includeChildRevision value is set to false, and the revision is not the latest, it will not be deleted.When the includeChildRevision value is set to true, the upward revisions and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.This command does not work for the revisions of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its revisions. |
| -du,--delete-user <username> | Deletes the given username(s) and all of its related information.To specify multiple usernames, use comma as a separator.If the username consists of two words (for example, dan smith) add double quotation marks (" ") before and after the username:text |
| -rrof, --remove-read-only-flag <-resourceID=<value1>,<value2> --categoryID=<value1>,<value2>> | Removes the read-only flag from a synchronized resource or category in the target server. Specify multiple resources or categories using a comma as a separator, for example:Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again. |
| -lb,--list-branch <resourceID=value> | Lists all branches of the given resource. |
| -lc,--list-cluster-ids | Lists previous cluster IDs and the current one. |
| -sgdb,--set-global-deny-edit-branch <resourceID=<value> branchID=<value> globalDenyEdit=<true/false>> | Sets whether the given branch should be denied to edit by global. |
| -sgdr,--set-global-deny-edit-resource <resourceID=<value1>,<value2> globalDenyEdit=<true/false>> | Sets whether the given resources should be denied to edit by global.Exampledatamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 |
| -lgdr,--list-global-deny-edit-resource <--resourceID=<value1>,<value2>> | Lists the status of deny edit by global of the given resource. |
| -lgdb,--list-global-deny-edit-branch <--resourceID=<value1>,<value2>> | Lists the status of deny edit by global for all branches of the given resource. |
| -lcat,--list-categories | Lists all the categories and their IDs. |
| -li,--list-index | Lists all available data that have indexes. |
| -lr,--list-resources | Lists all available resources. |
| -lrd,--list-marked-deleted-resources | Lists all resources that are marked as deleted. |
| -lrv,--list-revision <resourceID=value branchID=value> | Lists all revisions in the given branch. |
| -lu,--list-users <--no-info> | Lists all usernames and their information, sorted by name.You can add --no-info to show only the usernames. |
| -ram,--reset-administrator | Resets admin password, status, and permissions.If there is no Administrator account, it will be created.If the Administrator account exists in the database:If the account is for an external user, it will be reset to an internal user with a new password. This user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.If the account is an internal user, only a new password will be generated, and the user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.The default roles of the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be restored to make sure that this account can fully operate as the default administrator. |
| -rc,--recreate-cluster-id | Recreates a cluster ID. |
| -ri,--rebuild-index <data=value1,value2> | Rebuilds all indexes for the given data and uses the data=* option to rebuild all indexes of all available data. |
| -urd,--unmark-deleted-resources <resource ID> | Unmarks the given resource(s) previously marked as deleted. Specify multiple resource ID using space as a separator. |
| -vb,--validate-blob <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value --readAllWithCQL --createCompareOfThriftAndCQL> | Validates blob information. |
| -ve,--validate-eobject <resourceID=value1,value2 saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates the eObject information of a specific resource ID and uses the resourceID=* option to validate all resources. |
| -version | Displays the version of Data Manager. |
| -vi,--verify-index <data=value1,value2> | Verifies all indexes for the given data and uses the data=* option to verify all indexes of all available data. |
| -vr,--validate-rbac <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates a user, user group, role, and role assignment information. |
| -vrc,--validate-resource-commit <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates a metamodel, branch, and committed information. |
| -col,--cleanup-object-locks | Removes released object locks (that are no longer used) from the database.How to safely execute this command:Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal nodetool repair which you can find in the Cassandra/bin directory. Once nodetool repair is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal nodetool compact. After running the command in nodetool compact terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed. |
| -rcc,--rebuild-change-count <--resourceID=<resourceID1>,<resourceID2>> | Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.Example:datamanager -rccdatamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c |
| -crb,--clean-resource-blobs <--resourceID=<resourceID1>,<resourceID2>> | Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional resourceID argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned. |
| -cu,--change-username <username=value newUsername=value> | Changes the given username and generates a new password. For usernames containing a space, put double quotes, ", around the username. Example:datamanager -cu username="John Old" newUsername="Andrew New" |
| -ll,--list-ldap | Lists all LDAP realm information. |
| -rlu, --remap-ldap-users | Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.Exampledatamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b |
| -vlu,--validate-ldap-users <saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration. |
| -udb,--unmark-deleted-branches <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2>> | Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted. |

The **delete** command to delete resources and users works as follows.

###### Deleting selected resource

```bash
...]]>
```

These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:

- Tags information of the resource.
- Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories).
- Branch information.
- Commit information.
- Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed).
- Locking information.

###### Deleting selected user

```bash
, ...]]>
```

This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:

- Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).
- Session (the existing user session will be removed).
- Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).
- User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).

#### NOTE: Notes on deleting usernames

Notes on deleting usernames

- For usernames consisting of two words (such as *dan smith*), add double quotation marks (" ") before and after the username, as shown: text
- In the modeling tool's history panels, the word deleted will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.

###### Deleting selected branch

```bash
, includeChildBranch=>]]>
```

The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:

- Tags information of each revision in the branch
- Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch
- Commit information of each revision in the branch
- Locking information of each object locked in the branch
- Branch information of the branch
- If the branch has child branches, depending on the includeChildBranch value, the branch will be deleted or not:
  - When the includeChildBranch value is set to False and the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.
  - When the includeChildBranch value is set to True, then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.

###### Deleting revisions

```bash
includeChildRevision=>]]>
```

The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:

- Tags information of the revision
- Object information created/modified at the revision
- Commit information of the revision
- Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to
- Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch
- If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the includeChildRevision value, the revision will be deleted or not:
  - When the includeChildRevision value is set to False, and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.
  - When the includeChildRevision value is set to T rue, then revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If you need to run Data Manager remotely from the Cassandra server, modify the </span><strong>contact-points </strong>field for <strong>datastax-java-driver</strong><span><strong> </strong>in application.conf.</span></p><h4><span>Cassandra Authentication</span></h4><p><span>Data Manager will connect to Cassandra on localhost using anonymous authentication. You can change the location of the log file and the level of logging in logback.xml.</span></p><h3>Data Manager commands</h3><p>The following table outlines the commands you can use in Data Manager to manage your data. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Server tool command</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><p>-help</p></td><td>Displays Help information.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-clbl, --clear-liquibase-locks</p></td><td>Clears liquibase locks after an interrupted liquibase migration.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-rbs, --recalculate-blob-size</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Recalculates blob size of the resources. This should be used when the blob size is too large due to an overly big resource.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd1424db-54b5-492f-a9b5-f407e645da84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This process can take a while, depending on the size of the database.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-dk, --drop-keyspace &lt;keyspace=value&gt; --silent-drop</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Drops persisten keyspace to free up the storage space.</p><ul><li data-uuid="3d7146fd-42ce-492c-a3eb-bc8e6c492aaf">Add <strong>--silent-drop </strong>to run the command silently, i.e., without requesting user permission.</li><li data-uuid="65d7679e-8826-4710-825c-96d79e0afb44"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>You need to clear the removed keyspace snapshots using the <strong>nodetool</strong> utility command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8c312e9-91b9-486b-8369-34c5278b1154"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ nodetool clearsnapshot -- <keyspace>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-dr,--delete-resource &lt;resource ID&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given resource(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple resource IDs using space as a separator.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a3b9b41-1672-4a1d-90f7-c850b049f90a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-drb,--delete-branch &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt; includeChildBranch=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given branch(es) and all of its related information. Specify multiple branch ID using a comma as a separator.</p><p><strong>includeChildBranch</strong> has two values: <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong><em>. </em></p><ul><li data-uuid="a6b06a90-4cc7-4e78-9c9a-414d95ad479b">When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <strong>false</strong>, and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, this branch will not be deleted.</li><li data-uuid="99b3d808-998a-4f5d-8c7c-eb75d474d34d">When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <strong>true</strong><em>, </em>the branch including its child branches will be deleted.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d1bcbc-2d3c-48ad-8f9f-00ddb873487f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command does not work for the branches of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its branches.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-drd,--delete-marked-deleted-resources</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes all resources that were marked as deleted.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22febd0d-99ad-4f01-af62-a1f821385f84"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-drr,--delete-revision &lt;resourceID=value revision=&lt;revision1,revision2&gt; includeChildRevision=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given revision(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple revisions using a comma as a separator.</p><p><strong>includeChildRevision</strong> has two values: <strong>true</strong> and <strong>false</strong><em>. </em></p><ul><li data-uuid="8ffec28d-9821-483f-89bc-24372238425f">When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <strong>false</strong><em>, </em>and the revision is not the latest, it will not be deleted.</li><li data-uuid="2adea280-2d9a-4983-9342-cf5fae5a381c">When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <strong>true</strong><em>,</em> the upward revisions and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a8a1990-5cc3-4fae-bcea-8b43d2cdd8d4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command does not work for the revisions of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its revisions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-du,--delete-user &lt;username&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Deletes the given username(s) and all of its related information.</p><ul><li data-uuid="d581801b-e6dd-4cde-af63-b61c285abb1a">To specify multiple usernames, use comma as a separator.</li><li data-uuid="47fd225a-7828-4e0e-87b7-0f5861bb29f2"><p class="auto-cursor-target">If the username consists of two words (for example, <em>dan smith</em>) add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0302fbce-a9ca-4057-9ea5-680c8447cf0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
````html
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td>-rrof, --remove-read-only-flag &lt;-resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt; --categoryID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Removes the read-only flag from a synchronized resource or category in the target server. Specify multiple resources or categories using a comma as a separator, for example:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="502cc4e7-3c14-41c1-a0bf-cdaf58b591d0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e23054-2732-4c6e-85ef-3afcafee884e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.</p><p>The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-lb,--list-branch &lt;resourceID=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Lists all branches of the given resource.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-lc,--list-cluster-ids</p></td><td>Lists previous cluster IDs and the current one.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-sgdb,--set-global-deny-edit-branch &lt;resourceID=&lt;value&gt; branchID=&lt;value&gt; globalDenyEdit=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Sets whether the given branch should be denied to edit by global.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-sgdr,--set-global-deny-edit-resource &lt;resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt; globalDenyEdit=&lt;true/false&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Sets whether the given resources should be denied to edit by global.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f66c2956-ce83-46d5-8347-1cc052f3f753"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-lgdr,--list-global-deny-edit-resource &lt;--resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Lists the status of deny edit by global of the given resource.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lgdb,--list-global-deny-edit-branch &lt;--resourceID=&lt;value1&gt;,&lt;value2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td>Lists the status of deny edit by global for all branches of the given resource.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lcat,--list-categories</p></td><td>Lists all the categories and their IDs.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-li,--list-index</p></td><td>Lists all available data that have indexes.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lr,--list-resources</p></td><td>Lists all available resources.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lrd,--list-marked-deleted-resources</p></td><td>Lists all resources that are marked as deleted.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lrv,--list-revision &lt;resourceID=value branchID=value&gt;</p></td><td>Lists all revisions in the given branch.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-lu,--list-users &lt;--no-info&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Lists all usernames and their information, sorted by name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7236b80d-5f13-4722-89a9-f1395659fa85"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can add <strong>--no-info</strong> to show only the usernames.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-ram,--reset-administrator</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Resets admin password, status, and permissions.</p><ul><li data-uuid="6c68a15f-d157-4434-a590-f75a7284020c">If there is no Administrator account, it will be created.</li><li data-uuid="e17e15fd-2650-427b-852b-d64149d7f4e7">If the Administrator account exists in the database:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="ddd827a9-67b7-4e3e-870c-a92008fedf61"><ul><li>If the account is for an external user, it will be reset to an internal user with a new password. This user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li><li>If the account is an internal user, only a new password will be generated, and the user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li></ul></li></ul><ul class="alternate"><li data-uuid="91858594-35e2-4cf1-a202-ba7d6b2b2a0d">The default roles of the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be restored to make sure that this account can fully operate as the default administrator.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-rc,--recreate-cluster-id</p></td><td><p>Recreates a cluster ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-ri,--rebuild-index &lt;data=value1,value2&gt;</p></td><td><p>Rebuilds all indexes for the given data and uses the <strong>data=*</strong> option to rebuild all indexes of all available data.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-urd,--unmark-deleted-resources &lt;resource ID&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given resource(s) previously marked as deleted. Specify multiple resource ID using space as a separator.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-vb,--validate-blob &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value --readAllWithCQL --createCompareOfThriftAndCQL&gt;</p></td><td>Validates blob information.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-ve,--validate-eobject &lt;resourceID=value1,value2 saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates the eObject information of a specific resource ID and uses the <strong>resourceID=*</strong> option to validate all resources.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-version</p></td><td>Displays the version of Data Manager.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-vi,--verify-index &lt;data=value1,value2&gt;</p></td><td><p>Verifies all indexes for the given data and uses the <strong>data=*</strong> option to verify all indexes of all available data.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-vr,--validate-rbac &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates a user, user group, role, and role assignment information.</td></tr><tr><td><p>-vrc,--validate-resource-commit &lt;savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td>Validates a metamodel, branch, and committed information.</td></tr><tr><td>-col,--cleanup-object-locks</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removes released object locks (that are no longer used) from the database.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f91ddea-a561-4aad-b051-6f88b57e6abf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>How to safely execute this command:</p><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="9f63365c-55b1-44e8-b30a-8f785c1807a5">Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.</li><li data-uuid="83e91b30-306e-4e08-9bf3-31dd7b238e75">After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool repair</strong> which you can find in the <em>Cassandra/bin</em> directory. Once <strong>nodetool repair </strong>is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li><li data-uuid="bff136fd-1d45-4802-8923-ea1aa6e34245">DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool compact</strong>. After running the command in <strong>nodetool compact </strong>terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-rcc,--rebuild-change-count &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e9397cb-2a34-4dc4-9979-ab9aa5e39b0d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40487135-04e4-41b7-8e01-40229da20b94"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.</p><p>Example:</p><ul><li data-uuid="fb44ec7d-305a-4a8a-bc5d-cf0cb1cbe634">datamanager -rcc</li><li data-uuid="8963b321-bc55-455e-b9c9-97e915e7e507">datamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637</li><li data-uuid="7b1dd786-a7e3-4e4e-adc2-2a96c03dbc9b"><span>datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-crb,--clean-resource-blobs &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional <strong>resourceID</strong> argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-cu,--change-username &lt;username=value newUsername=value&gt;</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Changes the given username and generates a new password.<br />                                                                                                             </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e85862fb-7945-425b-a8d6-70450e7e40d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For usernames containing a space, put double quotes, &quot;, around the username. Example:</p><p>datamanager -cu username=&quot;John Old&quot; newUsername=&quot;Andrew New&quot;</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-ll,--list-ldap</p></td><td><p>Lists all LDAP realm information.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-rlu, --remap-ldap-users</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d17ead5-c338-4444-8707-2824840f8d12"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>datamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>-vlu,--validate-ldap-users &lt;saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-udb,--unmark-deleted-branches &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />The <strong>delete</strong> command to delete resources and users works as follows.</p><h4>Deleting selected resource </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abe1732a-965d-4141-8318-eb14b852a461"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> <resource ID> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of the resource. </li><li>Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories). </li><li>Branch information. </li><li>Commit information. </li><li>Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed). </li><li>Locking information.</li></ul><h4>Deleting selected user </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d89fa469-6680-4099-8b73-5b9199426522"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-du,--delete-user <username>,<username> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:</span></p><ul><li>Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).</li><li>Session (the existing user session will be removed).</li><li>Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).</li><li>User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdb4f757-4a39-4a2e-b0bc-bd228aefce15"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Notes on deleting usernames</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">For usernames consisting of two words (such as <em>dan smith</em>), add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username, as shown:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac17643d-503e-4234-bc3c-db295661ad2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the modeling tool's history panels, the word <em>deleted</em> will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Deleting selected branch </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82a7bd5f-fc36-4868-bcb6-59a08022d27f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of each revision in the branch</li><li>Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch</li><li>Commit information of each revision in the branch</li><li>Locking information of each object locked in the branch</li><li>Branch information of the branch</li><li>If the branch has child branches, depending on the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong><strong> </strong>value, the branch will be deleted or not: <em> </em><br /><ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <em>False </em>and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch </strong>value is set to <em>True, </em>then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Deleting revisions </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7deaa30e-49cc-4711-9f3b-1d2227ceb92f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of the revision</li><li>Object information created/modified at the revision</li><li>Commit information of the revision</li><li>Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to</li><li>Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch</li><li>If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong><strong> </strong>value, the revision will be deleted or not: <ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <em>False, </em>and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision </strong>value is set to <em>T</em><em>rue,</em> then<em> </em>revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925636 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00089: Data markings support

- page_id: `191925636`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925636/Data+markings+support
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:59:44.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Other features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings and Classification Plugin' space='DMP2024xR2'] to ensure consistency between documents and related Magic Collaboration Studio projects.The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classificationor sensitivity. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.

For information on how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Magic Collaboration Studio, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin' space='MCS2024xR2'].

The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.

The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.

Data markings are supported in tables as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings displayed in a table banner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);">Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ac:link> to ensure consistency between documents and related Magic Collaboration Studio projects. </span>The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classification<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">or sensitivity</span>. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81888686-7ea9-4d43-8e16-47dacf2e4a5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information on <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Magic Collaboration Studio, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_element_names.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Data markings support" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><p><br /></p><p>The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Data markings support" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.</h6><p>Data markings are supported in tables as well.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_tables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Data markings support" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings displayed in a table banner.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925467 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00090: Deleting a comment

- page_id: `191925467`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925467/Deleting+a+comment
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:50.547+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments > Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.

To delete a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening the comments panel' space='MCS2024xR2'].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the bottom of the comment and select Delete .

#### TIP: Useful to know

Useful to know

- Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.
- You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening the comments panel" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Cameo Collaborator comments panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Deleting a comment" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Deleting a comment" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="541e0626-073b-4cd7-8106-6c818482f294"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful to know</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.</li><li>You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924251 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00092: Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center

- page_id: `191924251`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924251/Deploying+Cassandra+Multi-Data+Center
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:58:12.099+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Deployment Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio Installation > Advanced Magic Collaboration Studio installation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**4**

Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh2 and newer versions support Apache Cassandra multiple data centers (Multi-DC) capability. Multi-DC Cassandra ensures continuous availability of data to Magic Collaboration Studio users. There are two main scenarios for using multiple data centers: live backup/disaster recovery and geographical location. The deployment configuration of a live backup data center is presented here as an example.

Please note that Multi-DC deployment requires in-depth knowledge of Cassandra architecture and administration. Incorrect deployment or operation can result in total data loss. Technical support is not available, as this is considered a custom deployment strategy.

##### **Scenarios for deploying a Multi-DC Cassandra cluster**

###### **Live backup/disaster recovery**

Multiple data centers can be deployed at different physical locations as a disaster recovery solution. In this situation, one Cassandra data center would be the primary access point, while an off-site secondary data center would continuously receive the latest database updates. If the primary data center goes down, data can be restored from the secondary data center by bringing new Cassandra nodes online at the primary data center. Magic Collaboration Studio can also be reconfigured to connect to the secondary data center as a temporary solution.

In this scenario, there is no degradation in performance. Read and write operations are performed on the primary data center first, then eventually synced to the second data center as a background operation.

###### ******Geographical location**

For organizations with users spread across multiple geographical locations, it may be desirable to optimize data access by location. Each region can have a dedicated data center. All of the data centers would form one Cassandra cluster, and all of them would have access to the latest data.

In this scenario, performance would mainly be affected by the database write operations. Write operations would be applied to all data centers. Read operations would remain local and thus not affect performance.

##### **Deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for Magic Collaboration Studio**

This is the recommended configuration procedure for deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for Magic Collaboration Studio.

To configure Multi-DC Cassandra cluster

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center' space='MCS2024xR2']
2. Start Cassandra cluster and verify all data centers are online.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center' space='MCS2024xR2']
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center' space='MCS2024xR2']
5. For an existing database, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center' space='MCS2024xR2'].
6. Start Magic Collaboration Studio services and verify operation.

**Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers**

Our example deployment is a single Cassandra cluster with two data centers located in separate geographical locations. Each Cassandra data center has three nodes. When performing the initial installation and configuration of Cassandra nodes, make sure the version of Cassandra is consistent across all nodes. Also, make sure to synchronize clocks across all nodes.

Start by updating the *cassandra.yaml* configuration file.

Set a unique cluster name for all nodes. This deployment is a single cluster spanning multiple data centers. The cluster name should be the same across all nodes.

```text

```

Set the seed provider for all data centers. Assign at least two seed nodes for each data center. Specify the address for all seeds. Alternatively, use a seed provider service to keep track of seed node addresses.

```text

```

Then, change the endpoint snitch implementation on each node. The default snitch is *SimpleSnitch*, which does not support Multi-DC. Cassandra uses snitch to gather network topology information. In this case, we are using *GossipingPropertyFileSnitch* for Multi-DC setup. Cassandra will obtain data center information from the *cassandra-rackdc.properties* file when *GossipingPropertyFileSnitch* is set.

```text

```

Refer to Cassandra documentation if you are updating snitch for an exiting database: [https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html)

Edit the *cassandra-rackdc.properties* file for each node. All nodes in a data center should have the same “dc” name.

For the primary data center:

```text

```

For the backup data center:

```text

```

Start each Cassandra node. Port 7000 is the default inter-node communication (encrypted and unencrypted) port for a Cassandra cluster. Make sure port 7000 is open for communication between data centers. Please note that the initial connection between data centers can take some time. Run *nodetool status* to confirm that all data centers have connected to the cluster.

For an existing Cassandra cluster, run *nodetool repair* and *nodetool cleanup* after the above configurations have been made.

###### **Configuring Magic Collaboration Studio for Multi-DC**

Once all Cassandra nodes are online and data center connections are confirmed, configure the Magic Collaboration Studio *application.conf* file for Multi-DC.

In the *esi.persistence.cassandra.keyspace* section, we have added the following parameters for our example. With 3 nodes in each data center, the replication factor is set to 3. The consistency level is set to “FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL" for a disaster recovery scenario. Please refer to the table below for consistency setting options. If deploying an entirely new database, the “twc” keyspace will be created with the replication strategy and factors specified here. For an existing database, you will have to manually edit the keyspace replication parameters (see Cassandra Keyspace Configuration below).

```text

```

- When *is-multi-dc* is set to *true*, the *replication-factor* parameter is ignored.
- The *dc-replication-factors* configuration will only be applied when creating a new “twc” keyspace. If there is an existing “twc” keyspace in Cassandra, Magic Collaboration Studio will use the existing replication factor set in the database. Use *ALTER KEYSPACE* to change the replication factor.

| Keyspace Consistency Setting | Write Consistency Level | Read Consistency Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_GLOBAL | ALL | ONE |
| BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_LOCAL | ALL | LOCAL_ONE |
| BEST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL | EACH_QUORUM | EACH_QUORUM |
| FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL | EACH_QUORUM | LOCAL_QUORUM |
| BEST_RELIABILITY_LOCAL | LOCAL_QUORUM | LOCAL_QUORUM |

In the *esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic* section, set the data center name and contact points to local values. The local data center will be used as the primary database for client requests.

```text

```

Use the seed nodes from the local data center specified in *cassandra.yaml*

```text

```

###### **Configuring Cassandra keyspace**

If you are deploying Multi-DC with an existing Cassandra database, you will need to manually reconfigure the replication parameters for the “twc” keyspace.

Use *cqlsh*, the Cassandra command-line interface, to modify “twc” keyspace replication parameters.

```bash

```

Run *nodetool repair* after keyspace modification to update all nodes.

To verify replication in *cqlsh*:

```bash

```

These steps can also be used to create the “twc” keyspace. Note that manual keyspace creation is not needed, as Magic Collaboration Studio will create the keyspace during initial startup.

```bash

```

##### **Multi-DC Cassandra Best Practices**

- The Cassandra version must be the same across all nodes and data centers
- At least two seed nodes should be assigned for each data center
- Clocks should be synchronized for all node

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/></ac:link></span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For an existing database</span>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring Cassandra keyspace"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update the “twc” keyspace for Multi-DC setup]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Start Magic Collaboration Studio services and verify operation.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Our example deployment is a single Cassandra cluster with two data centers located in separate geographical locations. This deployment is a single cluster spanning multiple data centers. The cluster name should be the same across all nodes.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74e767c7-e04a-4920-9421-eb5ba65a2644"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cluster_name: 'MDC Cluster']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Set the seed provider for all data centers. Alternatively, use a seed provider service to keep track of seed node addresses.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f21423e-fe82-4b53-b0a4-2354e7fe0f05"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[seed_provider:
````html
    - class_name: org.apache.cassandra.locator.SimpleSeedProvider
      parameters:
          - seeds: "10.0.0.1, 10.0.0.2, 10.0.1.1, 10.0.1.2"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Then, change the endpoint snitch implementation on each node. The default snitch is <em>SimpleSnitch</em>, which does not support Multi-DC. Cassandra uses snitch to gather network topology information. In this case, we are using <em>GossipingPropertyFileSnitch</em> for Multi-DC setup. Cassandra will obtain data center information from the <em>cassandra-rackdc.properties</em> file when <em>GossipingPropertyFileSnitch</em> is set. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc269aa0-2fed-430b-a612-84d77f41ce2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[endpoint_snitch: GossipingPropertyFileSnitch]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Refer to Cassandra documentation if you are updating snitch for an exiting database: <a href="https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html">https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html</a></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Edit the <em>cassandra-rackdc.properties</em> file for each node. All nodes in a data center should have the same “dc” name.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For the primary data center:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f9ec96e-2868-4b44-99ed-f47bc4134781"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[dc=datacenter1
rack=rack1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For the backup data center:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec0d8678-6728-4ff6-99b5-3e3aa70a6088"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[dc=datacenter2
rack=rack1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Start each Cassandra node. Port 7000 is the default inter-node communication (encrypted and unencrypted) port for a Cassandra cluster. Make sure port 7000 is open for communication between data centers. Please note that the initial connection between data centers can take some time. Run <em>nodetool status</em> to confirm that all data centers have connected to the cluster.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For an existing Cassandra cluster, run <em>nodetool repair</em> and <em>nodetool cleanup</em> after the above configurations have been made.</span></p><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Configuring Magic Collaboration Studio for Multi-DC</strong></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Once all Cassandra nodes are online and data center connections are confirmed, configure the Magic Collaboration Studio <em>application.conf</em> file for Multi-DC. </span></p><p><span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <em>esi.persistence.cassandra.keyspace</em> section, we have added the following parameters for our example. With 3 nodes in each data center, the replication factor is set to 3. The consistency level is set to “FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL&quot; for a disaster recovery scenario. Please refer to the table below for consistency setting options. If deploying an entirely new database, the “twc” keyspace will be created with the replication strategy and factors specified here. For an existing database, you will have to manually edit the keyspace replication parameters (see Cassandra Keyspace Configuration below).</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c8b0f69-b6b7-4080-b030-dccd9259860e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[is-multi-dc = true
strategy-class = "NetworkTopologyStrategy"
consistency = "FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL"
dc-replication-factors {
     datacenter1=3
     datacenter2=3
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">When <em>is-multi-dc</em> is set to <em>true</em>, the <em>replication-factor</em> parameter is ignored. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The <em>dc-replication-factors</em> configuration will only be applied when creating a new “twc” keyspace. If there is an existing “twc” keyspace in Cassandra, Magic Collaboration Studio will use the existing replication factor set in the database. Use <em>ALTER KEYSPACE</em> to change the replication factor.</span></li></ul><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Keyspace Consistency Setting</strong></span></p></th><th><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Write Consistency Level</strong></span></p></th><th><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Read Consistency Level</strong></span></p></th></tr><tr class=""><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_GLOBAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ALL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ONE</span></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_LOCAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ALL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">LOCAL_ONE</span></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">BEST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">EACH_QUORUM</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">EACH_QUORUM</span></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">EACH_QUORUM</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">LOCAL_QUORUM</span></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">BEST_RELIABILITY_LOCAL</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">LOCAL_QUORUM</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">LOCAL_QUORUM</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <em>esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic</em> section, set the data center name and contact points to local values. The local data center will be used as the primary database for client requests.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5434990-4ea4-4c49-91c3-f17b93684643"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[load-balancing-policy.local-datacenter = datacenter1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Use the seed nodes from the local data center specified in <em>cassandra.yaml</em></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="882ea537-923b-4bdf-86fd-cb7e8f683dee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[contact-points = ["10.0.0.1:9042","10.0.0.2:9042"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Configuring Cassandra keyspace</strong></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If you are deploying Multi-DC with an existing Cassandra database, you will need to manually reconfigure the replication parameters for the “twc” keyspace.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Use <em>cqlsh</em>, the Cassandra command-line interface, to modify “twc” keyspace replication parameters.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e609f3d-bc25-49be-afb6-538060dec874"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ALTER KEYSPACE twc WITH replication = {'class': 'NetworkTopologyStrategy', 'datacenter1' : 3, 'datacenter2' : 3};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Run <em>nodetool repair</em> after keyspace modification to update all nodes.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To verify replication in <em>cqlsh</em>:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd6ba8d6-7cf0-4045-a8cd-b57634c6bb58"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[select * from system_schema.keyspaces;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">These steps can also be used to create the “twc” keyspace. Note that manual keyspace creation is not needed, as Magic Collaboration Studio will create the keyspace during initial startup.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c7f50b5-4d1c-4b90-90f0-d972fed3e78a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[CREATE KEYSPACE twc WITH replication = {'class': 'NetworkTopologyStrategy', 'datacenter1' : 3, 'datacenter2' : 3}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Multi-DC Cassandra Best Practices</strong></span></h3><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Cassandra version must be the same across all nodes and data centers</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">At least two seed nodes should be assigned for each data center</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Clocks should be synchronized for all node</span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924296 space=MCS2024xR2 version=4 -->
