<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925257 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: Document templates

- page_id: `191925257`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925257/Document+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-29T15:25:22.799+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents
- labels: ['using-predefined-templates']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:

- **Predefined templates**, which are stored the*modelLibraries*folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e. g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.
- **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with custom document templates' space='MCS2024xR2']**, which are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user defined location. These templates appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog, if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a custom template' space='MCS2024xR2']

The following table briefly describes all predefined templates and lists No Magic's modeling tools where each of them is available.

| Template name | Short description | Available in... |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BPMN | Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:ProcessesData ObjectsOrganization StructuresInformation Systems | MagicDraw + Cameo Business Modeler PluginCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Entire Model | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model. | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Diagrams | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model. | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Requirements Document | Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal. | MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler PluginMagicDraw + SysML PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Requirements | Apply this template to publish a project as the Requirements portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureTest CasesTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler PluginMagicDraw + SysML PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Software Engineering | Apply this template to publish a project as the Software Engineering portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureImplementationTest CasesArchitecture TraceabilityTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| SysML | Apply this template to publish a project as the SysML portal, which provides the following views of the model:RequirementsStructureInterfacesConstraintsBehaviorDiagrams | MagicDraw + SysML PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| UAF | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Security ViewResources View - ResourcesSystems View - ResourcesPhysical Resources View - ResourcesResources View - Resource ExchangesSystems View - Resource ExchangesPhysical Resource View - Resource ExchangesResources View - FunctionsSystems View - FunctionsPhysical Resources View - FunctionsService Oriented View - Service InterfacesServices View - Service InterfacesService Oriented View - Service SpecificationsServices View - Service SpecificationsService Oriented View - Service FunctionsServices View - Service FunctionsProgramme View - ProjectsProject View - ProjectsAcquisition View - Projects50%Personnel View - Organisational RelationshipsOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsLogical View - Organisational RelationshipsLogical View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Operational ExchangesLogical View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational PerformersLogical View - Operational PerformersStrategic View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - CapabilitiesCapability View - CapabilitiesStrategic View - CapabilitiesDiagramsData and InformationDictionaryTechnical Standards | MagicDraw + UAF Plugin |
| UPDM | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Capability View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - CapabilitiesOperational View - NodesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsSystems View - ResourcesSystems View - FunctionsSystems View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service Accesses50%Services View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service InterfacesServices View - InterfacesServices View - Service FunctionsProject View - ProjectsStandardsDictionaryDataDiagrams | MagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Enterprise Architecture |

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space='MCS2024xR2']

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

There are two types of templates you can use:</p><ul><li><p><strong>Predefined templates</strong>, which are stored <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">the </span><em>modelLibraries</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and</span> appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e. g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.</p></li><li><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with custom document templates" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom templates]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>, which are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user defined location. These templates appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog, if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. <ac:link><ri:page template.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The following table briefly describes all predefined templates and lists No Magic's modeling tools where each of them is following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Processes</li><li>Data Objects</li><li>Organization Structures</li><li>Information Systems</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>MagicDraw + Cameo Business <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925368 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00099: Document view modes

- page_id: `191925368`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925368/Document+view+modes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-04-16T09:57:32.227+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can view a Cameo Collaborator document in several different view modes:

- Model view : In this view mode, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'] of a document portal shows only the content of the item selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space='MCS2024xR2'] . In addition, t he Model view shows model information and is the default mode of a Cameo Collaborator document.
- Document view : This mode hides model details and allows you to browse a Cameo Collaborator document continuously. You can scroll through the entire content of the selected model view, or select an item in the navigation pane to load the related content.
- **Wiki view**: Similar to the Document view, The Wiki viewhides model details while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view.

To change the view mode of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the model view whose view mode you want to change.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. In the open menu, click **Active view** and select the desired view mode. 
 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

After changing the document view mode, the selected model view is reloaded and displayed in a new mode.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure of the document portal' space='MCS2024xR2']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space='MCS2024xR2']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925602 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00100: Downloading and uploading PDF/HTML templates

- page_id: `191925602`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925602/Downloading+and+uploading+PDF+HTML+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-12T16:14:04.747+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Customizing PDF/HTML templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

PDF/HTML templates for exported documents are document-specific. You can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document, make any desired changes, and then upload the modified template back to the document. In addition, a single document can hold multiple PDF/HTML templates, enabling you to generate different outputs from the same document.

To download the default PDF/HTML template

1. Open the document whose PDF/HTML template you want to download.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select About .
3. On the left side of the open screen, select PDF/HTML templates .
4. In the Default template card, click the Download button. [ATTACHMENT filename='downloading_default_pdf_html_template.png']

The default PDF/HTML template for the open document is downloaded to your machine and you can customize it to meet your specific needs.

To upload a custom PDF/HTML template

1. Open the document to which you want to upload a custom PDF/HTML template.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select About .
3. On the left side of the open screen, select PDF/HTML templates .
4. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_icon.png'] .
5. In the Template title box of the open dialog, type the template name.
6. Drag the template file to the dialog or click the file icon to find the file on your machine. [ATTACHMENT filename='uploading_pdf_html_template.png']
7. When the template file is uploaded, click the Save button.

The uploaded template is displayed in the **PDF/HTML templates** screen.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=301368798 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00101: Downloading permissions report

- page_id: `301368798`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/301368798/Downloading+permissions+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-23T14:14:22.277+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can download a user's permission report and view the permissions they were assigned in Excel format.

To download the permissions report, you must have the following permissions: **List All Resources**,******Manage Security Roles**, and**Manage User Permissions**.

To download the permissions report

Do one of the following:

- In the Users application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the user and select Generate permissions report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- In the My account application, c lick [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom left corner to download the report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can download a user's permission report and view the permissions they were assigned in Excel format.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89f16d31-5ebf-41e2-8c09-1227705c5513"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To download the permissions report, you must have the following permissions: <strong><span>List All Resources</span></strong><span>,</span><strong><span> </span></strong><span><strong>Manage Security Roles</strong>, and </span><span><strong>Manage User Permissions</strong>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To download the permissions report</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2a8b23f9-20f1-4185-a36e-e59c72590ead">In the <strong>Users </strong>application, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the user and select <strong>Generate permissions report.<br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generate_permissions_report.jpg" /></ac:image></strong></li><li data-uuid="7e5f1551-b783-43e2-a7cc-e092087b6cbb">In the <strong>My account </strong>application, c<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Download icon.png" /></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">at the bottom left corner to download the report.<br /></span></li></ul><p><br /><ac:image ac:width="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="my_account_app_download.jpg" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925462 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00102: Editing a comment

- page_id: `191925462`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925462/Editing+a+comment
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:50.458+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments > Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

125789331

125789331

125789330

**On this page**

4

125789331

You can edit comments in the Cameo Collaborator comments panel as described below.

To edit a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to edit.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening the comments panel' space='MCS2024xR2'].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to edit.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit**. The **Edit comment** pane opens.
6. In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.
7. If needed, click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
8. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.

##### Discarding comments

If you do not want to save comment changes after starting to edit the comment, you can cancel the editing process.

To discard a comment

- After starting to edit a comment, click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_comment.png'] on the top left corner of the Edit comment pane to cancel the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

actions.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing a comment" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Edit</strong>. The <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane opens.</p></li><li>In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.</li><li>If needed, click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to change the comment priority.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing a comment" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.</li></ol><h3><br />Discarding comments</h3><p>If you do not want to save comment changes after starting to edit the comment, you can cancel the editing process.</p><p><br /></p><p>To discard a comment</p><hr /><ul><li>After starting to edit a comment, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing a comment" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the top left corner of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to cancel the changes.</li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925035 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00103: Editing an LDAP server configuration properties

- page_id: `191925035`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925035/Editing+an+LDAP+server+configuration+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:58:20.495+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the configuration properties of an LDAP server connected to the Magic Collaboration Studio system. The system will update the configuration properties with the new values automatically.

To edit the configuration properties of an LDAP server do one of the following

- On the **LDAP management** page, select card with LDAP server which you want to edit****and click **Edit**button.
  - Change wanted configuration parameters.
  - Click Save to save changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the configuration properties of an LDAP server connected to the Magic Collaboration Studio system. The system will update the configuration properties with the new values automatically.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit the configuration properties of an LDAP server do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>On the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">LDAP management</strong> page, select card with LDAP server which you want to edit<strong> </strong>and click <strong>Edit </strong>button. </p><ul><li>Change wanted configuration parameters.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong> to save changes. </li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925498 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00104: Editing and contextualizing

- page_id: `191925498`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925498/Editing+and+contextualizing
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:50.734+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Documents published from synchronized projects

1545184041

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Documents published from synchronized projects

1545184042

INLINE

1545184039

INLINE

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Documents published from synchronized projects

1545184041

INLINE

You can edit certain model elements right in a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document. This is very beneficial for the whole product team, because engineers and even stakeholders can make changes to a model without having to use a modeling tool. In addition, the model editing capability speeds up the collaboration on a model. It allows you to instantly correct property values in a Cameo Collaborator document and skip the commenting process.

The sections below explain several main workflows where the model editing feature can be used.

#### NOTE: Documents published from synchronized projects

Documents published from synchronized projects

If a Cameo Collaborator document is published from a synchronized project on the target Magic Collaboration Studio server, the commenting and model editing capabilities in the document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space='MCS2024xR2'].

##### Model editing to provide feedback

If you need to review a Cameo Collaborator document and provide feedback on the model, follow the workflow below.

To provide feedback on a model

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document you want to review.
2. Review the document, including its structure, element characteristics, and diagrams.
3. Do one or several of the following actions:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator' space='MCS2024xR2'] for items that need to be addressed.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space='MCS2024xR2'] if you know what they should be.
4. Inform the author that you have finished reviewing the document so that he/she could react to your feedback.

##### Model editing to react to feedback

When a reviewer provides feedback on a Cameo Collaborator document, address the comments right in the document without having to use a modeling tool.

To react to feedback

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document that has comments you need to address.
2. In the app bar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space='MCS2024xR2'] and read the comments.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space='MCS2024xR2'] in the document to address the reported issues.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Replying to comments' space='MCS2024xR2'] to inform the reviewer that required changes have been made or ask for additional information.
5. Inform the reviewer that you have finished updating the model and ask for another round of review.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00105: Editing custom roles

- page_id: `191924871`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924871/Editing+custom+roles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:36:13.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Roles application > Managing roles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editing roles

102943735

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editing roles

102943739

102943734

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editing roles

102943738

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating roles' space='MCS2024xR2'], you can modify it by changing its name, description, and permissions as described in the following sections.

#### NOTE: Editing roles

Editing roles

- You can edit only custom (user-created) roles.
- You must have the Security Manager role to edit custom roles.

##### Editing role details

In the Roles application, you can edit the name and description of a custom role as described below.

To edit role details

1. Do one of the following actions:
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Edit button in the role details card. [ATTACHMENT filename='editing_role_details.png']
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='actions.png'] next to a role name and select Edit role details .
2. When the Edit role details pane opens, change role name and or description and click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_role.png'] .

##### Changing role permissions

You can use the Roles application to add or remove [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='MCS2024xR2']of a custom role. Note that a role must have at least one permission.

To add or remove permissions of a role

1. Do one of the following actions:
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the role details card.
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='actions.png'] next to a role name and select Change permissions .
2. When the Change permissions pane opens, select the permissions you want to assign to the role (as shown below) and/or clear the check-boxes next to the permissions you want to remove. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_permissions_pane.png']
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_role.png'] to save the changes.

1976618753

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning users to resources' space='MCS2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925501 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00106: Editing element values

- page_id: `191925501`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925501/Editing+element+values
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:50.897+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing and contextualizing
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

If you need to provide feedback on a model or address reviewer's comments, edit certain element values right in the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

- In order to edit element values in a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing documents' space='MCS2024xR2'] .
- Before starting editing element values, read about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model editing behavior and constraints' space='MCS2024xR2'] to understand how this feature works.

To edit element values in a Cameo Collaborator document

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
4. In the **Edit** pane (displayed below), change the item value and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example shows how you can edit requirement text.

Once you finish the steps described above, the modified item value is saved both in the Cameo Collaborator document and Magic Collaboration Studio project. See the figures below for more examples of editing different types of values.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing a Boolean type of value.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing an Enumeration type of value.

#### TIP: Deleting property values

Deleting property values

To delete the value of an editable property:

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to delete. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. When the **Edit** pane opens, click next to the value you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

##### Reordering the values of multi-value properties

You can use the model editing feature to reorder the values of multi-value properties as described below.

To reorder the values of multi-value properties

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space='MCS2024xR2'].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024xR2'].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
4. When the **Edit** pane opens, drag a value to the desired position as displayed below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924669 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00107: Enabling and disabling users

- page_id: `191924669`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924669/Enabling+and+disabling+users
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:59:41.221+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Managing users > Managing users details
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to disable login for user. To enable or disable user login you must have **User manager** role.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Easily distinguish enabled user from disabled.

To enable/disable user login, do one of the following

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the user and from menu select to Disable user/Enable user .
- Go to User pane and select to edit user details,
  - Change sliders position next to Enabled (slide left to disable, slide right to enable user`s login).
- Go to User pane and click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on Account card, Actions menu will open.
  - From menu select to Disable user/Enable user.

If you disable a user's access while he or she is online or logged in, Teamwork Cloud Admin will not log them out immediately. Changes to access permission will take effect immediately once the user logs out.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to disable login for user. To enable or disable user login you must have <strong>User manager</strong> role. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabled_disabled_users.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling users" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Easily distinguish enabled user from disabled.</h6><p>To enable/disable user login, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling users" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </span> next to the user and from menu select to <strong>Disable user/Enable user</strong>.</li><li>Go to <strong>User</strong> pane and select to edit user details,<ul><li> Change sliders position next to <strong>Enabled </strong>(slide left to disable, slide right to enable user`s login). </li></ul></li><li>Go to <strong>User</strong> pane and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling and disabling users" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on <strong>Account </strong>card, <strong>Actions</strong> menu will open. <br /><ul><li>From menu select to <strong>Disable user/Enable user. </strong></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f37d2c2-3674-43dd-9dd0-560cee5f9f94"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you disable a user's access while he or she is online or logged in, Teamwork Cloud Admin will not log them out immediately. Changes to access permission will take effect immediately once the user logs out.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=198377665 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00108: Enabling conditional user groups

- page_id: `198377665`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/198377665/Enabling+conditional+user+groups
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-06T11:10:38.210+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing server settings
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using conditional user groups allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values. When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location. For instructions on how to create conditional user groups, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Conditional user groups' space='MCS2024xR2'].

To allow users to create conditional user groups, first, you have to enable this feature in the Settings application as described below.

To enable conditional user groups

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Server settings** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the User management card, turn on the **Conditional user groups** option.

Once conditional user groups are enabled, you can set a user group as conditional and determine user access rights dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling conditional user groups.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Using conditional user groups allows you to validate/invalidate user group membership based on SAML attribute values. When users are added to a conditional user group, they receive the group permissions only if the group condition is met, for example, if they log in from the right location. For instructions on how to create conditional user groups, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" ri:content-title="Conditional user groups" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To allow users to create conditional user groups, first, you have to enable this feature in the Settings application as described below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To enable conditional user groups</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Server settings</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the User management card, turn on the <strong>Conditional user groups</strong> option.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Once conditional user groups are enabled, you can set a user group as conditional and determine <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">user access rights dynamically by SAML IDPs on each login operation by taking network and user location (and possibly other criteria) into account.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabling_conditional_user_groups.png" /></ac:image></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Enabling conditional user groups.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924933 space=MCS2024xR2 version=4 -->
## PAGE 00109: Enabling external user creation

- page_id: `191924933`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924933/Enabling+external+user+creation
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-03-13T14:18:26.579+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing server settings
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **User management**section of **Server settings**has the options to manage the external user creation process.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing external user creation options.

- If you enable the Create external user on successful authentication option, an external user will be created upon successful authentication. This means that external users will be able to log in to Teamwork Cloud Admin even if no user existed before. An external user will be created upon such login. If you disable this option, no external users will be created if the user tries to log in.

After an external user is created, it takes some time for it to show up inthe **User** application list. This delay is caused by the properties caching.

- Once you enable external user creation, you can also enable the Default user permissions template . With this option enabled, external users created upon successful authentication are added to the Default Template user group and given its permissions and assignments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>User management </strong>section of <strong>Server settings </strong>has the options to manage the external user creation process.</p><p style="text-align: center;"> <ac:image ac:height="220"><ri:attachment ri:filename="user_management.jpg" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Managing external user creation options.</h6><ul><li data-uuid="212ce133-93ba-4993-a3f4-1211a7c98948">If you enable the<strong> Create external user on successful authentication </strong>option, an external user will be created upon successful authentication. This means that external users will be able to log in to Teamwork Cloud Admin even if no user existed before. An external user will be created upon such login.<br />If you disable this option, no external users will be created if the user tries to log in.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78a82261-7405-43f9-9dfb-b128f1cf1369"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After an external user is created, <span>it takes some time for it to show up in </span>the <strong>User</strong> application list. This delay is caused by the <span>properties</span> caching.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="7754b76b-415e-4293-81c7-cd1b595b37b1">Once you enable external user creation, you can also enable the <strong>Default user permissions template</strong>. With this option enabled, external users created upon successful authentication are added to the Default Template user group and given its permissions and assignments.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924918 space=MCS2024xR2 version=7 -->
## PAGE 00110: Enabling secure connection between client and server

- page_id: `191924918`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924918/Enabling+secure+connection+between+client+and+server
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-05-15T15:12:02.224+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing server settings
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

By default, communication between the Magic Collaboration Studio server and client is unencrypted. This page provides information on how to encrypt communication using TLS over TCP.

##### Enabling TLS encryption on server side

To enable a secure connection to Magic Collaboration Studio, you must enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's **Server Settings** page. This page also allows you to disable the option if you do not need to use a secure connection (see the following figure). You can always enable it whenever necessary. The most straightforward way to set up encryption is to use the keystore generated for Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. Locate and download the server Java keystore (default: *<install_root>/****TeamworkCloud/configuration/keystore.p12*). Make a note of the password used for the keystore. If there is a corresponding public certificate (e.g. *teamworkcloud.crt*) on the server, download it as well. If there is no public certificate, use the following command to extract the public certificate from the Java keystore:

```bash
-keystore  -file ]]>
```

If you need to generate a new keystore, see [Regenerating self-signed keystore](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/mcs2024xr2/Managing+SSL+certificate#ManagingSSLcertificate-Regeneratingself-signedkeystore).

The unencrypted port (default: 3579) remains active even after encryption is enabled. To prevent unencrypted connections, block the port at the firewall level.

To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud Admin

1. Go to the Settings app.
2. Move the slider to the right to enable the TLS protocol.
3. Enter a custom port for encrypted communication, or keep default value of 10002.
4. Upload the Java keystore file, and type the password.
5. Click Save .

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud.

##### Setting up client-side TLS

To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol on the client-side, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Server Certificates' space='MD2024xR2'] tool and provide the public certificate.

The default port for a secure connection is 10002. If you are using another port for a secure connection instead of the default, append the port number to the server name in the **Login** dialog:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The unecrypted port (3579) remains active even after TLS is enabled. Teamwork Cloud will continue to accept communication through both encrypted and unencrypted ports. Use firewall to disable port 3579 and block unencrypted communication.

#### EXPAND: Generating a new key pair manually

Generating a new key pair manually

To enable a secure connection between the client and the server, you need to generate two keystore files, each in their own keystores:

- keystore.jks (contains key and certificate) – server-side – upload it using Teamwork Cloud Admin console.
- cert.jks (contains public certificate) – client-side – add it using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Server Certificates' space='MD2024xR2'] option group in Environment Options.

1. Go to your Java directory and open the folder named bin .
2. Enter keytool -genkey -alias MyDomain -keyalg RSA -keystore keystore.jks -keysize 2048 and press Enter . You can give any name to*MyDomain* and **keystore.jks**.
3. Enter a password for your keystore and answer the questions asked. A certificate with a key is generated and placed into the keystore.
4. To extract a certificate without a key, type keytool -export -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore keystore.jks and press Enter .
5. To place a certificate into another keystore, e.g. cert.jks , type keytool -import -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore cert.jks and press Enter . You can give any name to **cert.jks**. However, by default, the tool searches for this exact name. Two keystore files are generated: one for the server ( keystore.jks ) and one for the client ( cert.jks ). You can now proceed to enable a secure connection between the client (modeling tool) and the server (Teamwork Cloud).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d25ed725-448f-48fd-991b-34be28cc466d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>By default, communication between the <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Magic Collaboration Studio</span> server and client is unencrypted. This page provides information on how to encrypt communication using TLS over TCP.</p><h3>Enabling TLS encryption on server side</h3><p>To enable a secure connection to <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Magic Collaboration Studio</span>, you must enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's <strong>Server Settings</strong> page. This page also allows you to disable the option if you do not need to use a secure connection (see the following figure). You can always enable it whenever necessary. The most straightforward way to set up encryption is to use the keystore generated for Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. <span>Locate and download the server Java keystore (default: <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/</em></span><em> </em><span><em>TeamworkCloud/configuration/keystore.p12</em>). Make a note of the password used for the keystore. If there is a corresponding public certificate (e.g. <em>teamworkcloud.crt</em>) on the server, download it as well. If there is no public certificate, use the following command to extract the public certificate from the Java keystore:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec4149b3-edfc-4638-9615-c21e2bb76fa4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[keytool -export -alias <teamworkcloud> -keystore <keystore.p12> -file <teamworkcloud.crt>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81d2bf01-d7ee-414c-b837-b008ca6ae249"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to generate a new keystore, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/mcs2024xr2/Managing+SSL+certificate#ManagingSSLcertificate-Regeneratingself-signedkeystore">Regenerating self-signed keystore</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16cd75e5-08bb-4b6c-895d-c0558e82a18d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The unencrypted port (default: 3579) remains active even after encryption is enabled. To prevent unencrypted connections, block the port at the firewall level.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span> </span>To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud Admin</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <strong>Settings</strong> app.</li><li>Move the slider to the right to enable the TLS protocol. </li><li>Enter a custom port for encrypted communication, or keep default value of 10002.</li><li>Upload the <strong>Java <span>keystore </span></strong>file, and type the password.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><h3 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP_Secure_Connection.jpg" /></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Enabling TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud</span><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">.</span></h6><h3>Setting up client-side TLS</h3><p>To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol on the client-side, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Server Certificates" /></ac:link> tool and provide the public certificate.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc7b6091-11cb-4b03-926c-04d2152afeeb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default port for a secure connection is 10002. If you are using another port for a secure connection instead of the default, append the port number to the server name in the <strong>Login</strong> dialog:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="login_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed6bf311-e684-4c82-a57f-7b7151f64604"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The unecrypted port (3579) remains active even after TLS is enabled. Teamwork Cloud will continue to accept communication through both encrypted and unencrypted ports. Use firewall to disable port 3579 and block unencrypted communication.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f799e476-60ca-42f0-92a9-e767a686d4cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Generating a new key pair manually</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span>To enable a secure connection between the client and the server, you need to generate two keystore files, each in their own keystores:</span><ul><li><strong><span>keystore</span>.jks </strong>(contains key and certificate) – server-side – upload it using Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li><li><strong>cert.jks</strong> (contains public certificate) – client-side – add it using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Server Certificates" /></ac:link> option group in Environment Options.</li></ul><br />To generate a key pair<hr /><ol><li>Go to your <strong>Java </strong>directory and open the folder named <strong>bin</strong>.</li><li>Enter<em> keytool -genkey -alias MyDomain -keyalg RSA -keystore <span>keystore</span>.jks -keysize 2048 </em>and press <strong>Enter</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cd4558d-c979-473d-bf41-edd5de405c45"><ac:rich-text-body>You can give any name to<em> MyDomain</em> and <strong>keystore.jks</strong>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Enter a password for your keystore and answer the questions asked. A certificate with a key is generated and placed into the keystore. </li><li>To extract a certificate without a key, type <em>keytool -export -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore <span>keystore</span>.jks</em> and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>To place a certificate into another keystore, e.g. <strong>cert.jks</strong>, type <em>keytool -import -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore cert.jks</em> and press <strong>Enter</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4db5da94-0333-4f2f-b369-8d4ec95b4c0d"><ac:rich-text-body>You can give any name to <strong>cert.jks</strong>. However, by default, the tool searches for this exact name.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>Two keystore files are generated: one for the server (<strong>keystore.jks</strong>) and one for the client (<strong>cert.jks</strong>). You can now proceed to enable a secure connection between the client (modeling tool) and the server (Teamwork Cloud). </li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925304 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00111: Enabling the Document View

- page_id: `191925304`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`

- version_number: 1

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Important

855329663

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Important

855329665

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Important

855329664

TheDocument Viewis the mode of a published project that allows you to view it as a continuous document. Essentially, by using theDocument View,you can scroll through the entire content of a project view instead of jumping from one navigation tree element to another as in the Model View.TheDocument Viewcan be enabled for individual views of a project. After selecting a view,you are presented with the document containing information from that view. The sections of the document are numbered hierarchically both in the Navigation pane and the content pane.

The Document View can be enabled by customizing the template that will be used for publishing or from the title bar of a published project.

To enable the Document View in the template used for publishing

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «view» stereotype. In the View Diagram of the template, each element with the «view» stereotype represents a separate navigation tree of a published project. If your template has more than one such element, you can enable the Document View for each of them separately.
3. In the open Specification window, set the **Document View** property as *true.* [ATTACHMENT filename='view_properties_specification_window.png'] The figure shows the Specification window of the element with the «view» stereotype with the Document View enabled.
4. Click the **Close** button.
5. Save the template file.

The Document View is enabled for the selected view of a published project.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

The **Document View** property can also be enabled in the Specification window of the element with the «Document» stereotype. Doing this overrides the property values set for individual elements with the «view» stereotype.

To enable the Document View from the title bar of a published project

1. In the app bar of a published model, click [ATTACHMENT filename='model_view_list.png'] and select the model view for which you want to enable the Document View mode.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Document View .

The Document View is enabled for the selected navigation tree of a published project.

When the mode is set from the title bar, it's only valid until the refresh of a web portal.

855329662

## PAGE 00112: Enabling the Single Tree View

- page_id: `191925312`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925312/Enabling+the+Single+Tree+View
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-07-07T08:51:16.324+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing documents > Document templates > Working with custom document templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

856664797

856664800

856664798

The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views.

To enable the Single Tree View

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.
3. In the open Specification window, set the Single Tree View property as true .
4. Click the Close button.
5. Save the template file.

When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.

856664795

## PAGE 00113: Encrypting property values

- page_id: `191924357`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924357/Encrypting+property+values
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-12T08:32:35.270+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Hardening Magic Collaboration Studio
- labels: ['encrypt-properties', 'property-encryption']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

##### Generating keys for encryption and decryption

By default, properties are stored as plain text. If required, any of these properties can be encrypted. For property encryption and decryption, you need to generate a pair of keys by using our property encryption tool. The tool is provided as the *encryptor.jar* file and the steps below explain how to use it.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

The encryption tool uses Java 11. Therefore, the system **PATH** variable needs to point to the location of the *bin*folder of the Java 11 home directory, for example, *C:\Java\Java11\bin* or */etc/alternatives/jre_11*.

#### NOTE: Note on special characters

Note on special characters

Encrypted properties (like passwords) can include special characters, such as exclamation marks, ampersands, or quotes. These characters must be escaped because the shell that runs the command interprets them within the property and does not invoke the encryptor.The characters that need to be escaped and the escape method depend on the command line shell used to run the command, socheck your shell documentation on how to escape the property value if it contains special characters.

The following examples use the property value **!&**:

- Invoking the command in the Linux bash shell: texttrue

- Invoking the command in the Windows Command Shell: texttrue

To encrypt properties

1. Click the link to download the [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.jar'] file.
2. Open the command-line interface and navigate to the directory where the encryptor.jar file is located.
3. Run the java -jar encryptor.jar command. You should see the information on how to use the tool as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor_open.png']
4. Run the **java -jar encryptor.jar generate-keys <path_to_folder_for_keys>** command to generate a pair of keys for property encryption and decryption. Make sure to replace the**<path_to_folder_for_keys>**placeholder with the actual path to the directory where you want to store the keys.You should see a confirmation that the keys have been successfully generated. Keys for encryption and decryptionAfter executing the command, the following keys are created in the specified directory:**propertiesEncryptionKey.pub** - a public key used to encrypt property values.**propertiesEncryptionKey** - a private key used to decrypt property values.
5. To encrypt a property, run the java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt <unencrypted_property_value> <path_to_the public_key> command. Make sure to replace the <unencrypted_property_value> and <path_to_the public_key> placeholders with the actual property value and the path to the propertiesEncryptionKey.pub file. The tool will output the encrypted property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.png']

##### Configuring Magic Collaboration Studio encrypted property values

To configure Magic Collaboration Studio encrypted property values

1. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/ directory and edit the application.conf file.
2. In the *application.conf*file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Cassandra password, should look similar to this one:**password=ENC("lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==")**.
3. In the same *application.conf* file, add the **esi.config.decrypt_key_file******property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **esi.config.decrypt_key_file=*<install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/**TeamworkCloud/keys/**propertiesEncryptionKey***. Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in the location which Magic Collaboration Studio can access.
4. Restart Magic Collaboration Studio service.

##### Configuring Web Application Platform encrypted property values

To configure Web Application Platform encrypted values

1. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the *webappplatform.properties* file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Magic Collaboration Studio administrator password, should look similar to this one:**twc.admin.password=ENC(lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==)**.
3. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory and open the catalina.properties file.
4. In the *catalina.properties* file, add the **properties.encryption.private.key.location** property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **properties.encryption.private.key.location=*<install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebApp**Platform/conf/keys/propertiesEncryptionKey****.* Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in the location which Web Application Platform can access.
5. Restart Web Application Platform service.
