located.</li><li>Run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar</strong> command. You should see the information on how to use the tool as displayed below.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor_open.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar generate-keys &lt;path_to_folder_for_keys&gt;</strong> command to generate a pair of keys for property encryption and decryption. M<span>ake sure to replace the </span><strong>&lt;path_to_folder_for_keys&gt;</strong><span> placeholder with the actual path to the directory where you want to store the keys. </span>You should see a confirmation that the keys have been successfully generated.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="876d7480-836d-49fb-8183-eefd51c7a612"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Keys for encryption and decryption</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After executing the command, the following keys are created in the specified directory:</p><ul><li><strong>propertiesEncryptionKey.pub</strong> - a public key used to encrypt property values.</li><li><strong>propertiesEncryptionKey</strong> - a private key used to decrypt property values.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>To encrypt a property, run the <strong>java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt &lt;unencrypted_property_value&gt; &lt;path_to_the public_key&gt;</strong> command. Make sure to replace the <strong>&lt;unencrypted_property_value&gt;</strong> and<strong> &lt;path_to_the public_key&gt;</strong> placeholders with the actual property value and the path to the <em>propertiesEncryptionKey.pub</em> file. The tool will output the encrypted property value.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="encryptor.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><h3>Configuring Magic Collaboration Studio encrypted property values</h3><p>To configure Magic Collaboration Studio encrypted property values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/TeamworkCloud/configuration/</em> directory and edit the <em style="text-align: left;">application.conf </em>file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>application.conf </em>file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: <strong>ENC(encrypted_property_value)</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e3c19e4-8921-4e37-880e-345bc54d9237"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of an encrypted property</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span>For example, an encrypted Cassandra password, should look similar to this one: </span><strong>password=ENC(&quot;lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/<br />+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN<br />TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==&quot;)</strong>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the same <em>application.conf</em> file, add the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>esi.config.decrypt_key_file</strong><strong> </strong></span>property and specify the path to the private key (the<em> propertiesEncryptionKey</em> file) as its value, e.g., <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">esi.config.decrypt_key_file=<em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/</em><em>TeamworkCloud/keys/</em></span><em>propertiesEncryptionKey</em></strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd243f64-8a04-4d9b-b879-093d108fd151"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Private key location</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The private key (the <em>propertiesEncryptionKey</em> file) should be in the location which Magic Collaboration Studio can access.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Restart Magic Collaboration Studio service.</li></ol><h3>Configuring Web Application Platform encrypted property values</h3><p>To configure Web Application Platform encrypted values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf </em>directory and open the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: <strong>ENC(encrypted_property_value)</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1c77c78-c806-4722-8882-ad45b415ab81"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of an encrypted property</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span>For example, an encrypted Magic Collaboration Studio administrator password, should look similar to this one: </span><strong>twc.admin.password=ENC(lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/<br />+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN<br />TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==)</strong>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Go to the <em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf</em> directory and open the <em>catalina.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>catalina.properties</em> file, add the <strong>properties.encryption.private.key.location</strong> property and specify the path to the private key (the<em> propertiesEncryptionKey</em> file) as its value, e.g., <strong>properties.encryption.private.key.location=<em style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;install_root&gt;</span>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebApp</em><em>Platform/conf/keys/propertiesEncryptionKey</em></strong><em>.</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c72858ac-6fa4-476d-954b-582d5e19ae7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Private key location</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The private key (the <em>propertiesEncryptionKey</em> file) should be in the location which Web Application Platform can access.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform service.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924284 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00114: Enterprise deployment

- page_id: `191924284`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924284/Enterprise+deployment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:58:12.750+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Deployment Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.

To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The enterprise deployment option for Magic Collaboration Studio and its services.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.</p><p>To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP_and_TWC_enerprise_deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enterprise deployment" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The enterprise deployment option for Magic Collaboration Studio and its services.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925596 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00115: Exporting comments to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `191925596`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925596/Exporting+comments+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:51.859+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics as shown below.

To export Cameo Collaborator comments to PDF or HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document containing the comments you want to export.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Comments .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the comments in the document's open section, which is selected in the Navigation pane.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose from which elements of the active section comments are exported. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_comment_scope.png']
  - Active view - exports the comments in the open view of the document which is selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='App bar' space='MCS2024xR2'] .
  - Whole document - exports all the comments in the document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. In the second step of the wizard, specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document has no custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click the Finish button.
8. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

Note that exporting a large number of comments may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing long-running tasks' space='MCS2024xR2'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes</span>. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics as shown below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To export Cameo Collaborator comments to PDF or HTML</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Cameo Collaborator document containing the comments you want to export.</li><li>On the right side of the app bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Exporting comments to PDF and HTML" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Export document to PDF or HTML</strong>.</li><li>In the open wizard, click the <strong>Type</strong> drop-down box and select <strong>Comments</strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Document scope</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following scope options: <ul><li><strong>Active section</strong> - exports the comments in the document's open section, which is selected in the Navigation pane.</li><li><strong>Active section's presentation elements</strong> - selecting this option allows you to choose from which elements of the active section comments are exported.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_comment_scope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Exporting comments to PDF and HTML" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Active view</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> - exports the comments in the open view of the document which is selected in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="App bar" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[app bar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Whole document</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> - exports all the comments in the document.</span></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Next</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the second step of the wizard, specify the following export properties:</p><ul><li><p>Choose the format of the target document by selecting the <strong>PDF</strong> or <strong>HTML</strong> radio button.</p></li><li><p><span>To add a cover page, turn on the </span><strong>Cover page </strong><span>switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.</span></p></li><li><p><span>To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the </span><strong>Table of contents</strong><span> switch .<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5497a82e-8ce2-4bc9-97b4-e5bde1cc0c4f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Adding the table of contents works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the <strong>Password protected</strong> switch and enter the desired password in the <strong>Password</strong> box. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa164650-1553-4a2b-a826-8e0edd916429"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Enabling password protection works only when exporting comments to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the <strong>Template</strong> drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document has no custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_export_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Exporting comments to PDF and HTML" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.</li><li>When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the <strong>Download</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />Note that exporting a large number of comments may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing long-running tasks" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Task Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925588 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00116: Exporting documents to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `191925588`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925588/Exporting+documents+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-04-28T12:05:25.872+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator allows you to export an open document or a part of it to PDF and HTML formats. You can generate PDF documents of 100,000 pages or more.

#### TIP: Customizing PDF templates

Customizing PDF templates

PDF templates are customizable and can be modified according to your needs. You can find them in the *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/**shared/conf/data/collaborator/pdf* directory.

#### NOTE: Limitations

Limitations

By default, you can export up to 400 sections to PDF and up to 3000 sections to HTML.

To change the export to PDF/HTML limits:

1. Open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties file.
2. Do one or both of the following:
  - Add the export.limit.size.pdf property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to PDF as its value (the default value is 400).
  - Add the export.limit.size.html property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to HTML as its value (the default value is 3000).
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

To export a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF/HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on right side of the app bar and select an Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Document .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the open section of the document.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose which elements of the active section will be exported as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_scope.png']
  - Active view - exports the open view of the document selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='App bar' space=''] .
  - Whole document - exports the whole document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. Specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.
  - Click the Media numberin **g** drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Generate automatically - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.
    - Use current numbering - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.
  - Click the Section numbering drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Automatic numbering - all sections in the generated document are numbered automatically.
    - None - sections in the generated document are not numbered.
  - Click the Break page at drop-down box and select the heading after which page breaks are added in the generated document. You cannot add page breaks when exporting documents to the HTML format. [ATTACHMENT filename='export_properties.png']
7. Click the Finish button.
8. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

Note that exporting a large document may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing long-running tasks' space=''].

#### NOTE: Microsoft TrueType fonts

Microsoft TrueType fonts

If your Cameo Collaborator document uses fonts that are different from the default font used in documents, the exported PDF/HTML files may not support these fonts.

To avoid this, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Cameo Collaborator allows you to export an open document or a part of it to PDF and HTML formats. You can generate PDF documents of 100,000 pages or more.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a52e3116-ac6c-4de9-bcac-424533f65682"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Customizing PDF templates</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>PDF templates are customizable and can be modified according to your needs. You can find them in the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/</em><em>shared/conf/data/collaborator/pdf</em> directory.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d112bc1-848f-441a-a9cd-e25f80a269dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Limitations</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, you can export up to 400 sections to PDF and up to 3000 sections to HTML.</p><p>To change the export to PDF/HTML limits:</p><ol><li>Open the <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li>Do one or both of the following:<ul><li>Add the <strong>export.limit.size.pdf</strong> property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to PDF as its value (the default value is 400).</li><li>Add the <strong>export.limit.size.html</strong> property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to HTML as its value (the default value is 3000).</li></ul></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To export a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF/HTML</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Cameo Collaborator document you want to export.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on right side of the app bar and select an <strong>Export document to PDF or HTML</strong>.</li><li>In the open wizard, click the <strong>Type</strong> drop-down box and select <strong>Document</strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Document scope</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:<ul><li><strong>Active section</strong> - exports the open section of the document.</li><li><strong>Active section's presentation elements</strong> - selecting this option allows you to choose which elements of the active section will be exported as displayed below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_scope.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><strong>Active view</strong> - exports the open view of the document selected in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="App bar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[app bar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><strong>Whole document</strong> - exports the whole document.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Next</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the following export properties:</p><ul><li><p>Choose the format of the target document by selecting the <strong>PDF</strong> or <strong>HTML</strong> radio button.</p></li><li><p><span>To add a cover page, turn on the </span><strong>Cover page </strong><span>switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.</span></p></li><li><p><span>To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the </span><strong>Table of contents</strong><span> switch .<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5497a82e-8ce2-4bc9-97b4-e5bde1cc0c4f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the <strong>Password protected</strong> switch and enter the desired password in the <strong>Password</strong> box. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aa164650-1553-4a2b-a826-8e0edd916429"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the <strong>Template</strong> drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.</span></li><li><span>Click the </span><strong>Media numberin</strong><span><strong>g</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following options:</span><ul><li><strong>Generate automatically</strong><span> - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.</span></li><li><strong>Use current numbering</strong><span> - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.</span></li></ul></li><li>Click the Section numbering drop-down box and select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Automatic numbering</strong><span> - all sections in the generated document are numbered automatically.</span></li><li><strong>None</strong><span> - sections in the generated document are not numbered.</span></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Break page at</strong><span> drop-down box and select the heading after which page breaks are added in the generated document.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1daeee3d-6606-4c2f-ba0c-20c311b97331"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot add page breaks when exporting documents to the HTML format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="export_properties.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.</li><li>When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the <strong>Download</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />Note that exporting a large document may take a while, so be patient while the file is being generated. You can check the file status in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing long-running tasks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Task Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e82d5a9-8e7a-4714-90b3-ca1b4af2ea51"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Microsoft TrueType fonts</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If your Cameo Collaborator document uses fonts that are different from the default font used in documents, the exported PDF/HTML files may not support these fonts.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To avoid this, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install Microsoft TrueType fonts for Document Exporter]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925592 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00117: Exporting tables

- page_id: `191925592`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925592/Exporting+tables
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-05T13:51:57.760+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Sharing and exporting > Exporting documents to PDF and HTML
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

You can export Generic, Instance, and custom tables in a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF, HTML, or Excel.

##### Exporting tables to PDF or HTML

To export a table to PDF or HTML

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the top right corner of the table and select Export to PDF/HTML .
3. An export wizard opens. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
In the open wizard, specify the following export properties:
  - Choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
  - To add a cover page, turn on the**Cover page**switch. A cover page contains the document name, export date, project information, and document information.
  - To add a page with the table of contents, turn on the**Table of contents**switch . Adding the table of contents works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - To protect the generated file with a password, turn on the **Password protected** switch and enter the desired password in the **Password** box. Enabling password protection works only when exporting documents to the PDF format.
  - If you want to use a custom template to generate a PDF or HTML, click the **Template** drop-down box and select the template you want to use. If the document does not have any custom templates, the drop-down box is disabled and the default template is used.
  - Click the Media numberin **g** drop-down box and select one of the following options:
    - Generate automatically - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.
    - Use current numbering - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.
4. Click the Finish button.
5. When you get a message that the file is ready to download, click the Download button.

##### Exporting tables to Excel/CSV/JSON

To export a table to Excel/CSV/JSON

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the top right corner of the table and select Export to Excel/CSV/JSON.
3. An export wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='cc_export.png']
4. With each of the options, select whether you want to Include HTML tags.
5. If you select CSV , also choose the CSV delimiter in the list.
6. Click Export and save the file.

## PAGE 00119: File locations

- page_id: `191925673`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925673/File+locations
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-16T13:39:07.035+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > FAQ and Troubleshooting
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

The default locations for log and configuration files are listed on this page from a standard server installation.

###### Installation paths

| PACKAGE | OS | VERSION | INSTALLATION DIRECTORY |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Teamwork Cloud | Linux | 22xR2, 24x | <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices |
| 22xR1 and older | <install_root>/TeamworkCloud |  |  |
| Windows | 22xR2, 24x | <install_root>\CATIANoMagicServices |  |
| 22xR1 and older | <install_root>\TeamworkCloud |  |  |
| Magic Collaboration Studio | Linux | All | <install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio |
| Windows | All | <install_root>\MagicCollaborationStudio |  |

CATIANoMagicServices

TeamworkCloud

MagicCollaborationStudio

<install_root>

###### Web Application Platform

Log files (e.g., *webapp.log*, *collaborator.log*, etc.) 
Linux:*<install_root>**/WebAppPlatform/logs/webappplatform*Windows:*<install_root>**\WebAppPlatform\logs\webappplatform*

Configuration 
Linux: *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties*

###### Magic Collaboration Studio

Log file for 2024x and newer 
Linux*: <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/logs/server.log***Windows:*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\logs\**server.log*

Configuration 
Linux:*<install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf* 
Windows:*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf*

Log file for earlier versions 
Linux:*<user.home>/.twcloud/<version>/server.log* 
Windows:*C:\Users\<user>\.twcloud\<version>\server.log*

###### Authentication server

Log file for 2022x and newer 
Linux: *<install_root>**/WebAppPlatform/logs/webappplatform/authentication.log* 
Windows: *<install_root>\**WebAppPlatform\logs\webappplatform\authentication.log*

Configuration for 2022x and newer 
Linux: *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties*

Log file for earlier versions 
Linux: *<user.home>/.authserver/<version number>**/authserver.log* 
Windows: *C:\Users\<user>\.authserver\<version>\authserver.log*

Configuration for earlier versions 
Linux: *<install_root>**/AuthServer/config/authserver.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\**AuthServer\config\authserver.properties*

###### Cassandra

Log file 
Linux: */var/log/cassandra/system.log*

Configuration filesLinux:*/etc/cassandra/conf*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925204 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00120: Filtering usages

- page_id: `191925204`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925204/Filtering+usages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:58:22.175+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Resource Usage Map
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating resource usage maps' space='MCS2024xR2'], you can use the Filters pane located on the left side of the application portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. As you can see below, there two filter groups: main filters which you can use in every resource usage map, and filters for suspicious usages which only exist if there are suspicious usages in a map.

To filter resources in a resource usage map, select the check-boxes next to the types of usages you want to view and clear the check-boxes next to the types of usages you want to hide.

#### TIP: Why a map is empty?

Why a map is empty?

If no usages in your resource usage map meet the criteria of the selected filters, the map is displayed as empty. Select more filters to see resource usages in the map.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This example of a resource usage map illustrates different types of filters you can use in the Filters pane.

The following tables describes all the filters you can use in a resource usage map:

| Resource usage map filter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Main filters | Main filters are located at the top of the Filters pane. You can use them in every resource usage map to show or hide non-suspicious usages and standard profiles including local used projects. |
| Display non-suspicious usages | Select this filter to show non-suspicious usages in a resource usage map. |
| Display standard profiles | Select this filter to show the usages of standard profiles and local used projects in a resource usage map. |
| Filters for suspicious usages | Filters for suspicious usages are located under the main filters in the Filters pane. You can use them to show or hide different types of suspicious usages. However these filters are displayed only if there are suspicious usages in a resource usage map. |
| Inconsistent version usages | Select this filter to show the resources that use different versions of the same resource directly and through other used projects. |
| Cyclic usages | Select this filter to show the resources that use themselves through other used projects and form a usage loop. |
| Unused resources | Select this filter to show the resources that are not used by other resources stored in the Magic Collaboration Studio repository. The Unused resources filter can only be displayed in a map created for all the resources in the repository. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating resource usage maps" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating a resource usage map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can use the Filters pane located on the left side of the application portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. As you can see below, there two filter groups: main filters which you can use in every resource usage map, and filters for suspicious usages which only exist if there are suspicious usages in a map.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To filter resources in a resource usage map, select the check-boxes next to the types of usages you want to view and clear the check-boxes next to the types of usages you want to hide.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3df5ff02-5fc0-4496-9a1e-81651afaec23"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Why a map is empty?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If no usages in your resource usage map meet the criteria of the selected filters, the map is displayed as empty. Select more filters to see resource usages in the map.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filters_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Filtering usages" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">This example of a resource usage map illustrates different types of filters you can use in the Filters pane.</h6><p>The following tables describes all the filters you can use in a resource usage map:</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 76.4125%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 20.2586%;" /><col style="width: 79.7414%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Resource usage map filter</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><strong>Main filters</strong></td><td>Main filters are located at the top of the Filters pane. You can use them in every resource usage map to show or hide non-suspicious usages and standard profiles including local used projects.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><strong>Display non-suspicious usages</strong></td><td>Select this filter to show non-suspicious usages in a resource usage map.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><strong>Display standard profiles</strong></td><td>Select this filter to show the usages of standard profiles and local used projects in a resource usage map.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><strong>Filters for suspicious usages</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Filters for suspicious usages are located under the main filters in the Filters pane. <span>You can use them to show or hide different types of suspicious usages. </span>However these filters are displayed only if there are suspicious usages in a resource usage map.</span></td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><strong>Inconsistent version usages</strong></td><td colspan="1">Select this filter to show the resources that use different versions of the same resource directly and through other used projects.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><strong>Cyclic usages</strong></td><td colspan="1">Select this filter to show the resources that use themselves through other used projects and form a usage loop.</td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;" colspan="1"><strong>Unused resources</strong></td><td colspan="1">Select this filter to show the resources that are not used by other resources stored in the Magic Collaboration Studio repository. The <strong>Unused resources</strong> filter can only be displayed in a map created for all the resources in the repository.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924379 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00121: General parameters

- page_id: `191924379`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924379/General+parameters
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-20T08:29:40.608+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following parameters are used with the authentication server to ensure proper working functionality.

| Parameter | Description | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| authentication.token.expirity | The authentication ID token expiration time in seconds. | 900 |
| authentication.code.token.expirity | The authentication code expiration time in seconds. | 15 |
| authentication.implicit.token.expirity | The authentication ID token expiration time in seconds for the implicit authentication flow (e.g., used for MagicDraw UML). | 86400 |
| authentication.unlimited.token.expirity | Authentication ID token that has extremely long expiration time and should only be used in server-server requests when there is a need to perform regular batch jobs. | 630720000 |
| authentication.client.ids | The valid client IDs, separated by commas. | MAGICDRAW,webApplicationPlatform,twcSynchronizationManager |
| authentication.client.implicit | The implicit authentication flow client IDs (separated by commas). | MAGICDRAW |
| authentication.client.unlimited | Client IDs that use token ID of unlimited expiration (separated by commas). | twcSynchronizationManager |
| authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist | A list of allowed redirect URIs. The authentication server client should pass a valid redirect URI to an authorized endpoint. | https://${webapp.server.ip}:8443/,https://${twc.server.ip}:8111/,https://md_redirect/ |
| authentication.disable.credentials | Ability to disable username/password authentication if external user authentication (CAC, SAML) is used. | false |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

value</th></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">authentication.token.expirity</span></td><td>The authentication ID token expiration time in seconds.</td><td colspan="1"><span>900</span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="17efc81d-2a86-4ecd-a27b-4ff8d34bd359"><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">authentication.code.token.expirity</span></td><td>The authentication code expiration time in seconds.</td><td colspan="1">15</td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">authentication.implicit.token.expirity</span></td><td>The authentication ID token expiration time in seconds for the implicit authentication flow (e.g., used for MagicDraw UML).</td><td colspan="1">86400</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">authentication.unlimited.token.expirity</td><td colspan="1">Authentication ID token that has extremely long expiration time and should only be used in server-server requests when there is a need to perform regular batch jobs.</td><td colspan="1">630720000</td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">authentication.client.ids</span></td><td>The valid client IDs, separated by commas.</td><td colspan="1"><p>MAGICDRAW,webApplicationPlatform,twcSynchronizationManager</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">authentication.client.implicit</span></td><td>The implicit authentication flow client IDs (separated by commas).</td><td colspan="1">MAGICDRAW</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">authentication.client.unlimited</td><td colspan="1">Client IDs that use token ID of unlimited expiration (separated by commas).</td><td colspan="1">twcSynchronizationManager</td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</span></td><td>A list of allowed redirect URIs. The authentication server client should pass a valid redirect URI to an authorized endpoint.</td><td colspan="1"><p>https://${webapp.server.ip}:8443/,</p><p>https://${twc.server.ip}:8111/,</p><p><span class="nolink">https://md_redirect/</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>authentication.disable.credentials</p></td><td colspan="1">Ability to disable username/password authentication if external user authentication (CAC, SAML) is used.</td><td colspan="1">false</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=194641945 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00122: Generating a personal access token

- page_id: `194641945`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/194641945/Generating+a+personal+access+token
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-11-19T15:35:47.195+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Admin web applications > My account application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A personal access token allows you to authenticate command-line tools without using your username and password.

Always keep your token secure and never share it with anyone you do not trust.

To generate a personal access token

1. Open the My account application.
2. In the Personal access token area, click the Generate Token button.
3. Once the token is generated, click [ATTACHMENT filename='copy.png'] to copy it to your clipboard.

You can use a personal access token in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Used Projects Auto Update Wizard' space='MD2024xR2'] with an SSO user.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating a personal access token.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A personal access token allows you to authenticate command-line tools without using your username and password.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b667c4fc-bed0-48d0-9104-4eb193eb0901"><ac:rich-text-body>Always keep your token secure and never share it with anyone you do not trust.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To generate a personal access token</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the My account application.</li><li>In the <strong>Personal access token</strong> area, click the <strong>Generate Token</strong> button.</li><li>Once the token is generated, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy.png" /></ac:image> to copy it to your clipboard.<br /><br /></li></ol><p><br />You can use a personal access token in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Used Projects Auto Update Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Projects Auto Update Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with an SSO user.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generating_personal_access_token.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Generating a personal access token.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20c9d454-4427-4a71-8462-0f4afd0364df"><ac:rich-text-body>To invalidate the personal access token, delete it by clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png" /></ac:image>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925227 space=MCS2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00123: Generating a report

- page_id: `191925227`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925227/Generating+a+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-29T10:12:13.812+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Reports application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a report

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accessing the Reports application' space='MCS2024xR2']Open the **Reports** application.
2. Select the desired report template. In this example we use the User details report template: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Select the scope of users. You can select a single, multiple, or all users.
4. Click **Next**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Next, select the format of the report and choose whether to include assigned roles in the reported data: [ATTACHMENT filename='report_options.png']
6. Click Finish and Save to generate and save your report. Below is an example of a User details report file: [ATTACHMENT filename='user_details_report.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In this example we use the User details report template:</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Reports_application_User_details.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating a report" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><br /></span></li><li style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">Select the scope of users. You can select a single, multiple, or all users.</span></li><li style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="User_details_report_scope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating a report" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><br /></span></li><li style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">Next, select the format of the report and choose whether to include assigned roles in the reported data:</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);"><br /></span><ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating a report" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li style="text-align: left;">Click <strong>Finish</strong> and <strong>Save</strong> to generate and save your report. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925179 space=MCS2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00125: Generating resource revisions report

- page_id: `191925179`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925179/Generating+resource+revisions+report
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T15:58:21.809+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Resources application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export the history of any Magic Collaboration Studio project into an Excel spreadsheet. Sharing project revision reports can be a quick and easy way to show progress to team leads or project managers.

To generate and download the resource revisions report

1. Navigate to the Resources application and go to the category where the relevant resource is located.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the resource and select Generate revisions report.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and editing resource details' space='MCS2024xR2']Open the **Resource** pane. On the resource details card click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Generate revisions report .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and editing resource details' space='MCS2024xR2']Open the **Resource** pane . On the top right of the History card click the icon displayed below: [ATTACHMENT filename='generate_revisions_report_icon_on_history_card.png']
3. In the window that opens, select Download to generate and download the report.

Below is an example of a resource revisions report in XSLS format:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resource revisions report.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can export the history of any Magic Collaboration Studio project into an Excel spreadsheet. Sharing project revision reports can be a quick and easy way to show progress to team leads or project managers.</p><p>To generate and download the resource revisions report</p><hr /><ol><li>Navigate to the <strong>Resources</strong> application and <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">go to the category where the relevant resource is located.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Do one of the following:</span></span><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating resource revisions report" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the resource and select<strong> Generate revisions report.</strong></li><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Viewing and editing resource details" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Resource</strong> pane</ac:link-body></ac:link>. On the resource details card click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating resource revisions report" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and </span>select<strong> Generate revisions report</strong><span>.</span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Viewing and editing resource details" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Resource</strong> pane</ac:link-body></ac:link>. On the top right of the <strong>History</strong> card click the icon displayed below:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generate_revisions_report_icon_on_history_card.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating resource revisions report" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>In the window that opens, select<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Download </strong>to generate and download the report.</li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Below is an example of a resource revisions report in XSLS format:</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Resource revisions report" ac:alt="Resource revisions report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_revisions_report.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating resource revisions report" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Resource revisions report.</span></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925376 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00126: Getting document information

- page_id: `191925376`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925376/Getting+document+information
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:50.067+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document contains detailed document and project information. The following steps outline how to access this information.

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document information

1. Open a Cameo Collaborator document.
2. In the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Document information .

The document information screen opens, displaying the following information:

- Document information:
  - Document creation time
  - The name of the user who created the document
  - The last time the document was published
  - The name of the user who published the latest document version
  - The number of the latest document version
  - Indication if commenting and/or editing is enabled and the template that was used to publish the document
- Project information:
  - Project creation time
  - The name of the user who created the project
  - The last time the project was modified
  - The name of the last user to modify the project
  - The number of the latest project version
  - Project branch name

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of the Cameo Collaborator document information screen.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Each Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document contains detailed document and project information. The following steps outline how to access this information.</p><p><br /></p><p>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document information</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a Cameo Collaborator document.</li><li>In the app bar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting document information" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Document information</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />The document information screen opens, displaying the following information:</p><ul><li>Document information:<ul><li>Document creation time</li><li>The name of the user who created the document</li><li>The last time the document was published</li><li>The name of the user who published the latest document version</li><li>The number of the latest document version</li><li>Indication if commenting and/or editing is enabled and the template that was used to publish the document</li></ul></li><li>Project information:<ul><li>Project creation time</li><li>The name of the user who created the project</li><li>The last time the project was modified</li><li>The name of the last user to modify the project</li><li>The number of the latest project version</li><li>Project branch name</li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="document_information.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting document information" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of the Cameo Collaborator document information screen.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191925133 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00127: Getting resource links

- page_id: `191925133`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191925133/Getting+resource+links
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-10-04T16:00:48.896+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > User Guide > Resources application > Working with resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you want to share a Magic Collaboration Studio project or Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document link, you can get it as described below.

To get a Magic Collaboration Studio project link

1. Open the Resources application and go to the category where the project is located.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the project, and select Get project link .
3. When the window with the project link opens, click Copy .

Once you complete the steps above, the link to the project is copied to your clipboard.

To get a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document link

1. Open the Resources application and go to the category where the project is located.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the project, and do one of the following:
  - Select Get resource link to if you want to get the link for registered users.
  - Select Get public resource link if you want to share the link publicly.
3. When the window with the project link opens, click Copy .

Once you complete the steps above, the document link is copied to your clipboard and you can share it with reviewers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you want to share a Magic Collaboration Studio project or Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document link, you can get it as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To get a Magic Collaboration Studio project link</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Resources application and go to the category where the project is located.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting resource links" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the project, and select <strong>Get project link</strong>.</li><li>When the window with the project link opens, click <strong>Copy</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Once you complete the steps above, the link to the project is copied to your clipboard.</p><p><br /></p><p>To get a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document link</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Resources application and go to the category where the project is located.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting resource links" ri:space-key="MCS2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the project, and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select <strong>Get resource link</strong> to if you want to get the link for registered users.</li><li>Select <strong>Get public resource link</strong> if you want to share the link publicly.</li></ul></li><li>When the window with the project link opens, click <strong>Copy</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />Once you complete the steps above, the document link is copied to your clipboard and you can share it with reviewers.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191924460 space=MCS2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00128: Global element usage indexing and search

- page_id: `191924460`
- space_key: `MCS2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024xR2/pages/191924460/Global+element+usage+indexing+and+search
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-30T09:11:37.043+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio > Administration Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**5**

You can index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of the Magic Collaboration Studio repository and later query these usages through the modeling tool. This enables you to evaluate what impact modifying or deleting certain model elements will have on other models that are using it. To enable this functionality, Magic Collaboration Studio needs to be incorporated with a dedicated search engine - Elasticsearch. This component makes it possible to index model element usage data and serve it quickly when queried from a modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Conceptual solution for the global element usage indexing and search functionality.

To start using the global element usage search functionality, you need to install and set up Elasticsearch (v7 series) as described below.

##### Installing and setting up Elasticsearch

###### System requirements

- We recommend using NVMe SSD disks for the Elastic search instance.
- The -Xmx value should be set to 4GB as a minimum.
- The expected indexing speed under recommended hardware settings is roughly 1 revision per second.
- The following formula can be used to roughly determine the required disk space per single Indexing Configuration:

*Used Project size (element count) x 400 bytes x N x # of Using Projects (specified scope)*,

where N equals the number of times both using and used elements get changed throughout their history (N tends to range from 4 to 10 as noted from experiments with production data). 
The formula also assumes that actual element-level reference ratio is 15-20% from all of the elements in a Used Project. 
E.g. A DB size of 250GBs yields an index size of 60GBs under the above mentioned conditions.

###### Installing Elasticsearch

Go to [https://www.elastic.co/downloads/elasticsearch](https://www.elastic.co/downloads/elasticsearch) and install Elasticsearch (v7 series).

We highly recommend deploying Elasticsearch on a machine separate from Magic Collaboration Studio and Cassandra due to additional server resource consumption.

In the Elasticsearch installation directory, open the **jvm.options** file and make sure the **-Xms** and **-Xmx** properties are uncommented.

###### Enabling global element usage indexing and search

Once you install Elasticsearch, configure Magic Collaboration Studio as described below to start using the global element usage search functionality.

To enable global element usage search

1. Go to <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration , open the application.conf file, and add the following lines at the end of the file:
  1. To enable the querying component, add the following property and set it to *true*:
  2. To enable resource indexing, add the following property and set it to *true*:
  3. If Elasticsearch and Magic Collaboration Studio run on different machines, add the following line: "]]>
  4. If Elasticsearch is set up to listen on a different port from the default one (9200), add the following line: ]]>
2. Restart Magic Collaboration Studio services.

Now you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating indexing configurations' space=''] in the Settings application and use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage search' space='MD2024xR2'] in a modeling tool.

###### Enabling Encrypted Communication (SSL) with ElasticSearch Server

https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/7.16/security-minimal-setup.html

To enable encrypted communication between Magic Collaboration Studio and ElasticSearch servers

1. Prepare type PCKS #12 (*.p12) certificate. You can:

- 
  - Use an existing certificate for the machine that will host Elasticsearch, or
  - Generate a new certificate for Elasticsearch using elasticsearch-certutil ( https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/certutil.html ).

Example:

```text
]]>
```

Alternatively, if the certificate subject name does not match the hostname, you can generate the certificate to facilitate the **Subject Alternative Name**extension:

```text
--dns  --ip ]]>
```

2. Configure *elasticsearch.yml.*

Enable security:

```text

```

Enable SSL for transport:

```text
xpack.security.transport.ssl.keystore.password: changeit]]>
```

Enable SSL for HTTP (REST API):

```text
xpack.security.http.ssl.keystore.password: changeit]]>
```

3. Import the certificate into Java's trusted certificates keystore*cacerts*.

First, export the X.509 certificate (usually *.crt or *.cer file) from the *.p12 file (for this use tools like *OpenSSL*or*KeyStore Explorer*). Then import the certificate file using Java's keytool:

```text
-cacerts -alias ]]>
```

4. Set up Magic Collaboration Studio: in application.conc, set the following properties:

```text
"
esi.query.es.node.protocol = "https"]]>
```

##### Pointing multiple Teamwork Cloud instances to one Elasticsearch instance

Instead of using a separate Elasticsearch instance for each Teamwork Cloud instance, you can point multiple Teamwork Cloud instances or clusters toa single Elasticsearch instance.

To point multiple Teamwork Cloud instances to one Elasticsearch instance

1. Go to *<install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration*, open the **application.conf** file.
2. Uncomment the following setting and specify a valid suffix. Note that each Teamwork Cloud instance pointing to the same Elasticsearch instance needs a unique suffix:

```text

```

This enables you to evaluate what impact modifying or deleting certain model elements will have on other models that are using it. To enable this functionality, Magic Collaboration Studio needs to be incorporated with a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b0a6d07a-4c4f-4aa3-a312-1e3879f531a0">dedicated search engine</ac:inline-comment-marker> - Elasticsearch. This component makes it possible to index model element usage data and serve it quickly when queried from a modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="elastic_search_engine.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="letter-spacing: normal;line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b5ccf3f9-bb04-4b4e-95c8-9e94306c039f">Conceptual solution for the global element usage indexing and search functionality.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6><p>To start using the global element usage search functionality, you need to install and set up Elasticsearch (v7 series) as described below.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Installing and setting up Elasticsearch</h3><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">System requirements</h4><ul><li>We recommend using NVMe SSD disks for the Elastic search instance.</li><li>The <strong>-Xmx</strong> value should be set to 4GB as a minimum.</li><li>The expected indexing speed under recommended hardware settings is roughly 1 revision per second.</li><li>The following formula can be used to roughly determine the required disk space per single Indexing Configuration:</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><em>Used Project size (element count) x 400 bytes x N x # the <strong>-Xms</strong> and <strong>-Xmx</strong> properties are uncommented.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Enabling
